acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

acceptance:
1. All scoped caller files no longer declare `*bead.Store` fields or local variables except the documented allowlist exceptions.
2. `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` exists and fails if any Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` imports `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`.
3. `cd cli && go build ./...` green.
4. `cd cli && go test ./...` green.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A large caller surface still depends on `*bead.Store` even after the per-concern method removals. `cli/cmd/try.go:178-209` still constructs and passes a concrete store, and `cli/internal/activework/activework.go:45` still accepts `*bead.Store` directly. The same pattern remains across `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/backend.go:35-152` already defines the finer-grained interfaces needed for the caller refactor, but those abstractions have not been propagated through the call graph. As a result, callers still hold the public concrete type in fields and locals, keeping the storage implementation visible in places that should only know about `Backend` or the narrowest sub-interface.

PROPOSED FIX
- Change scoped caller fields and locals from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` or the narrowest matching sub-interface (`BeadReader`, `BeadQueries`, etc.).
- Preserve the documented allowlist exceptions in `bead_doctor.go`, the file-level paths in `bead.go`, and `sync.go`.
- Add `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` as an AST-based scan of `cli/` that rejects imports of `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle` outside `cli/internal/bead/`.
- Keep the public API surface working only through the new factories created by the storage and lifecycle beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Storage file relocation.
- WatcherHub relocation.
- Lint analyzer cleanup.
title: BL callers: narrow bead store fields to Backend and add module-boundary import test

acceptance:
1. cli/go.mod pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.15.0 exactly, cli/go.sum is updated, and no go.mod/go.work replace, pseudo-version, vendored copy, or fork supplies Fizeau.
2. TestFizeauV015PublicContractCompiles imports only exported Fizeau packages and proves the typed terminal and portable-runtime surfaces compile from the released module.
3. TestFizeauV015TerminalAndCostPresenceRoundTrip proves Outcome, Cause, and Stage survive the public terminal contract and absent, explicit 0.0, and positive CostUSD remain distinguishable.
4. TestFizeauV015ExecuteCancellationWaitsForWrappedTree launches a wrapped process that spawns a child, cancels Execute, and proves it returns only after both PIDs are gone.
5. TestFizeauV015PortableRuntimePlanIsOpaqueAndRouteNeutral proves DDx can retain and pass the public plan without reading, selecting, or reconstructing concrete harness, provider, model, endpoint, or server-instance routing.
6. rg -n "github.com/easel/fizeau.*(replace|=>)|github.com/easel/fizeau v0\\.0\\.0-" cli/go.mod cli/go.sum go.work go.work.sum returns no local replacement or pseudo-version.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "^(TestFizeauV015PublicContractCompiles|TestFizeauV015TerminalAndCostPresenceRoundTrip|TestFizeauV015ExecuteCancellationWaitsForWrappedTree|TestFizeauV015PortableRuntimePlanIsOpaqueAndRouteNeutral)$" -count=1 -timeout 300s passes with every named test present.
8. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx consumers need one released Fizeau contract for typed terminal evidence, exact cost presence, process-tree containment, and portable runtime preparation. cli/go.mod still pins v0.14.50. Multiple child beads would otherwise integrate independently against unreleased commits, local replacements, concrete harness internals, or incompatible assumptions. The repository needs one immutable pin and one public-API conformance suite before those consumers can claim proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/go.mod:5-8 pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.50 and cli/go.sum:88-89 locks that version. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:196-216 builds the public Execute request and lines 283-367 consume current routing and final events without proving the v0.15 typed terminal and cost-presence contract. cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:17-48 is the sole production Fizeau constructor seam. docs/helix/06-iterate/phase1-lower-the-altitude-plan-2026-07-13.md:126-146 requires a released tag, one pin, and public-boundary conformance before WB-1 consumers proceed. No DDx test currently proves typed terminal fields, absent-versus-zero cost, wrapped-tree cancellation completion, and opaque route-neutral portable planning from one released module.

PROPOSED FIX
After fizeau#fizeau-b9a2037c publishes immutable v0.15.0, update cli/go.mod and cli/go.sum to that exact semantic version. Add cli/internal/agent/fizeau_v015_conformance_test.go using only exported Fizeau APIs. Prove typed Outcome, Cause, Stage, and CostUSD presence round-trip; cancellation returns only after a wrapped parent and grandchild exit; and DDx can retain/pass the public portable runtime plan as an opaque value without selecting or reconstructing harness, provider, model, endpoint, or server instance. Keep this bead limited to dependency pinning and compile/runtime conformance.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement DDx retry, escalation, close, or review policy; adopt Docker portable runtime; delete DDx cleanup shims; add route catalogs or provider probes; select a concrete harness/model/provider; or use Fizeau cost, billing, or route evidence as an execution-policy input. Downstream beads own production consumers after this prerequisite closes.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
External blocker fizeau#fizeau-b9a2037c must publish v0.15.0 with public typed terminal fields, absent/zero/positive cost presence, wrapped-process-tree containment, and opaque route-neutral portable runtime planning.

DDx remains pinned to v0.14.50 and must not use a pseudo-version, replace, or independent child repin.

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteLoop_StartupOrphanReaperUsesInjectedScanner` proves worker startup uses the injected bounded scanner and never falls through to host `/proc`.
2. `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` and `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` prove the named default fixtures use the seam while preserving queue assertions.
3. A shared fixture helper or tripwire ensures every non-recovery worker-loop fixture reached by the default package suite uses a bounded scanner; `TestExecutionLoop_DefaultFixturesUseHermeticOrphanScanner` proves the audit.
4. `TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup` and sibling Linux orphan-reaper tests continue to exercise the production scanner/reaper path.
5. `cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent -run '^(TestExecuteLoop_StartupOrphanReaperUsesInjectedScanner|TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestExecutionLoop_DefaultFixturesUseHermeticOrphanScanner|TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes, and `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without default fixtures invoking the production scanner.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The normal `internal/agent` race gate invokes production host-wide process discovery from default worker-loop fixtures. On Niflheim this produced multi-gigabyte Go test cache output, five concurrent provider-launch helpers, rising cgroup file/slab memory, and validation times long enough to prevent otherwise small reliability fixes from landing. The initial three-child split separated the scanner seam from fixture migration, but each intermediate bead must independently pass `lefthook run pre-commit`; a seam-only commit leaves the full package gate non-hermetic, while migration cannot compile before the seam lands. This child therefore owns the smallest independently green atomic slice: seam plus default-fixture migration plus production-coverage proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1685-1700` unconditionally constructs `newOrphanHarnessProcessScanner()` during worker startup.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper_linux.go:22-66` enumerates every `/proc` PID and reads cmdline, cwd, and stat.
- Default worker-loop fixtures including `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go:87-188` and `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain` enter that production startup path even though they test queue behavior, not orphan recovery.
- Linux recovery tests such as `TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup` are the intentional production-scanner coverage and must remain real.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an explicit worker-loop/orphan-reaper scanner seam whose nil/default preserves production behavior. Add a shared bounded fake/no-op scanner helper for tests and inject it into every default worker-loop fixture that is not testing orphan discovery/recovery, including the two named queue fixtures. Keep the Linux orphan-reaper tests on the production scanner. Add a tripwire that fails if those default fixtures fall through to host `/proc` discovery. Land these changes together so the staged package-wide hook is green in this attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Changing production orphan ownership or reaping policy, weakening Linux real-process integration coverage, changing queue ordering, changing provider routing, or skipping the mandatory package-wide hook.

PARENT
`ddx-393abd29`. This child supersedes the original seam-only intermediate scope because that split could not satisfy its own full-gate acceptance independently.

DEPS
None. Later children `ddx-d9caa6fc` and `ddx-5011e1a8` remain as already-satisfied verification closures after this atomic slice lands.
notes:
Expanded from seam-only to the atomic independently-green slice on 2026-07-13 after live validation proved the original seam→fixture dependency split could not pass the mandatory package-wide hook at either intermediate commit.

Attempt 20260713T174439-e938b97a produced checkpoint c6dd2b61b0adb0f9bb124ca4a5773690a460d63f. The exact focused scanner gate passed in 25.973s, and the first and only cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent run passed in 22m30.061s. The run crossed the old 600-second failure point without host-wide scanner fan-out, but generated a 625,717,248-byte Go testlog, about 1.99 GB rchar, 1.19 GB write_bytes, and reached about 177 MiB RSS during its late fixture-build tail. After the green tool returned, the routed Codex harness made no further tool call for more than ten minutes; the operator checkpointed the clean diff and terminated only model PGID 1968095. DDx recorded result_rev c6dd2b61b and transport termination. Recover that checkpoint instead of reimplementing it, reuse the durable green gate evidence, and do not launch an unchanged second package run.
title: Make worker-loop test process discovery hermetic

2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkerSupervisorExposesLatestBlockedTerminalReason|TestWorkerSupervisorPrefersStructuredFDExhaustionDiagnosis'` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
A worker can exit because fd exhaustion was restartable, but the supervisor only keeps the terminal record in memory and does not expose a queryable summary to status callers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:181-184 defines blockedTerminal as private state only.
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:392-520 records terminal history and expiry, but nothing returns the latest terminal reason/classification for CLI status consumers.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small server-side helper/DTO that returns the most recent blocked terminal reason for a project and preserves the structured fd-exhaustion diagnosis already emitted by the agent path when present. Keep the helper read-only and suitable for `ddx worker status` to consume.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired-state persistence, worker restart policy, or any CLI formatting. Do not weaken byte/inode resource guards. Do not alter the stale operator-attention fixes tracked by ddx-b17cad6d and ddx-4d92f2cf. Do not implement a new low-level EMFILE classifier beyond reuse of existing structured fields and test fixtures.

Governing artifact: ddx-9c53e551.

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation simulates staged code, a failed pre-commit gate, and an implementation commit --no-verify, and proves the attempt is preserved with failure_mode=attempt_integrity rather than merged.
2. TestExecuteBeadPromptForbidsNoVerify asserts every rich and embedded execute-bead prompt explicitly forbids --no-verify, hook disabling, and committing after failed or interrupted required gates.
3. Prompt snapshot or embedded-prompt assertions cover every execute-bead prompt variant that can reach an implementation harness.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The live execute-bead path does not supply gate or implementation-command evidence to its integrity validator, and its prompts do not explicitly prohibit hook bypass. A provider can therefore run git commit --no-verify after a required gate fails or is interrupted and still reach merge/close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 invokes ValidateAttemptIntegrity without GateRuns or implementation-command evidence. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2108-2113 and 2143-2148 ask for tests and lefthook but do not forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, or committing after a failed or interrupted required gate.

PROPOSED FIX
Capture foreground gate commands, outputs, exit status, ordering relative to the final staged mutation and implementation commit, plus implementation-agent git commands from the harness session. Pass that evidence to ValidateAttemptIntegrity before landing. Preserve an invalid attempt with failure_mode=attempt_integrity. Update every rich and embedded execute-bead prompt to forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, and committing after required-gate failure or interruption.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- nearest harness-session evidence adapter
- execute-bead prompt builders and snapshot tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not redesign the pure validation policy owned by ddx-c4497391. Do not rewrite existing execution history or forbid DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, or durable-audit commits.

PARENT
ddx-9c3e703b.

DEPS
ddx-c4497391.
title: agent: wire gate evidence and hook-bypass guardrails into execute-bead

2. TestExecutionCleanup_DoesNotSynthesizeOwnershipForHostGlobalMetadataLessScratch proves the missing cleanup.json path stays unowned even when the directory prefix matches a current-project scratch pattern.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesMetadataLessHostGlobalScratch|TestExecutionCleanup_DoesNotSynthesizeOwnershipForHostGlobalMetadataLessScratch" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.

description:
PROBLEM
A cleanup pass currently treats an old directory name as ownership proof, which lets metadata-less host-global scratch trees be traversed and removed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:667-681 synthesizes ExecutionCleanupMetadata when cleanup.json is missing. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:738-755 then measures and removes the resulting tree. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:829-844 also treats os.TempDir as a legacy scratch root, so a prefix like ddx-home- at cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:25-45 is only a discovery hint, not evidence of ownership.

PROPOSED FIX
Before reclamation, classify scratch-root trust. For host-global or legacy process-temp roots, preserve matching metadata-less directories instead of synthesizing current-project ownership, do not call measureTreeWithContext or removal on them, and emit a structured unowned-scratch observation. Continue reclamation only for metadata-marked paths that pass the existing liveness and project checks.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add cleanup time budgets; ddx-de1ff4b4 owns bounded reclamation. Do not migrate scratch producers; ddx-6a2ce3bd owns producer placement. Do not disable cleanup of metadata-marked stale paths or weaken active-worktree preservation.

PARENT
None; root cleanup ownership regression.

DEPS
None.

2. A named regression test such as TestNewExecuteLoopTestStoreUsesPrivateRoots, or an equivalent assertion inside TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes, proves the store/config roots are private to the test fixture and not inherited from process-global DDx/config roots.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes|NewExecuteLoopTestStoreUsesPrivateRoots)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes once in the foreground without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees; an incomplete, killed, backgrounded, or setsid-wrapped run does not satisfy this criterion.

description:
PROBLEM
TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes can still inherit process-global DDx/config roots through the execute loop store/config test helper. That host-dependent fixture path can enumerate a shared DDx scratch tree or module cache and block reliable full-package proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go:3014-3027 creates stores through the normal config loader, so newExecuteLoopTestStore can drift onto shared roots unless the helper explicitly pins private fixture/config roots.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the execute loop test store/config helper used by TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes so each test allocation uses private fixture and config roots. Add or adjust a focused regression around newExecuteLoopTestStore if needed to prove the helper does not read process-global DDx/config roots.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change interruption semantics, claim handling, or cleanup production code. Do not treat a killed, backgrounded, or incomplete run as success. Do not modify the disruption/interrupted-work behavior except where shared helper call sites must compile. Supersedes only the resource-preflight portion of ddx-12a41acc, which remains as failed-attempt audit only.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e6abf4a6. Original parent bead: ddx-34113f35. Deps: none.

1. TestIntegration_WorkersCoordinateThroughReachableServer passes with real server/manual/managed DDx subprocesses and proves exactly one claimant and landing for contested work.
2. TestIntegration_ManualWorkerContinuesOfflineAndReconciles proves the manual DDx PID survives, offline work lands under the cross-process lock, reconnect acknowledges the journal, and no tracker event or commit is duplicated.
3. TestIntegration_ManagedWorkerDiesWithServer proves the managed DDx worker exits while durable claim/attempt evidence remains recoverable.
4. TestIntegration_FizeauCallerDeathOwnsProviderCleanup uses the pinned fake Fizeau fixture/public result to prove provider cleanup without DDx process scanning or signaling.
5. TestServerNeverSignalsProviderProcess installs a signaling tripwire and remains green across shutdown and recovery.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/integration/... -run "TestIntegration_(WorkersCoordinateThroughReachableServer|ManualWorkerContinuesOfflineAndReconciles|ManagedWorkerDiesWithServer|FizeauCallerDeathOwnsProviderCleanup)|TestServerNeverSignalsProviderProcess" passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Integration tests use a real server, git, bead store, DDx worker subprocesses, and a deterministic fake Fizeau fixture · no hosted providers</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 defines online server serialization, offline manual-worker progress and journal reconciliation, and managed DDx-worker death with the server.</adrs>
<governing>TP-021 requires deterministic multi-worker integration coverage. Fizeau owns provider routing and provider process-tree lifetime.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Unit coverage cannot prove that independently started DDx command processes stop conflicting, survive the intended outage boundary, and reconcile without duplicate tracker/git effects. The regression must exercise real DDx process and transport topology without assigning provider supervision to DDx.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe_integration_test.go:37-88 proves event registration only. cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:875-1007 proves in-process coordinator landing only. No test combines a real server, manual DDx worker, managed DDx worker, server loss, offline journal, restart, reconciliation, and a fake Fizeau caller-death fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend TP-021 with a real built DDx binary, real TLS server, real git/bead fixture, deterministic script harness, and fake Fizeau endpoint. Prove online contention yields one claim/landing, a manual worker continues offline and reconciles once, and a server-managed DDx worker exits with the server. Assert that DDx never signals a provider process. Verify provider-tree cleanup only through Fizeau's pinned caller-death fixture and public typed result.

NON-SCOPE
Do not invoke hosted providers, inspect provider descendants from DDx, signal provider processes from DDx, implement routing in DDx, use Docker, or substitute mocks for server/git/bead/landing behavior.

PARENT
ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
ddx-2e49980d: full production coordination wiring must exist before end-to-end proof.
notes: Retrofitted 2026-07-13 to preserve the DDx/Fizeau boundary: managed DDx worker lifetime is DDx/server-owned; provider-tree cleanup is proven only through Fizeau's caller-death fixture and public result.

acceptance:
1. `TestDoctorUnjam_ReleasesStaleClaimLease` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` creates a claimed bead with a stale claim lease/worker disk record, runs `ddx doctor --unjam`, and asserts the bead is unclaimed, a `bead.stopped` or unjam-specific audit event is appended, and the JSON summary records the released bead ID.
2. `TestDoctorUnjam_PreservesFreshLiveClaim` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` creates a fresh claim lease that represents a live worker, runs `ddx doctor --unjam`, and asserts the bead remains claimed and the JSON summary records a preserved claim rather than a release.
3. `TestDoctorUnjam_ClaimLeaseReleaseIsIdempotent` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` runs `ddx doctor --unjam` twice after a stale release and asserts the second run exits 0 without appending duplicate release events.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'DoctorUnjam' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doctor --unjam` must clear stale claim leases so a wedged or dead worker does not leave a bead permanently claimed while recovery waits for an operator. The codebase already has stale-lease safety logic, but the new unjam command does not call it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1419` implements `stopStaleDiskEntry`, which releases a bead claim only after checking `staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim`. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1480` documents and implements the lease-safety predicate that preserves fresh live claims and releases stale/missing heartbeat leases. `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:45` dispatches only to worktree cleanup and never scans worker records or claim leases, so the existing lease-release path is unavailable to `ddx doctor --unjam`.

PROPOSED FIX
Expose a narrow server/worker cleanup entry point, or add an equivalent internal helper, that `ddx doctor --unjam` can call for the current project root. The helper must scan stale worker disk records and claim leases, use the same freshness rules as `staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim`, append the existing audit event shape when releasing a claim, and report released versus preserved claims in the JSON summary. Fresh claims with live heartbeat/PID evidence must be reported as preserved and left untouched. The command must remain safe while a worker is live and must not hold git or tracker locks across long waits.

NON-SCOPE
Gate/supervisor wiring; new gate semantics; dirty-state checkpoint/stash remediation; worktree orphan cleanup; phantom dependency repair; changing claim TTL semantics; forcibly killing live workers; implementing sibling parent acceptance criteria outside this stale-lease slice. Preserve parent context: Parent bead ddx-f8582f42; parent scope references ddx-9f21457d; no deps; governing artifact reference not specified in parent text.

acceptance:
1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
2. TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.
3. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_XDGPath asserts Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`.
4. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_LocalFallback covers the no-remote `local/<basename>-<sha>/` form.
5. TestDDxRoot_URLParsing covers https, ssh, custom-host, missing-remote cases (table-driven).
6. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapInitsGitRepoInXDG asserts the XDG dir is created with `git init` on first bootstrap and a HEAD commit exists.
7. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapIdempotent asserts two concurrent calls do not race-create.
8. TestWorktreeRegistry_FirstWorktreeIsMaster asserts auto-master on first access.
9. TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
10. TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
11. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.
12. Specs listed under SPEC IMPACT are updated in the same commit as the code refactor. Each updated spec references this bead ID.
13. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. Existing tests pass without modification.
14. lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor.

description:
PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
title: C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e6abf4a6 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The normal internal/agent unit-test gate reads and deletes host-global DDx scratch trees. On 2026-07-13, TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim drove about 431 MB of physical reads, timed out after 120 seconds in isolation, and caused the required 600-second package gate to time out. This makes test duration depend on accumulated host cache state and adds substantial I/O/CPU pressure while ddx work is trying to drain the queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:269-288 creates a production NewExecutionCleanupManager for t.TempDir but does not override TempRoot or ScratchRoots. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 therefore derives the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory. Cleanup reaches cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747, where it recursively measures and removes every old matching scratch directory. The timeout stack was in measureTreeWithContext from cleanupScratchRoots, not in the predispatch fixture under test.

PROPOSED FIX
- Give every execution-cleanup unit fixture an explicit hermetic TempRoot and ScratchRoots beneath its own t.TempDir before calling Cleanup.
- Add a shared test helper if needed so future fixtures cannot silently fall back to ~/.cache/ddx or the process temp root.
- Add a sentinel/tripwire proving the loop-cleanup test cannot enumerate, measure, or remove a scratch root outside its fixture root.
- Audit adjacent NewExecutionCleanupManager test call sites reached by ./internal/agent and apply the same boundary where they invoke Cleanup.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go and nearest cleanup test helpers as required

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production cleanup, change retention policy, or skip cleanup assertions. Production cleanup latency is tracked separately.

PARENT
None; root gate-reliability regression.

DEPS
None.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: Cancelled as a redundant verification-only fallback child. Its package gate and lefthook acceptance are now included in executable children ddx-12a41acc and ddx-34113f35.
title: tests: isolate cleanup fixtures from shared scratch roots: part 3

description:
PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: launch managed worker subprocesses with persisted PID/PGID

acceptance:
1. New test TestLandAdvancer_HardStopsOnForeignDirtyPaths in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go creates a dirty or staged non-DDx evidence path such as cli/internal/agent/dirty_impl.go, runs the land advancer, and proves the land is blocked: the target ref does not advance, no checkpoint commit includes the foreign path, and the error names the dirty path.
2. TestLandAdvancer_HardStopsOnForeignDirtyPaths also proves the blocked foreign path is surfaced as operator attention through the land coordination/reporting path, with status land_operator_attention or retry_action/operator_attention as appropriate for the existing result model.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestLandAdvancer_HardStopsOnForeignDirtyPaths' passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The land boundary must self-recover only DDx-owned execution evidence. A dirty or staged path outside .ddx/executions/ must still block landing and surface operator attention instead of being committed, reset, or hidden.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:730 recovers staged .ddx/executions/* evidence, but cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:737 can checkpoint remaining staged local changes at the land boundary. That is too broad for the parent incident: it risks treating foreign dirty files as recoverable land noise. The land error is wrapped by cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:299 and classified as land coordination operator attention by cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report.go:74, so the hard-stop path must remain observable through that reporting path.

PROPOSED FIX
Constrain land-boundary recovery so only DDx-owned execution evidence paths are self-recovered. When any dirty or staged path outside .ddx/executions/ is present, return the existing staged-changes/dirty-worktree land error, preserve the result ref if applicable, and classify the attempt as land_operator_attention/operator_attention without advancing the target ref or creating a checkpoint commit for the foreign path.

NON-SCOPE
- Tracker git sync across nodes (ddx-739ce984).
- The gitignore status of .ddx/executions (already handled on main).
- The queue-progress behavior after a successful evidence-only recovery; that belongs to the sibling child.

PARENT AND RELATED IDS
Parent: ddx-f8fae018. Related/dependency context preserved from parent: ddx-739ce984, ddx-57faa17c.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
No explicit spec-id/governing artifact was provided on the parent bead.

reason: infra_enospc_bash_unavailable

The Bash tool was unavailable for this entire attempt: every invocation
(including from a freshly-spawned sub-agent, and including trivial commands
like `echo x` or `df -h /tmp`) failed at the tool-infrastructure level with:

  ENOSPC: no space left on device, open '/tmp/claude-501/.../tasks/*.output'

This happened on ~15 separate attempts across the full session, both direct
and via a fresh Agent sub-process, so it is not a transient single-command
failure — the harness cannot even persist its own command-output capture
file for this session/worktree right now.

Work completed before hitting the blocker (research only, no code changes):
- Read cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go end to end. Identified the
  ROOT CAUSE call site: within waitForEmptyGitIndex (execute_bead_land.go,
  the "Real staged work is committed" fallback that calls
  checkpointLandingWorktreeLocalChanges unconditionally after
  checkpointOrphanedExecutionEvidence/unstageOrphanedExecutionEvidence both
  decline to act on a mixed/foreign staged set), and the general-purpose
  checkpointLandingWorktreeLocalChanges call inside landLocked() (the
  unconditional "pre-land <targetRef>" checkpoint taken under the main git
  lock before the ref is advanced).
- Read cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report.go: confirmed
  classifyLandCoordinationAction / allGeneratedLandCoordinationPaths already
  distinguish "all-generated-path" staged sets (retry) from sets containing
  a genuine foreign path (operator attention) for the "staged changes after
  waiting" error text — the wiring this bead needs to reuse/extend already
  exists on the reporting side.
- Read cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go: found that
  TestLand_StagedOperatorFilesBlockLanding,
  TestLand_DirtyProjectRootDoesNotBlockSuccessfulLand, and
  TestLand_SyncDeferredWhenOperatorCheckoutDirtyOverlap currently assert the
  OPPOSITE of this bead's contract — they assert that a staged/dirty
  non-evidence path (e.g. operator.txt, README.md) IS silently
  checkpoint-committed and does NOT block landing (this is the
  "never reset or refuse operator changes" behavior from commit 1856c8e0c /

  updating those three tests' expectations in the same commit, not just
  adding the new TestLandAdvancer_HardStopsOnForeignDirtyPaths test —
  otherwise the suite will not be green (AC 4 requires the full
  ./internal/agent/... suite to pass).

No source or test files were modified. No commit was created.

This bead should be retried once the environment's /tmp (or the tool
harness's per-session output-capture quota) has free space again; at that

rationale: status: open
reason: retryable — the execution host has no free disk space (ENOSPC). Every Bash tool
invocation fails before running the requested command, because the harness cannot even
mkdir its own sandbox working directory at /tmp/claude-501/<worktree-slug>. This was
reproduced repeatedly (plain `echo`, `df -h`, and a retry with dangerouslyDisableSandbox
all failed identically), so it is not a one-off flake in a specific command. The Read and
Write tools still function (this file was written successfully), which narrows the
failure to whatever filesystem backs /tmp/claude-501 on this host, not the repo worktree
itself.

Bead ddx-074a82c2 requires editing cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go and adding a
test in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go (TestLandAdvancer_HardStopsOnForeignDirtyPaths),
then running `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` before
committing. None of that is possible without a working Bash tool, and committing red/
unverified code would violate the "do not commit red code" and "run tests before
committing" constraints in the execute-bead instructions. No source changes were made.

Retry once the host's /tmp (or wherever CLAUDE_SANDBOX_DIR/claude-501 resolves) has free
space — the fix here is host/infra disk cleanup, not a code change to this bead's scope.
</content>

description:
PROBLEM
  Story 14 requires federated query resolvers that aggregate beads, runs, and projects across registered spokes. Today the GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql defines FederatedBead, FederatedRun, and FederatedProject types and the corresponding query fields, but write-routing (hub forwards mutations to owning spoke) is not implemented, and the resolver_federation.go file has no mutation forwarding.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:61 defines FederatedBeads, :124 defines FederatedRuns, and :184 defines FederatedProjects — all read-only fan-out via FederationProvider.FanOut (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:31). cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:208 provides the FanOutClient.Execute fan-out transport. No mutation resolver in resolver_mutation_*.go calls FanOut or routes writes to an owning spoke; mutations remain hub-local. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces "hub forwards, never broadcasts."

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.
  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.
  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract ("hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts").
  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).
  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).
title: federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields

2. `TestIsCandidateCycleNonMergeable_CoversAllNonSuccessCycleStatuses` (internal/agent) proves the new predicate returns true for ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock, ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap, and false for ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess and ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied.
3. `TestTrySinglePolicyAttempt_ReviewMalfunctionDoesNotTriggerLand` (cli/cmd package) proves the same guard applies to the `ddx try` code path in cmd/try.go.

5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "^(TestSinglePolicyAttempt_ReviewMalfunctionDoesNotTriggerLand|TestTrySinglePolicyAttempt_ReviewMalfunctionDoesNotTriggerLand)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.

7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx-0c7d976c fixed applyWorkerCandidateCycle so ExecuteBeadResult.Status correctly becomes review_malfunction/review_request_changes/review_block/repair-cycle-exhausted after a pre-land candidate-cycle failure. That fix is necessary but not sufficient: the two CLI call sites that trigger the actual git merge never look at res.Status at all, so a candidate whose review malfunctioned can still be fast-forwarded into the target branch.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:475 `if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {` gates the call to agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks (which calls agent.LandBeadResult, the real merge) solely on ResultRev-changed + ExitCode==0. It does not check res.Status, res.ReviewVerdict, or res.ReviewSkipReason.
- cli/cmd/try.go:421 has the identical gap for `ddx try`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:163 LandBeadResult itself only inspects res.ExitCode, res.Outcome, res.FailureMode, and GateResults (EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult); it has no awareness of res.Status/res.ReviewVerdict/res.ReviewSkipReason (the fields ddx-0c7d976c made meaningful) at all.
- Only after SubmitWithPreMergeChecks/LandBeadResult returns does ApplyLandingToResult (execute_bead_orchestrator.go:336) overwrite res.Status with the landing outcome — so even if the worker correctly reported review_malfunction, that classification is silently discarded by a successful (ungated) merge before anyone downstream can act on it.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared predicate (e.g. `agent.IsCandidateCycleNonMergeable(status string) bool` in execute_bead_status.go, covering ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock, ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap) and check it at both cmd/ call sites before entering the SubmitWithPreMergeChecks branch — route to the existing "preserve, don't merge" branch (the same one used today for res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed / res.ExitCode != 0) instead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change LandBeadResult's git merge, gate-evaluation, or preserve-ref mechanics; do not change the pre-land candidate-cycle classification logic in candidate_cycle.go or applyWorkerCandidateCycle; do not touch the approved/no-reviewer landing path; do not add a new CLI flag.

PARENT
None.

DEPS
None. Continuation of ddx-0c7d976c (that bead fixed the Status propagation into ExecuteBeadResult; this bead makes the cmd/ landing gate actually consult it).
title: cmd: gate land call on candidate-cycle status, not just ResultRev/ExitCode

acceptance:
1. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionPublishesResolvingLiveness blocks a hermetic decomposition hook and proves the sidecar reports the candidate bead ID, phase=resolving, refreshed last_activity_at, and provider-child metadata before any claim or lease exists.
2. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionUsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout injects a blocking hook plus a short configured preclaim timeout and proves DDx returns within the bound, emits a structured timeout observation, and leaves the bead unclaimed.
3. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionReapsProviderDescendants covers success, deterministic fallback, error, timeout, and cancellation; it proves hook-created child process groups are terminated and reaped while a provider process present in the pre-hook baseline survives.
4. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionClearsTransientLiveness covers success, fallback/error, and cancellation and proves no stale candidate/phase remains after the hook returns.
5. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionPreservesLoopStartOrdering proves loop.start remains the first structured event even though preclaim liveness now emits before claim.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecomposition(PublishesResolvingLiveness|UsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout|ReapsProviderDescendants|ClearsTransientLiveness|PreservesLoopStartOrdering)" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
While the managed DDx worker decomposed ddx-29491a0e on 2026-07-13, the preclaim-decompose Codex provider remained live for more than two minutes, but `ddx work status --json` changed to active_work.count=0 and reported no bead_id, phase, or last_activity_at. After the decomposer returned, an interactive nested `codex --no-alt-screen` process (PID 607041, process group 607041) remained directly under the DDx worker until the operator terminated it. Operators therefore see an apparently idle worker while queue-critical model work is running and preclaim providers can leak CPU after successful fallback.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1604-1617 constructs the sidecar liveness reporter but explicitly defers emission until a bead is claimed. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without setting a resolving phase, ticking liveness, or wrapping the hook in provider-descendant cleanup. cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:16 and :59-74 give this preclaim operation a separate fixed eight-minute timeout, so it bypasses the configured five-minute preclaim bound from cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:15 and :95-99. Attempt-end descendant reaping in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1150-1163 is never reached because decomposition happens before an implementation attempt exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Publish candidate-scoped resolving liveness before dispatching preclaim decomposition, heartbeat it while the provider runs, and include provider-child observations without creating a bead lease. Bound the call by the configured preclaim timeout supplied to the execute loop rather than a longer hard-coded timeout. Establish a preclaim lifecycle process baseline and reap every provider descendant introduced by the hook on success, deterministic fallback, error, timeout, and cancellation. Clear transient resolving state on every exit and preserve the existing loop.start event ordering contract.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
None; root reliability regression.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

notes:

The operator terminated only those leaked groups while preserving active execute-bead/decomposition providers. Each had a private PTY, cwd /home/erik/Projects/ddx, no DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, and no child process. PID 1140692 was created at the preclaim-intake to preclaim-decompose boundary and consumed about 8.5 percent CPU plus 186 MB RSS for more than one minute. A later interactive PID 1227198 was initially suspected as a fifth lifecycle leak, but testlog timing tied it to TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace in the active pre-commit gate; that reproduction belongs to ddx-aa6cc320.

It had private PTY pts/15, cwd /home/erik/Projects/ddx, no DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, no children, 185520 KB RSS, and about 16 percent CPU. description:
PROBLEM
  TD-027 §21 still leaves callers holding `*bead.Store`, and `backend.go` still defines `Backend` as the legacy concrete method set instead of the TD-027 interface composite. That keeps the caller side coupled to the concrete tracker type.

ROOT CAUSE
  `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:35-72` defines `Backend` as a monolithic API, not the 11-way composite. Representative concrete callers still live in `cli/cmd/bead.go:317-333,468,504,1634,1664`, `cli/internal/server/server.go:929,5269`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:30,664`, `cli/internal/exec/store.go:40-41`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:218,243`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go:651-654`.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Redefine `Backend` in `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` as `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
  - Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` as the reader-only composite called for by TD-027 §6.1.
  - Replace `*bead.Store` fields and locals in the caller clusters above with `bead.Backend` or the narrowest fitting sub-interface, keeping only the documented file-level exceptions in `bead_doctor.go`, the git/file paths in `bead.go`, and `sync.go`.
  - Keep the existing compile-time `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)` assertion valid after the composite rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
  - Moving concrete storage types under `internal/`.
  - Relocating `WatcherHub`.
  - Lint analyzer removal.
  - AxonStore implementation work.
title: bead: redefine Backend composite and narrow caller Store types

description:
PROBLEM
The watchdog reaper still mirrors the Stop path's pid-limited cleanup. A stalled managed worker can be marked reaped and have its bead claim released, yet still leave descendants behind unless reap uses the same managed-tree cleanup path as Stop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1347-1414 selects reaping candidates and passes only pid into reapWorker.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1417-1482 emits bead.reaped, releases the bead claim, and then calls terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace) only when pid > 0.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 shows the same pid-only limitation that exists in Stop, but the reap path is separate and can drift if it does not reuse the shared cleanup helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route watchdog reaping through the same managed-worker tree cleanup helper used by Stop.
- Keep bead.reaped emission, claim release, and terminal-state persistence intact.
- Make the reap path idempotent so repeated sweeps do not double-clean the same worker.
- Add a process-tree test that proves watchdog reap follows the SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL path and releases the claimed bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add restart policy logic in this bead.
- Do not change Stop beyond the shared helper reuse already required by the stop bead.
- Do not touch external workers or CLI commands.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree proves watchdog reap follows the same SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL cleanup path and releases the claimed bead.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: reap managed worker process trees through watchdog

2. `TestDoctorUnjam_PreservesArchivedDependencyTargets` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` creates a dependency whose target exists only in `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, runs `ddx doctor --unjam`, and asserts that dependency remains.
3. `TestDoctorUnjam_BeadDoctorRepairSummaryAndIdempotency` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` asserts the first run reports bead-doctor repair findings and backup/action metadata, while a second run exits 0 with no additional repair actions.
4. Existing internal proof `TestBeadDoctor_DetectsAndFixesDanglingDep` in `cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go` remains green.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doctor --unjam` must prune phantom dependency edges as part of one codified remediation suite. The repository already has `ddx bead doctor --fix` logic for dangling/phantom deps, but operators still have to remember and run a separate command during jam recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:106` builds the active-plus-archive known ID set, `cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:231` flags dangling dependency edges whose target exists in neither active nor archive tracker files, and `cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:465` removes those edges during `BeadDoctorFix`. `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:118` wires that logic only under `ddx bead doctor --fix`. `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:45` never invokes bead doctor repair, so `ddx doctor --unjam` leaves phantom dependencies in place even though the repair path exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a bead-doctor remediation step to `ddx doctor --unjam` that runs the existing `bead.BeadDoctorFix` against the current workspace tracker. Include the fixed findings count, repaired bead IDs, and backup/artifact paths where available in the unjam JSON summary. Preserve archive-aware behavior and idempotency from `BeadDoctorFix`: archived dependency targets must survive, only dangling/phantom edges are removed, and a clean second run must be a no-op. Return nonzero only if the repair function errors, not merely because findings were repaired.

NON-SCOPE
Gate/supervisor wiring; new gate semantics; dirty-state checkpoint/stash remediation; worktree orphan cleanup; stale claim lease release; changing `ddx bead doctor --fix` semantics beyond what unjam integration requires; implementing sibling parent acceptance criteria outside this phantom-dependency slice. Preserve parent context: Parent bead ddx-f8582f42; parent scope references ddx-9f21457d; no deps; governing artifact reference not specified in parent text.

title: doctor: wire unjam phantom dependency repair

After wiring: spawn a worker, drain a few beads, verify execution evidence shows the new code paths firing. 4. Save the audit findings + decisions per function to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/wire-decisions.md. 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. The 5 originating beads (ddx-da11a34a, ddx-aede917d, ddx-cfedee8e, etc.) verified via test execution showing their behavior is now active in production.
description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill).
title: audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: MIG: Migration tooling factory — Migrator interface + NewMigrator + remove 7 *Store migration methods

description:
PROBLEM: execute-bead workers hold git locks across the entire LLM subprocess invocation, blocking concurrent tracker operations.

FIX: Refactor to acquire locks only for the duration of git mutations, not across subprocess waits. Specifically:
- Scope .git/index.lock acquisition to actual git commands
- Scope .ddx/.git-tracker.lock acquisition to tracker file writes
- Never hold locks while waiting on claude/codex/gemini subprocess

CROSS-REF: Parent ddx-57c40485; original Fizeau incident AR-2026-05-17-repo.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing)
- cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go (if needed)
- Test: git_index_lock_test.go

description:
PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.

description:
PROBLEM
The concurrent predispatch regression still needs an explicit guard that proves it does not shell out to `codex`, `claude`, `gemini`, or `__provider-launch`, and it needs a timeout/failure path that proves fixture-owned subprocesses are canceled and reaped instead of lingering after the test exits.
ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:35-55 is the fixture entrypoint that can still reach live provider execution if the runner seam regresses. cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113 installs the provider-launch PATH shim before service construction, which is the path a regression would use to spawn provider descendants.
PROPOSED FIX
Add a fixture-local provider-spawn tripwire for `codex`, `claude`, `gemini`, and `__provider-launch`. Add a failure/timeout-path test that cancels the fixture context and proves every child process is waited on or killed before the test returns. Keep the tripwire local to this regression fixture; do not change production provider discovery.
NON-SCOPE
Changing routing policy, broadening the concurrency beyond five, or modifying unrelated agent tests.
PARENT
Root regression bead; no separate dependency ID.
DEPS
None.
title: Add provider-spawn and subprocess-reap tripwires to the regression

description:
PROBLEM
Observed directly during a live human-in-the-loop session against a real project (pqueue, /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue), not synthesized. Three related, concrete observations from one session:

1. DUPLICATE CLAIM RACE: a project-scoped `ddx work --server-managed` daemon that had been running for hours, and a foreground `ddx work --json` the operator started manually, both claimed the SAME bead within ~40 seconds of each other on two separate occasions (pqueue-70c85ac1 at 15:26:50/15:27:32, pqueue-5c5353f7 at 16:18:07/16:18:44), each spinning up its own `.ddx-exec-wt` worktree. The second occurrence produced a `pre_claim_intake.system_unready` warning ("project root mutation rejected... revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment") twice on the bead's event log, warn-only, non-blocking. This looks like the same class of issue tracked by ddx-eea2dfca/ddx-69672921 (repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings not surfaced as a clear operator-visible signal) -- filing this as a concrete, reproducible real-world instance rather than duplicating that epic's scope.

2. MAIN WORKTREE LEFT DIRTY DURING A LIVE ATTEMPT: while a project-scoped server-managed worker was `phase=running` on a bead (pqueue-6f8d85db, worktree=.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-pqueue-6f8d85db-...), the PROJECT'S MAIN WORKTREE (not the isolated attempt worktree) showed 17 uncommitted, modified files spanning many unrelated crates, sitting dirty for several minutes across repeated `git status` checks with no new commits landing in between (HEAD stayed at the same `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...` commit throughout). An operator or an agent (Claude Code, in this case) working in that same main worktree during an active attempt has no reliable signal for which uncommitted changes are "the live attempt's WIP, do not touch" versus safe to edit/commit -- a `git status`-only view is ambiguous. If this dirty-main-worktree state during `phase=running` is intentional (e.g. a staging step before a checkpoint commit), it should be documented and/or surfaced by `ddx work status`/`ddx bead show` so a human or agent sharing the worktree can tell not to touch it; if it is not intentional, it looks like an isolation leak from the attempt worktree back into the main one.

3. MERGE GATE DID NOT CATCH A WORKSPACE-WIDE COMPILE BREAK: a bead landed to `main` (pqueue commit 75190c3, "feat: add change-record emission foundation") that changed a shared struct (`ProjectionImageItem` in pqueue-projection) without updating one of its several construction call sites (`crates/pqueue-sqlite/src/relational.rs:5410`), leaving `cargo build --workspace` (and `--all-features`) broken on `main` for every subsequent bead attempt forked from that point, including a completely unrelated one (pqueue-postgres) the operator was validating by hand at the time. This suggests that bead's own acceptance/merge gate ran a narrower build/test scope than `cargo build --workspace --all-features`, so a cross-crate breakage in a sibling crate was not caught before landing on shared `main`.

PROPOSED FIX
Not prescribed here -- filing as observations/evidence for ddx maintainers to triage against existing related epics (ddx-eea2dfca, ddx-69672921, ddx-2189a07f, ddx-693d504f) rather than guessing at implementation. Concretely worth deciding:
- Whether #1's warn-only pre_claim_intake outcome should escalate after N repeats within a short window, per the existing part-3 epic.
- Whether #2's dirty-main-worktree-during-phase=running is by design; if so, expose it via `ddx work status` (e.g. an explicit "main worktree has in-flight attempt WIP, do not modify" flag) rather than leaving it to a bare `git status` read.
- Whether execute-bead's default merge/acceptance gate should require a full `cargo build --workspace --all-features` (or project-equivalent) regardless of the bead's own narrower test scope, specifically to catch cross-crate breakage like #3.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement any of the three fixes here -- this bead is the observation/triage request only.
- Do not touch the pqueue repository or its bead tracker from this bead.
title: Concurrent-worker race left a dirty main worktree + duplicate claim during live pqueue session
