description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doctor --unjam` must handle leaked work that derives from preserve-ref material, not just stale execute-bead worktrees. Today there is no stash path that ties a dirty checkout fragment back to its `refs/ddx/iterations/**` preserve ref.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:16` has no preserve-ref-specific action field in the summary model. `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:55` calls `unjamExecuteBeadWorktrees` directly, so there is no pre-pruning pass that can locate preserve-derived dirt. `cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:85` exposes dirty-path detection internally, but `cmd` cannot currently use it to decide preserve-ref stashing.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the project-root dirty-state scan in `ddx doctor --unjam` to detect paths that match or derive from an existing `refs/ddx/iterations/**` preserve ref. When such dirt is found, create a git stash with a message that names the preserve ref, record the stash action in the JSON summary, and ensure the leaked path is clean afterward. Keep the logic idempotent by avoiding duplicate stashes when the same preserve-ref-derived dirt has already been captured.

NON-SCOPE
DDX-owned checkpointing; report-only unknown dirt; gate/supervisor wiring; stale claim lease release; phantom dependency repair; expanding worktree pruning beyond existing execute-bead cleanup; sibling child bead behaviors outside preserve-ref stashing. Preserve parent context: parent bead ddx-f8582f42; parent scope references ddx-9f21457d; no deps; governing artifact reference not specified in parent text.

1. `TestDoctorUnjam_StashesPreserveDerivedPaths` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` creates a `refs/ddx/iterations/**` preserve ref and matching leaked worktree content, runs `ddx doctor --unjam`, and asserts `git stash list` contains a message naming that preserve ref.
2. `TestDoctorUnjam_StashCleansPreserveDerivedPath` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` asserts the leaked path is clean after the command completes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'DoctorUnjam_StashesPreserveDerivedPaths|DoctorUnjam_StashCleansPreserveDerivedPath' -count=1` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: doctor: stash preserve-ref derived dirt in unjam pass

acceptance:
1. `TestDoctorUnjam_Idempotent` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` remains green after the expanded cleanup path and verifies a second run is a no-op.
2. `TestDoctorUnjam_PrunesStaleWorktrees` in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` continues to assert the registered-worktree pruning path and summary remain intact.
3. Add or update a `TestDoctorUnjam_PrunesAfterCleanup` symbol in `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam_test.go` that proves `git worktree prune` runs after successful orphan cleanup.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'DoctorUnjam' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Even when `ddx doctor --unjam` removes stale execute worktrees, the repository may still retain stale worktree registration data until `git worktree prune` runs. The current flow returns early when no prunable entries are found, so prune is not guaranteed to happen after a successful orphan cleanup pass.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:68` returns immediately when `listPrunableExecuteBeadWorktrees` is empty, which prevents a later prune step from running. The existing implementation at `cli/cmd/doctor_unjam.go:100` is tied to one narrow path and does not enforce a post-cleanup prune invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
Restructure the unjam flow so that a successful cleanup pass always ends with `git worktree prune`, regardless of whether the cleanup was driven by registered prunable worktrees or by orphan execute-worktree directories. Record the prune invocation count in the machine-readable summary and keep the cleanup path idempotent.

NON-SCOPE
Changing supervisor behavior; adding new unjam gates; stashing or repairing dirty worktrees; deleting non-execute worktree directories; inventing new pruning heuristics beyond post-cleanup prune execution; any parent acceptance criteria not covered by the child AC below. Preserve parent context: Parent bead `ddx-b05e6a90`; parent scope references `ddx-9f21457d`; no deps; governing artifact reference not specified in parent text.

1. Ensure `ddx doctor --unjam` calls `git worktree prune` after successful cleanup, even when no registered prunable worktrees were present.
2. Keep the cleanup path idempotent so a second run after a successful prune produces no additional removals.
3. Record prune execution in the machine-readable summary for downstream diagnostics.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'DoctorUnjam' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: doctor: always run git worktree prune after cleanup

acceptance:
1. A committed agent-test-timing-before-after.md records the command, host conditions, package total, and top 20 individual tests before and after the change; it demonstrates two consecutive cold-cache internal/agent runs at or below 480 seconds each without increasing the 600-second timeout.
2. TestSummarizeIncludesPackageElapsedAndHeadroom verifies cli/tools/test-timing reports package elapsed time and a clear failure or warning when the 480-second headroom target is exceeded; .github/workflows/ci.yml invokes that wired-in check for the internal/agent timing artifact.
3. TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded and TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceRecorded remain enabled and pass; no t.Skip, reduced worker count, or deleted assertion is used to obtain the runtime reduction.
4. The optimized default gate launches no codex, claude, gemini, or __provider-launch descendants and does not read or remove shared execution-cache roots.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes twice consecutively from a cold Go test cache, with each run completing within 480 seconds.
6. cd cli && go test ./tools/test-timing/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The required cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s gate has again exhausted its entire package-wide timeout. During attempt 20260713T062233-4a0585c0 it timed out three times after distinct cleanup-fixture fixes; the latest run reached TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceRecorded only at the end and that test had run for about one second when the package alarm fired at 600.034s. Focused tests are green and no live provider fan-out remains, so the gate has insufficient cumulative runtime headroom and prevents otherwise valid bead work from landing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:21-29 runs all 1,341 root-package tests beneath one TestMain and therefore one 600-second package budget. The heavy Git, execution-worktree, cleanup, and concurrency fixtures accumulate serial wall time. The prior fix in closed bead ddx-1a0d4d26 saved only about twelve seconds by shortening lsof and one sleep; subsequent suite growth and shared-root cleanup regressions consumed that margin. .github/workflows/ci.yml:248-270 records test JSON and a timing summary, but the job is non-gating and cli/tools/test-timing/main.go:19-72 reports only individual tests, not package total or required headroom, so this regression was not detected before the default gate became un-runnable.

PROPOSED FIX
- Capture a cold-cache go test -json baseline for internal/agent and use cli/tools/test-timing to identify the cumulative hot path, including subprocess, Git fixture, polling, and cleanup costs.
- Reduce the top contributors with deterministic synchronization, smaller private fixtures, and safe t.Parallel use where tests have no process-global environment, cwd, Git config, or shared-cache state. Split genuinely integration-only coverage into an explicit subpackage or integration lane only when its default-gate coverage is preserved elsewhere.
- Extend cli/tools/test-timing and the CI timing path to report package elapsed time and flag loss of the required 20 percent headroom, so suite growth is observable before the hard timeout.
- Preserve all existing assertions and the five-worker concurrency coverage. Do not skip tests, weaken race/cleanup checks, raise the 600-second timeout, or serialize around a live external provider.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production worker routing, provider selection, or queue ordering. Provider fixture isolation is owned by ddx-aa6cc320; shared scratch-root isolation is owned by ddx-e6abf4a6; bounded production reclamation is owned by ddx-de1ff4b4.

PARENT
None; root performance regression.

DEPS
ddx-e6abf4a6 must land first so performance measurements use private test scratch roots instead of host-wide cleanup work.
notes:
Fresh baseline from merged attempt 20260713T163832-72b324a1 after ddx-427370dc: the focused cleanup tests passed in 21.609s, but the staged pre-commit internal/agent race gate took 27m0.869s for a one-file test-only change. Its agent.test child generated a 622 MiB Go test-cache input log; after the test binary exited, the parent go test process retained about 1.5 GiB RSS and spent several minutes of CPU processing the log. Observed process I/O reached approximately 2.9 GB rchar and 1.26 GB write_bytes. Use this attempt as the before baseline and include Go testlog/cache amplification among the top contributors.

A second post-cleanup baseline from ddx-232aa2c0 attempt 20260713T171608-d879ec96 confirms the non-race mandatory package gate still cannot finish: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s timed out at 600.026s in existing TestWork_NonRouteProviderIsActuallyReaped, inside scanProviderChildProcessesImpl. The bead-owned focused tests were already green in 25.761s. This named tail failure is additional evidence that production host-process discovery inside default test fixtures consumes the entire package budget.

A third baseline after the hermetic scanner patch in ddx-02200b1e attempt 20260713T174439-e938b97a shows the improvement and remaining gap: cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent passed in 22m30.061s with no host-wide scanner fan-out, but still exceeded the 480-second target by 14m30s. The test binary generated a 625,717,248-byte Go testlog, approximately 1.99 GB rchar and 1.19 GB write_bytes, and reached about 177 MiB RSS. Its late tail launched a fixture __provider-launch wrapper to compile a temporary DDx binary, not a live external model. Use checkpoint c6dd2b61b as the post-scanner measurement and optimize cumulative fixture compilation/cache validation next.

Fourth baseline from ddx-5653688f attempt 20260713T195457-44c4a97d after provider-shim hermeticity: the final cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent run contained exactly one agent.test and no real or recursive provider for its full lifetime, but ended red after 1321.735s. TestPerformance_PreDispatchMutationWindowP95UnderBudget measured three tracker-lock samples with p95/max 3.523577873s against the 2s ceiling, and TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold hit context deadline exceeded after 8.48s. The Go action log reached about 628 MB during TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded. Treat these as host-load/runtime-headroom failures, not provider-shim failures; ddx-85d27282 owns removal of the landing chaos matrix from -short.
title: tests: restore 20-percent runtime headroom to internal/agent gate

acceptance:
1. TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever covers a terminal newer than desired.UpdatedAt becoming restartable after DefaultTerminalBlockTTL.
2. TestWorkerSupervisor_ActiveBlockedTerminalConsumesOneDesiredSlot proves one active block does not suppress all starts when DesiredCount > 1.
3. TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalFeedsRestartBackoff proves repeated expired operator_attention/dirty_root terminals are throttled by restartEvents/backoff.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerSupervisor_(ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever|ActiveBlockedTerminalConsumesOneDesiredSlot|ExpiredBlockedTerminalFeedsRestartBackoff)"` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A stale terminal worker record can suppress all desired worker restarts indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:411-418 returns false whenever blockedTerminals is non-empty. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:441-453 clears blocked terminals only when desired.UpdatedAt is newer than the terminal time. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:374-386 records operator_attention/dirty_root terminals into blockedTerminals but cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:387-388 does not add those terminals to restartEvents, so restart backoff does not throttle expired blocks.

PROPOSED FIX
Add DefaultTerminalBlockTTL = 10m in the supervisor, expire blocks older than the TTL, clear blocks when desired.UpdatedAt is newer, and make active blocks consume one desired slot instead of suppressing all starts. When an expired block permits a restart, append a restartEvents entry with reason/evidence sufficient for existing restart backoff to throttle repeat expired-block restarts.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add a new worker-slot store. Do not change worker launch argv or liveness predicates in this bead.
notes: Reviewed design clarification: terminal suppression reasons must be canonicalized before storage/comparison. Map dirty_root/dirty-root/dirty root to dirty_root, operator_attention to operator_attention, and EMFILE/too many open files/resource_exhausted to resource_exhausted; expired resource_exhausted blocks feed the same restart throttle as expired operator_attention/dirty_root blocks.
title: Expire supervisor terminal blocks without restart loops

acceptance:
1. `cli/internal/server/server.go` no longer references `bead.WatcherHub` or `bead.NewWatcherHub`; server construction uses `bead.NewLifecycleSubscriber` while preserving the existing `SubscribeLifecycle` behavior.
2. `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` no longer embeds `*bead.WatcherHub` from the public bead package; its spy uses the lifecycle subscriber factory or a local interface-compatible test double.
3. `TestServerShutdownCallsWorkerManagerShutdown`, `TestGraphQLBeadLifecycleSubscription`, and `TestIntegration_Subscription_BeadLifecycle` pass against the relocated lifecycle implementation and unchanged subscription semantics.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`cli/internal/server/server.go:94-99` documents the server lifecycle hub in terms of `*bead.WatcherHub`, `cli/internal/server/server.go:186-188` constructs that concrete public type with `bead.NewWatcherHub`, and `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go:17-25` embeds `*bead.WatcherHub` in a test spy. After TD-027 §21 moves the watcher hub behind `internal/lifecycle`, server code must use the public lifecycle factory and interface rather than a concrete public bead type.

ROOT CAUSE
The server depends on the exported concrete watcher API from `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:29-41` instead of the existing interface at `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:144-147`, so relocating the implementation will otherwise break server construction and shutdown tests.

PROPOSED FIX
Change server construction to call `bead.NewLifecycleSubscriber` and keep `beadHubCloser` expressed as the minimal `bead.LifecycleSubscriber` plus `Close()` behavior needed by shutdown. Update shutdown tests and subscription tests so they use the public factory or local test doubles instead of embedding or constructing `*bead.WatcherHub` from the public bead package.

Parent: ddx-74452926. Governing artifact: TD-027 §21. No additional parent dependency IDs were supplied.

NON-SCOPE
- Storage backend relocation.
- Caller-field narrowing.
- Boundary import test.
- Lint analyzer cleanup.

1. TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriagePublishesResolvingLiveness blocks a hermetic triage hook and proves ddx active-work state carries the candidate bead ID, phase=post_attempt_triage, refreshed last_activity_at, and provider-child metadata without recreating a claim lease.
2. TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriageUsesConfiguredTimeoutAndContinues injects a blocking hook and short configured deadline, proves the loop returns within the bound, appends post_attempt_triage_timeout evidence, preserves the original ExecuteBeadReport, and remains able to select the next bead.
3. TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriageReapsProviderDescendants covers success, malformed output, error, timeout, and cancellation; hook-created process groups are terminated and reaped while a provider present in the pre-hook baseline survives.
4. TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriageClearsTransientLiveness proves success, error, timeout, and cancellation leave no stale candidate, phase, attempt ID, or provider-child record.
5. TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriagePreservesEventOrdering proves the implementation routing/outcome evidence remains durable before triage evidence and timeout/failure stays fail-open.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkLoop_PostAttemptTriage(PublishesResolvingLiveness|UsesConfiguredTimeoutAndContinues|ReapsProviderDescendants|ClearsTransientLiveness|PreservesEventOrdering)" -count=1 passes.

description:
PROBLEM
After attempt 20260713T062233-4a0585c0 failed, the managed worker launched post-attempt bead-lifecycle triage as Codex PID 1130622. During that live provider call, ddx work status --json reported active_work.count=1 but bead_ids=[] and no provider_children, even though the prompt was explicitly classifying ddx-aa6cc320. A slow or leaked classifier is therefore indistinguishable from an idle worker and can block the single queue worker indefinitely after the implementation lease is already released.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3623-3625 clears the attempt liveness record immediately when the implementation attempt returns. The later parking path at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4103-4117 releases the claim and invokes runPostAttemptTriage without publishing a candidate-scoped phase. runPostAttemptTriage at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:640-653 calls the hook directly with the long-lived loop context and no operation deadline or descendant cleanup. cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:109-123 dispatches the model provider, so any provider child created there is outside the attempt watchdog and absent from status.

PROPOSED FIX
- Publish transient candidate-scoped post_attempt_triage liveness before dispatch, heartbeat it with provider-child metadata, and keep it explicitly lease-free.
- Add a configurable bounded post-attempt triage deadline with a conservative default; on expiry append a structured post_attempt_triage_timeout event, preserve the original report, and continue the watch loop.
- Establish a provider-process baseline and terminate/reap only descendants introduced by the triage hook on success, malformed output, error, timeout, and cancellation.
- Clear transient triage state on every return while preserving the existing fail-open report semantics and event ordering.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change bead-lifecycle classification vocabulary, retry/cooldown policy, implementation-attempt watchdog budgets, or preclaim decomposition lifecycle owned by ddx-092f0d34. Do not kill providers that predate the triage hook or belong to another worker.

PARENT
None; root reliability regression.

DEPS
ddx-2afca9c9 must land first so the required internal/agent gate has reliable runtime headroom.
title: work: bound and surface post-attempt triage lifecycle

2. TestWork_ServerLossSwitchesOfflineAndRetries verifies one unknown-response mutation is journaled/applied once, periodic discovery reconnects, and reconciliation completes before a new online write.

4. Production try/work claim, tracker-transition, and landing paths all receive the shared coordination client; rg -n "NewLocalLandCoordinator" cli/cmd shows no unconditional command-path construction.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "Test(Try_UsesReachableServerCoordinator|Work_UsesReachableServerCoordinator|Work_ServerLossSwitchesOfflineAndRetries|ManagedWork_UsesSameCoordinationClient)" passes.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Cobra CommandFactory · one production code path · real server/local coordination implementations · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6: ddx try, manual ddx work, and server-managed work use the same reconnecting coordination client; only managed process lifetime differs.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 require identical coordination semantics across all execution entry points.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
ddx try and ddx work currently start workerprobe for observability but unconditionally construct process-local land coordinators. The server-managed subprocess reaches the same command path, while the server also owns a separate LandCoordinator. This creates competing single writers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/try.go:232-259 wires workerprobe.TeeJSONL and NewLocalLandCoordinator independently. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:254-282 repeats the same split. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1008-1059 separately submits managed results to the server coordinator.

PROPOSED FIX
- Construct one reconnecting coordination client in the shared execution bootstrap and inject it into claim/transition/landing paths for try and work.
- While Connected, use the server HTTP implementation; on transport failure atomically switch the current operation to the offline journal/local implementation.
- Reconcile offline mutations before returning to online coordination.
- Remove unconditional process-local coordinator construction and the server-managed double-submit path.
- Preserve server-managed process ownership: managed workers still terminate with server shutdown.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change picker order, Fizeau routing/readiness, server desired-state policy, or cross-host federation.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- ddx-b45dcbd4: server and local coordination implementations must exist before command wiring.

description:
# PROBLEM
2026-07-06T14:36Z on eitri: 'ddx try ddx-739ce984' failed with outcome resource_exhausted, detail 'preparing execute-bead git isolation: stat worktree .git: stat /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-739ce984-20260706T143635-baffe7ee/.git: no such file or directory'. Root cause: a prior attempt (died overnight without cleanup) left a git worktree registration whose gitdir no longer exists; 'git worktree prune --dry-run' reported 'gitdir file points to non-existent location'. Worktree creation for the new attempt then failed, and the failure was misclassified as resource_exhausted (which implies quota/disk — 430G was free).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. In execute-bead isolation setup (the code path emitting 'preparing execute-bead git isolation'), run 'git worktree prune' (and remove orphaned .ddx-exec-wt dirs whose registration is gone) before 'git worktree add', and retry the add once after pruning.
2. Reclassify this failure: stat/registration errors during isolation setup are environment_repairable, not resource_exhausted.

# NON-SCOPE
Why the overnight attempt died without cleanup (no evidence remains); full unjam suite (ddx-f8582f42 includes prune — this bead is the narrow in-path guard).

Parent: ddx-9f21457d. No deps.

2. TestHighWaterCleanupUsesShorterPressureAgeButPreservesLiveScratch proves pressure cleanup removes stale DDx scratch sooner while preserving live/recent scratch and registered worktrees.

4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimHighWaterInodeCleanupRunsAggressiveDDXScratchCleanup|TestHighWaterCleanupUsesShorterPressureAgeButPreservesLiveScratch|TestWorkerStopsWhenHighWaterCleanupCannotRestoreHardMinimum" passes.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx workers continue attempting claims and dispatches while /tmp is already near inode exhaustion, so one failing attempt can cascade into many ENOSPC failures. On 2026-07-08, DDx and Cayce workers repeatedly failed shell startup with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir /tmp/claude-501/...`; manual stale cleanup restored progress immediately. Cleanup exists, but it is not escalated aggressively when inode use crosses a critical threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1664 runs cleanup at worker startup and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2044 runs cleanup pre-claim, but the cleanup pass is generic and does not escalate based on inode high-water state.
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:821-825 uses a fixed default scratch min age, so even obvious DDx-owned scratch may survive until the age threshold despite active resource pressure.
cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:250-267 checks roots after cleanup but does not trigger an aggressive DDx-owned scratch cleanup mode when inode use is critically high.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an inode high-water cleanup mode before claim and before dispatch: if a checked temp root has inode usage above a critical threshold or free inodes below the hard minimum, run an aggressive-but-safe cleanup pass for DDx-owned scratch prefixes (ddx-home-*, ddx-provider-shim-*, ddx-fixture-bin-*, stale claim-liveness tmp files) with a shorter pressure-only age threshold. If cleanup cannot restore hard minimum capacity, stop the worker with a clear resource_exhausted/operator_attention event rather than repeatedly claiming beads that cannot run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go or resource preflight tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not delete non-DDx-owned /tmp content. Do not weaken ordinary cleanup safety when no resource pressure exists. Do not hide resource_exhausted; if cleanup fails, the worker must stop or surface operator attention clearly. Do not change provider routing.
title: work: run aggressive DDx scratch cleanup at inode high-water

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-74452926 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadRe
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: BL (Boundary Lockdown): caller narrowing + relocate concretes to interna: part 2

2. TestReleaseWorkflowConsumersEmbedVerifiedFrontendArtifact proves the test job and every Linux/Darwin build-matrix path depend on the frontend producer, download the same artifact into cli/internal/server/frontend/build, and invoke go build only afterward.

4. Existing TestSPAServesIndexHTML and TestSPAFallbackForClientRoute remain green against a rebuilt bundle.

description:
PROBLEM
The tag-triggered release workflow can build and publish four DDx binaries from a fresh checkout that contain only cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep. The Go embed compiles, but the released server has no SvelteKit application assets.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .github/workflows/release.yml:77-83 builds the test binary with go build before any Bun setup or frontend build.
- .github/workflows/release.yml:113-137 gives every platform matrix job a fresh checkout and runs go build directly without downloading or building the frontend bundle.
- cli/Makefile:25-35 defines the required frontend-build dependency and cli/Makefile:43-63 defines frontend-verify, but release.yml bypasses both.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep is the only tracked file in the build tree, so go:embed succeeds while embedding no usable SPA.

PROPOSED FIX
Build the SvelteKit frontend once in a dedicated release job using the pinned Bun lockfile, run frontend-verify, and upload the complete build directory as a workflow artifact. Make the release test job and every platform build job depend on and download that verified artifact into cli/internal/server/frontend/build before go build. Add a repository test that parses release.yml and proves the producer, dependency, artifact path, verification, and consumer ordering from a fresh checkout.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change UI behavior, remove the tracked .gitkeep compile fallback, alter the supported Linux/Darwin matrix, publish the frontend separately, or redesign the release version/tag scheme.

PARENT
None; this is an independently discovered release-blocking regression.

DEPS
None.
title: release: embed the verified SvelteKit bundle in every release binary

9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.

11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.

acceptance:
1. TestOSExecutor_ReportsSignalTermination starts hermetic children terminated by SIGTERM and SIGKILL on supported platforms and proves ExecResult carries exact signal metadata; TestOSExecutor_NonSignalExitPreservesCode proves a normal exit status remains unchanged.
2. TestClassifyLoopReportFailure_ProviderSignal proves SIGTERM and SIGKILL produce distinct structured transport outcome details without claiming either signal is OOM, while an ordinary exit status 1 remains generic transport.
3. TestWorkLoop_ClusteredTransportFailuresDelayNextClaim injects three transport outcomes within ten minutes and proves the next claim is delayed at least five minutes with a transport_cluster_cooldown event containing count, window, and resume_at.
4. TestWorkLoop_TransportClusterWindowExpiresAndSuccessResets proves failures outside the ten-minute window and a successful attempt reset the cluster and do not delay the next claim.
5. TestWorkLoop_TransportClusterCancellation proves cancelling the worker during cooldown exits promptly and does not mutate any bead with a retry-after, blocked, or proposed state.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(OSExecutor_(ReportsSignalTermination|NonSignalExitPreservesCode)|ClassifyLoopReportFailure_ProviderSignal|WorkLoop_(ClusteredTransportFailuresDelayNextClaim|TransportClusterWindowExpiresAndSuccessResets|TransportClusterCancellation))" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
On 2026-06-29 DDx recorded four unrelated provider subprocess failures only as transport: exit status 1, then immediately claimed another bead after each failure. That erased whether the child was killed by SIGKILL/SIGTERM and allowed a clustered infrastructure failure to consume multiple beads and model contexts without a recovery interval.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/executor.go:301-330 records only an integer ExitCode and discards exec.ExitError.ProcessState/syscall.WaitStatus signal evidence. cli/internal/agent/runner.go:351-390 copies only that exit code and stderr into Result. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:6036-6098 then collapses transport-like text into the generic transport outcome. The watch loop maintains no rolling timestamps for transport outcomes, so three failures inside ten minutes do not delay the next claim.

PROPOSED FIX
Carry portable termination metadata from OSExecutor through Result and ExecuteBeadReport, including a signal name/number when ProcessState proves a signal termination. Preserve ordinary nonzero-exit behavior. In watch mode, track transport-class failure timestamps for the current worker; after three within ten minutes, emit a transport_cluster_cooldown observation and apply a context-cancellable delay of at least five minutes before another claim. Reset/expire the rolling window after successful work or elapsed window time.

NON-SCOPE
Do not infer OOM without kernel evidence, change queue ordering, mutate the failed bead to blocked/proposed, add host-load sampling, or change durable_audit commit flow. The cooldown is worker/infra pacing and must not become a per-bead retry-after.

PARENT
ddx-29491a0e. This child owns provider termination taxonomy and clustered transport pacing only.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first because this child changes internal/agent code and its required package gate currently launches live providers.
notes: Cancelled as superseded by Phase 1 typed Fizeau outcomes. Signal/provider exit interpretation and transport availability belong to Fizeau; DDx consumes a typed cause/stage/retry result and applies queue-level retry or escalation without parsing provider stderr or signal identity.
title: agent: classify signaled provider exits and cool clustered transports

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-6a2ce3bd is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-owned temporary homes, provider shims, and helper scratch are created directly under raw os.TempDir(), so /tmp remains the choke point even when DDx has a configured execution/runtime temp root with available space. The 2026-07-08 incident had /tmp at 100% inode use while the configured execution worktree root under /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt still had capacity.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:91 creates provider shims with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-provider-shim-").
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29 and runner_test.go:56 create ddx-home-* with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-"); equivalent command tests create the same prefix in cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:80, cli/cmd/try_test.go:823, and cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:85.
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:116 creates ddx-fixture-bin-* with os.MkdirTemp("", ...).
These producers bypass config.ExecutionScratchRoot / config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot and strand inode-heavy scratch under /tmp.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared helper for DDx-owned scratch temp dirs that prefers config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot) or the configured execution/runtime root when a project root is available, and falls back to os.TempDir() only when no configured root exists. Migrate provider shims and DDx-owned temp homes/fixture bins to the helper where production/runtime context is available. Keep tests hermetic by setting the helper root explicitly in tests.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go
- cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go or a new scratch-temp helper package
- relevant tests in cli/internal/agent and cli/cmd

NON-SCOPE
Do not move claim-liveness in this bead; ddx-3986b89a covers claim heartbeat root selection. Do not change git worktree placement. Do not store long-lived user config in execution scratch. Do not require a project root for all tests; fallback behavior must remain.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestDDXScratchTempDirUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot proves a DDx scratch temp helper creates ddx-home/provider-shim style directories under the configured execution/runtime root when configured.
- TestDDXScratchTempDirFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutConfiguredRoot proves fallback behavior remains when no project/config root is available.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

2. Add TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot with a sentinel outside the fixture roots, proving cleanup neither enumerates nor mutates that sentinel.
3. Add TestExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots proving every audited internal/agent fixture that invokes a production ExecutionCleanupManager keeps its effective temp and scratch roots beneath its own t.TempDir.
4. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees, TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes, and TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead use private fixture/config roots and complete without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.

description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup-fixture split is not independently landable. Attempt 20260713T153145-d424151a correctly isolated TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim, added its outside-root sentinel regression, and passed the focused gate. The required full internal/agent gate still entered host-dependent fixture work and had to be interrupted after five minutes; the SIGQUIT snapshot was in TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead. Leaving the remaining production ExecutionCleanupManager fixtures for a dependent part 2 means part 1 cannot satisfy its own package acceptance before part 2 is allowed to run.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:269-288 constructs a production NewExecutionCleanupManager without explicit TempRoot or ScratchRoots. The valid unlanded attempt patch shows this fixture needs roots below its own t.TempDir and an outside-root sentinel.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go:102-114 centralizes project-root setup but does not enforce hermetic effective cleanup roots across the many NewExecutionCleanupManager call sites in internal/agent tests.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 derives the configured cache parent and os.TempDir whenever ScratchRoots is unset, so any missed test fixture can enumerate accumulated host state.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go:3014-3027 creates stores through the normal config loader. In the failed full-suite snapshot, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_disruption_test.go:250-279 was blocked under that helper while the test process remained host-dependent. This path must be audited along with cleanup runners so the package gate has no hidden global-root dependency.

PROPOSED FIX
Make this the atomic fixture-hermeticity repair. Recreate the valid attempt-20260713T153145-d424151a work-loop change: configure fixture-local TempRoot and ScratchRoots and add TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot. Add or extend a shared cleanup-test helper that requires every internal/agent fixture invoking a production ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup to use roots beneath its own t.TempDir, and audit all such call sites. Add a regression for newExecuteLoopTestStore/runInterruptedWorkAttempt if it can inherit process-global DDx/config roots. The completed change must make the focused fixtures and the full internal/agent package gate independently green in one commit.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go and the nearest cleanup fixture helpers/call sites
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go and execution_resources_test.go where production cleanup is invoked
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go and execute_bead_loop_disruption_test.go only as needed to make the observed store/config fixture hermetic

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production cleanup behavior, retention policy, or deletion semantics. Do not add a package-wide TestMain sandbox, skip tests, weaken timeouts, or treat a killed/incomplete full-suite run as success. Do not rely on an uncommitted worktree or /tmp artifact; the valid part-1 behavior is fully stated above.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e6abf4a6. Deps: none. Supersedes the execution scope of ddx-12a41acc; that bead retains the failed-attempt audit and waits on this atomic repair.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-74452926 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadRe
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go calls RunConflictRecovery (or an adapter in try/conflict_recovery.go) when the Outcome indicates a preserved ref is available.
2. execute_bead_loop.go:847 block replaced with try.Attempt delegation; no direct RunConflictRecovery call remains in the loop.
3. TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: stub executor returns a conflict report, Attempt returns Outcome with disposition Merged.
4. TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: auto-recover returns error, Attempt returns OutcomePark.
5. TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor (existing) still passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/try/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for the remaining gap. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` already splits the planned work into TC-018.4 and TC-019.4, while the existing Playwright coverage in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256` already covers the mocked forwarded paths. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
FEAT-026 routes `beadCreate`, `beadUpdate`, `beadApprove`, `beadBlock`, `beadCancel`, `beadReopen`, and `documentWrite` only to the owner node and never broadcasts (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`). The stale bead mixed that live owner-targeted write persistence work with already-covered UI smoke and the separate ts-net guard.

PROPOSED FIX
- Track only the live owner-targeted write persistence gap: bead create/edit and `documentWrite` forwarding, offline-spoke refusal, and idempotent request IDs.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate bead if it still needs work.
- Guard the narrowed scope with `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Do not expand this bead back into federation read smoke or mocked Playwright smoke coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead.

RECONCILIATION NOTES
- `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` isolates the bead and document write rows this bead is narrowing to.
- `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` already covers the mocked spoke bead-write forwarding smoke.
- `spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256` already covers the mocked spoke document-write forwarding smoke.
- `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

reason=AC1-3 are already satisfied by the bead's current committed state, but AC4 cannot be met and the fix is out of this bead's own declared scope. Verified AC1-3 (no changes needed): - AC1: `ddx bead show ddx-359e812b` already shows the narrowed body (PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE/RECONCILIATION NOTES) — committed in 652aeca21 "chore: update tracker (execute-bead 20260704T011338)". `git diff HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl` is empty. - AC2: the bead body already names TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner, TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner, TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke, and TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID. - AC3: the reconciliation notes already cite TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363, beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268, spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256, and federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307. AC4 status: - `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` PASSES (ok, 151.253s and 3.316s). - `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` FAILS: 8 of 10 tests fail (2 passed). Failures span both the federation-forwarding tests and unrelated local-only tests (create bead, edit bead, lifecycle actions, save helix spec, stale-write refusal, graph refresh) — i.e. this is not a narrow federation gap, the whole feature surface these specs exercise is broken. Root cause (verified via git, not guessed): Commit cc1ab1954 ("Merge origin/main into main", 2026-07-02) silently discarded already-implemented and already-tested federation write-forwarding + bead/document UI work that had been merged by beads ddx-e81c01de, ddx-1644ead2, ddx-abd08e19, ddx-3c399bec, and ddx-c3b15c32 (all closed, all ancestors of HEAD per `git merge-base --is-ancestor`). Evidence: - `git diff --stat d4266aa9b cc1ab1954 -- 'cli/internal/server/federation*' 'cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation*' 'cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation*' 'cli/internal/federation/*'` shows 3868 deleted lines across 14 files, including cli/internal/federation/forwarding.go (116 lines, deleted entirely), cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go (199 of 199 lines deleted, dropping ForwardMutation entirely), resolver_mutation_beads_federation.go + its 705-line test file (deleted entirely), and resolver_mutation_docs_federation_test.go (434 lines, deleted entirely). - `git diff --stat d4266aa9b cc1ab1954 -- cli/internal/server/frontend/` shows matching frontend regressions: BeadDetail.svelte lost 294 lines (Edit/Approve/lifecycle-action UI), documents/[...path]/+page.svelte changed substantially, and beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts / spec-editing.spec.ts themselves also changed in that merge — so the *current* test files expect UI/resolver behavior that the *current* implementation no longer has. - At d4266aa9b (pre-bad-merge), the dropped test files already contained TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner (exact AC2 name) plus close variants of the other three AC2 names (TestFederatedBeadCreate_ForwardsToOwner / TestFederatedBeadUpdate_ForwardsToOwner instead of a single TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner, TestFederationForwardMutation_RejectsOfflineOrReadOnly instead of TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke, TestFederationForwardMutation_IdempotentRequestID instead of TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID). Why this bead cannot fix it: - Recovering ~3868 backend lines + ~2000+ frontend lines across 30+ files, reconciled against whatever legitimate independent changes landed on both sides of that merge, is a multi-bead-sized undertaking by this repo's own size-check rubric (Step 0: >500 LOC across 5+ unrelated files). - This bead's own NON-SCOPE line explicitly forbids it: "Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead." AC4 requires exactly those mocked Playwright specs to pass, which is now self-contradictory given their current failing state. Existing ownership: - Sibling bead ddx-3f3e1cbe ("federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke", priority 1, open, parent ddx-a038a090, depends on ddx-b303cf96) already targets proving live owner-targeted bead/document/worker writes route to the spoke, via a live two-node e2e path (federation-2node.spec.ts) rather than the reverted mock-based resolver tests. That bead is the correct home for recovering/reimplementing this functionality. Suggested action: rewrite ddx-359e812b's AC4 to drop the beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts / spec-editing.spec.ts requirement (already NON-SCOPE'd) and defer the live-write proof entirely to ddx-3f3e1cbe, or close ddx-359e812b as superseded by ddx-3f3e1cbe.

AC4 status: - `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` PASSES (ok, 151.253s and 3.316s). - `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` FAILS: 8 of 10 tests fail (2 passed). Failures span both the federation-forwarding tests and unrelated local-only tests (create bead, edit bead, lifecycle actions, save helix spec, stale-write refusal, graph refresh) — i.e. this is not a narrow federation gap, the whole feature surface these specs exercise is broken. Root cause (verified via git, not guessed): Commit cc1ab1954 ("Merge origin/main into main", 2026-07-02) silently discarded already-implemented and already-tested federation write-forwarding + bead/document UI work that had been merged by beads ddx-e81c01de, ddx-1644ead2, ddx-abd08e19, ddx-3c399bec, and ddx-c3b15c32 (all closed, all ancestors of HEAD per `git merge-base --is-ancestor`). Evidence: - `git diff --stat d4266aa9b cc1ab1954 -- 'cli/internal/server/federation*' 'cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation*' 'cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation*' 'cli/internal/federation/*'` shows 3868 deleted lines across 14 files, including cli/internal/federation/forwarding.go (116 lines, deleted entirely), cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go (199 of 199 lines deleted, dropping ForwardMutation entirely), resolver_mutation_beads_federation.go + its 705-line test file (deleted entirely), and resolver_mutation_docs_federation_test.go (434 lines, deleted entirely). - `git diff --stat d4266aa9b cc1ab1954 -- cli/internal/server/frontend/` shows matching frontend regressions: BeadDetail.svelte lost 294 lines (Edit/Approve/lifecycle-action UI), documents/[...path]/+page.svelte changed substantially, and beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts / spec-editing.spec.ts themselves also changed in that merge — so the *current* test files expect UI/resolver behavior that the *current* implementation no longer has. - At d4266aa9b (pre-bad-merge), the dropped test files already contained TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner (exact AC2 name) plus close variants of the other three AC2 names (TestFederatedBeadCreate_ForwardsToOwner / TestFederatedBeadUpdate_ForwardsToOwner instead of a single TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner, TestFederationForwardMutation_RejectsOfflineOrReadOnly instead of TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke, TestFederationForwardMutation_IdempotentRequestID instead of TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID). Why this bead cannot fix it: - Recovering ~3868 backend lines + ~2000+ frontend lines across 30+ files, reconciled against whatever legitimate independent changes landed on both sides of that merge, is a multi-bead-sized undertaking by this repo's own size-check rubric (Step 0: >500 LOC across 5+ unrelated files). - This bead's own NON-SCOPE line explicitly forbids it: "Do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths into this bead." AC4 requires exactly those mocked Playwright specs to pass, which is now self-contradictory given their current failing state. Existing ownership: - Sibling bead ddx-3f3e1cbe ("federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke", priority 1, open, parent ddx-a038a090, depends on ddx-b303cf96) already targets proving live owner-targeted bead/document/worker writes route to the spoke, via a live two-node e2e path (federation-2node.spec.ts) rather than the reverted mock-based resolver tests. That bead is the correct home for recovering/reimplementing this functionality. Suggested action: rewrite ddx-359e812b's AC4 to drop the beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts / spec-editing.spec.ts requirement (already NON-SCOPE'd) and defer the live-write proof entirely to ddx-3f3e1cbe, or close ddx-359e812b as superseded by ddx-3f3e1cbe.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-43b6b5fe is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 4 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode m
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section documents global vs project install and references ddx-937de9fc.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/registry/... green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
title: agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes

3. TestOfflineJournal_OrderedDurableResume verifies compaction does not drop an unacknowledged mutation and preserves idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, and outcome fields.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Disconnected/manual worker mutations have no durable ordered journal, so a process crash can lose ordering, idempotency keys, preconditions, outcomes, or acknowledgement state needed for later reconciliation before online writes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 buffers only workerprobe events in memory. The parent bead identifies that no durable idempotency-keyed coordination record exists, and cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 plus cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 bypass any journal by constructing local landing coordination directly.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a project-scoped offline journal under cli/internal/agent with monotonic sequence numbers, idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, outcome, durable acknowledgement-through state, resume ordering, and safe compaction that never drops unacknowledged mutations.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not implement the HTTP coordination client. Do not wire Cobra command flows to the journal in this bead beyond package-level production APIs and tests.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
A 2026-06-19 doc/impl alignment audit surfaced several internal inconsistencies in docs/helix that are pure documentation defects (no code change). They are bundled here because each is a small, independently verifiable doc edit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
1. Triplicate TDs: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-035-artifact-search.md, TD-038-artifact-search.md, and TD-039-artifact-search.md are three near-identical "Artifact Search Semantics" designs (same title, same depends_on [TD-026, TD-029]), all produced by the closed bead ddx-23cbcb4b from non-idempotent execution attempts each grabbing a fresh TD number. None is cross-referenced elsewhere.
2. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:11 has a self-contradictory status: '**Status:** Proposed (Accepted after operator review)'.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:18 cites the upstream contract as CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md, while audit docs (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md, AR-2026-05-02-fictional-upstream-deps.md) name it CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md.
4. FEAT status vocabulary across the 26 docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-*.md is internally inconsistent: 'Frame' vs 'Framing' (two spellings of one state) and 'Complete' vs 'Implemented' (used interchangeably).
5. A TD-030 design doc cites '/tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md' as load-bearing background, violating CLAUDE.md (no /tmp/ plan citations; they do not survive between machines).

PROPOSED FIX
Make each edit; no code changes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the canonical FEAT Status enum to match HELIX's [Draft|Specified|Approved] (DDx intentionally tracks build progress); only make the existing vocabulary self-consistent. Do not touch implementation.
title: docs: reconcile spec inconsistencies surfaced by 2026-06-19 doc/impl alignment audit

description:
PROBLEM
The worker self-healing fixes need an end-to-end proof that one project can fail while other supervised projects continue reconciling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
SupervisorRegistry.ReconcileAll already iterates projects with per-project error isolation, but the combined failure modes from this plan are not covered by one multi-project regression. Prior incidents crossed DDx, Cayce, Snorri, and Pqueue-shaped projects.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a multi-project integration test fixture with four project roots. Seed one stale blocked terminal, one unresolved preserved-review bead using Bead.Extra markers, one resource-exhausted terminal, and one empty queue. Prove reconcile restarts eligible desired workers, does not reclaim unresolved preserved-review work, surfaces resource attention with backoff, and continues other projects independently.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require real external providers or network. Use fakes/stubs for worker launches where possible.
title: Prove multi-project worker self-healing end to end

acceptance:
1. Add TestWorkerSet_RetriesDuringRestartListenerReadiness covering cli/cmd/worker.go: it simulates a local server URL that refuses the first connection or two during restart, then accepts PUT /api/agent/workers/desired, and asserts `ddx worker set` succeeds without operator retry.
2. Add TestWorkerSet_ReadinessRetryBudgetExpires or equivalent coverage asserting persistent connection refused returns a clear error naming the local server readiness timeout.
3. If the fix moves server address publication instead of client retry, add or update a server test proving `server.addr` is not advertised as ready until the listener is accepting connections.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Immediately after `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` on 2026-06-16, `ddx worker set --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx --count 1 --restart` failed with `server request: Put "https://127.0.0.1:7743/api/agent/workers/desired": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:7743: connect: connection refused`. Retrying after the listener accepted requests succeeded. Operators should be able to issue worker control commands immediately after a managed service restart without a manual sleep/retry loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:351-363 writes `server.addr`, calls `reconcileDesiredWorkersAfterStartup`, and only then enters `http.ListenAndServeTLS`. During that window clients can resolve the restarted server URL but the listener is not accepting connections yet.
- cli/cmd/worker.go:103-110 sends a single PUT to `/api/agent/workers/desired` and returns the raw connection error without bounded readiness retry when the recorded server is alive but not yet listening.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:373-382 intentionally delays startup reconcile passes, so service startup is already asynchronous; the worker-control CLI needs a matching readiness contract or the server needs to write/advertise readiness only after bind.

PROPOSED FIX
Make server restart readiness deterministic for worker-control commands. Either bind/listen before writing the advertised ready address, or add a bounded retry/readiness wait in worker control for connection-refused failures against the local server immediately after restart. Keep failures fast for non-local, TLS/auth, or persistent server-down cases, and surface an actionable error if the readiness budget expires.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker reconciliation semantics, worker count caps, or GraphQL worker mutations. Do not mask real HTTP 4xx/5xx responses. Do not alter startup desired-worker restore behavior except as required to make listener readiness observable.
title: server: tolerate listener readiness after restart

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

description:
PROBLEM
Operators lack a concise, durable guide for diagnosing a `ddx work --watch` loop that appears alive but does not claim ready beads. The parent incident's suggested action pointed to route/harness configuration even though the actionable fault was DDx's readiness decoder/worker behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The user-facing README is the natural top-level diagnostic surface, but the pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle escalation behavior need to be stated there near the quick-start operational commands in `README.md:40-45`. Worker behavior is implemented in the readiness classification and work loop, but operators should not need to read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` to know what warn-only, operator-attention, and hard errors mean.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the README to name the `readiness_checks` contract, canonical verdict forms, failure-class mapping, same-fingerprint/operator-attention behavior, claim-success-rate warning knobs, and first diagnostic commands/logs for a full ready queue that is idling. Keep the guidance implementation-neutral enough to remain valid as the specific worker internals evolve.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change runtime behavior, decoder behavior, schema validation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not add long incident narrative or depend on the external Fizeau log path. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder, same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warning, and schema-lock children so the docs describe landed behavior.
title: Document pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle diagnosis

2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.
