description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.
title: bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures

description:
PROBLEM
The unattended ddx work drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on next pre-claim found .crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. A stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (added by ddx-df77e668). Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are not covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (implementer/operator picks)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink materialized into a dir whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink). RECOMMENDED.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills), analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these paths (currently committed as symlinks; deliberate structural change).

NON-SCOPE: do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits; do not change the external skill tooling.
CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668.
title: execute-bead: unattended drain halts on tool-managed skill-symlink churn (.crush/skills/*); make pre-claim resilient

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

2. A test in cli/internal/docprose (e.g. TestValeVersionAcceptsCompatibleRange or TestValeBootstrapResolvesBinary) covers the new behavior without requiring a preinstalled exact vale.

description:
PROBLEM: The ddx prose checker requires an EXACT Vale version string and neither accepts a compatible range nor installs Vale itself. Operators without precisely that build are hard-blocked, with no bootstrap.
ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/docprose/vale_runner.go:13 SupportedValeVersion="vale version 3.13.0"; cli/cmd/doctor.go:24 const supportedValeVersion + :631 errors when version != supportedValeVersion (exact-string equality); vale_runner.go:62 exec.LookPath("vale") only — no install/bootstrap. No version-range tolerance and no self-install.
PROPOSED FIX: either (a) accept a documented compatible version range (e.g. >= 3.13.0 within the 3.x line) instead of exact-string equality, or (b) bootstrap the pinned Vale (download the pinned release into a ddx-managed cache dir and invoke that) so the check is self-sufficient on a clean machine. Prefer (b) with (a) as fallback.
NON-SCOPE: changing Vale styles/rules.
title: doctor/prose: Vale check requires exact pinned version and self-installs nothing — make it flexible or bootstrap Vale

acceptance:
1. TestResourcePreflightReportsTopInodeConsumersOnLowInodes proves a low-inode preflight failure includes a bounded top-consumers list with path, entry count, size, age, and cleanup-prefix match.
2. TestResourcePreflightTopInodeConsumerScanIsBounded proves the diagnostic stops at a configured or hard-coded safe limit and does not walk unbounded trees.
3. TestResourceExhaustedPayloadIncludesInodeDiagnostics proves the structured resource_exhausted event/report carries the top-consumer diagnostic through existing JSON output.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestResourcePreflightReportsTopInodeConsumersOnLowInodes|TestResourcePreflightTopInodeConsumerScanIsBounded|TestResourceExhaustedPayloadIncludesInodeDiagnostics" passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When /tmp inode exhaustion occurs, DDx currently reports ENOSPC or resource_exhausted without naming the directories consuming inodes. Operators had to manually run df/find/du to discover that ddx-home-* and ddx-claim-heartbeats were responsible. This slows recovery and hides whether cleanup is effective.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:250-267 enforces resource thresholds on known roots but does not collect top inode-consuming children for the failing root.
cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:309-323 collapses writable probe failures into generic messages, and later resource_exhausted payloads lack a compact per-root top-consumer list.
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:140-168 has structured summary fields, but no standard observation for top inode consumers after cleanup/preflight failure.

PROPOSED FIX
When resource preflight fails for ENOSPC, low inode count, or EMFILE-adjacent temp creation failures, attach a bounded top-inode-consumers diagnostic for the failing root. The diagnostic should include child path, file/dir entry count, byte size if cheap, age/mtime, and whether the child matches a DDx-owned cleanup prefix. Keep the scan bounded and avoid recursively walking untrusted huge trees without limits.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go only if shared diagnostic structs live there
- cli/internal/server or cmd rendering only if existing JSON/text reports need pass-through

NON-SCOPE
Do not delete anything in this bead. Do not change resource threshold values. Do not perform unbounded full-filesystem scans. Do not include sensitive file contents; paths and counts only.

reason: Host /tmp is out of disk space/inodes on this execution worker, so the Bash tool cannot even create its own sandbox working directory (mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...') before running any command. Every Bash invocation fails with ENOSPC at that step, including with the sandbox bypass flag and a plain file Write targeting /tmp (also ENOSPC). This makes `cd cli && go test ...` (AC 4, 5) and `lefthook run pre-commit` (AC 6) impossible to run, and blocks `git add`/`git commit` entirely, so no commit can be produced or verified. Confirmed persistent across 5 separate Bash tool attempts (`echo hello`, `true`, `df -h`, dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true variant, and a Write to /tmp/ddx-disk-test.txt) in this session; only Read/Write/Edit against the in-repo worktree path (not /tmp) succeed. This is an external blocker on the worker host's own filesystem, not something addressable by editing DDx source under cli/internal/agent/. Needs operator/infra action to reclaim /tmp on this worker (or reschedule the bead on a worker with free /tmp) before this bead can be executed and verified.

acceptance:
1. TestDockerAttemptConsumesOpaqueFizeauRuntimeBundle injects a bundle with arbitrary generic mount and environment names and proves Docker preparation calls the public materializer exactly once.
2. TestDockerAttemptForwardsRuntimePlanByteForByte proves every returned mount, read-only bit, target, and environment name reaches docker run unchanged and DDx performs no route or harness filtering.
3. TestDockerAttemptNeverNamesConcreteHarnessOrCredential proves production attempt_backend code contains no claude, codex, gemini, anthropic, openai, openrouter, provider credential path, provider quota filename, or provider-specific environment-prefix registry.
4. TestDockerAttemptRuntimeBundleCleanup proves success, preparation failure, execution failure, cancellation, and retained-container policy invoke documented cleanup without logging secret values.
5. TestDockerAttemptUnpinnedExecuteUsesFizeauRouting proves the inner ServiceExecuteRequest remains unpinned while Fizeau alone returns actual harness/provider/model.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestDockerAttempt(ConsumesOpaqueFizeauRuntimeBundle|ForwardsRuntimePlanByteForByte|NeverNamesConcreteHarnessOrCredential|RuntimeBundleCleanup|UnpinnedExecuteUsesFizeauRouting)' -count=1 -timeout 300s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Docker-clone attempts currently make DDx responsible for installing concrete Claude, Codex, and Gemini binaries, copying their private credentials and settings, selecting provider environment-variable prefixes, and seeding provider-specific Fizeau quota caches. This duplicates Fizeau runtime knowledge and becomes stale whenever Fizeau adds or changes a harness.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:263-280 prepares a DDx-owned synthetic home for the embedded runtime.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:290-347 constructs the container invocation and calls dockerToolMounts before dispatch.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:803-871 applies DDx-owned tool mounts and credential environment forwarding.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:902-914 hard-codes claude, codex, and gemini binary discovery.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:941-975 copies Codex and Claude credentials/settings plus Claude, Codex, and Gemini Fizeau quota snapshots.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:1002-1020 hard-codes Anthropic, Codex, Fizeau, Gemini, Google, OpenAI, and OpenRouter environment prefixes.
- Fizeau bead fizeau-725d478d owns the missing public portable-runtime materialization seam; fizeau-b9a2037c must publish that seam in the pinned module before DDx consumes it.

PROPOSED FIX
Pin the released Fizeau version containing PreparePortableRuntime or its final public equivalent. During Docker workspace preparation, ask Fizeau to materialize one complete route-neutral runtime bundle under the attempt runtime root. Store only the returned opaque generic mount/environment/cleanup plan. Apply returned mounts and environment names byte-for-byte without inspecting, filtering, naming, or selecting a harness/provider/model. Invoke bundle cleanup during normal cleanup and failed preparation. Delete dockerToolMounts, provider-specific home copies, provider environment prefixes, and all concrete harness/credential/quota strings from the Docker backend. Retain DDx-owned container identity, worktree mount, prompt path, DDX correlation variables, resource limits, project caches, network selection, candidate transport, and DDx container cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not design or duplicate the portable-runtime contract in DDx, choose a concrete route from bundle contents, make Fizeau own DDx worktrees/container resource policy/verification/landing/tracker state, remove provider-process supervision or text classification before separately pinned v0.15 guarantees, bake credentials into images, or emit secrets in evidence.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
ddx-98e9fe56 establishes route-neutral Fizeau-only dispatch. fizeau#fizeau-725d478d supplies the opaque portable runtime contract. fizeau#fizeau-b9a2037c publishes it in the supported module and is the structured external blocker.
notes: Fizeau v0.15 pin and public conformance are centralized in ddx-00d406ce; this consumer must not repin Fizeau independently.
title: attempt-backend: consume an opaque Fizeau runtime bundle in Docker attempts

acceptance:
1. `TestCleanupCommand_JSONIncludesProcessFindings` proves `ddx cleanup --json` includes process findings/counts and still exposes the stale temp dir, run-state, and scratch fields that already existed.
2. `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsProcessFindingsInText` (or equivalent) proves dry-run text includes process findings/counts and still prints the existing stale temp dir/run-state/scratch summary lines.
3. The existing cleanup command regression tests, especially `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting` and `TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape`, continue to pass with the widened output contract.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'CleanupCommand|Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx cleanup` can already report stale temp dirs, run-state files, scratch dirs, and evidence retention, but it has no way to show stale attempt-descendant process findings to the operator. A dry-run can therefore look clean while the host still has DDx-owned CPU/port pressure.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:11-30` defines `cleanupCommandReport` with only file/resource counters, warnings, blocked errors, and observations; there is no process field in the JSON/text contract.
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:69-72` constructs `agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager` and runs it, but `runCleanup` only copies the current file/resource summary fields into the report and prints those fields in text output.
`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` currently exposes file/resource counts only, so the CLI has nothing process-related to forward.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend `cleanupCommandReport` with process counts and structured process findings from the cleanup summary, keeping the existing resource counters intact.
- Update `runCleanup` so both `--json` and the text report include process findings/counts without dropping stale temp dir, run-state, scratch, or evidence fields.
- Keep dry-run semantics unchanged: the report should show what `--apply` would terminate, but dry-run must not kill anything.
- Add focused command tests that assert the widened JSON/text shape while preserving the current resource-reporting assertions.

NON-SCOPE
- Process detection/reaping logic itself lives in the sibling `agent` bead.
- Startup/pre-claim cleanup wiring lives in the sibling `work startup` bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No change to the command name, flag set, or non-cleanup subcommands.
title: cmd: surface cleanup process findings in JSON and text

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteLoop_StartupOrphanReaperUsesInjectedScanner` proves a worker loop can use a bounded injected scanner and does not fall through to host `/proc` discovery.
2. `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` and `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` prove the named default-suite fixtures use the bounded seam while preserving their queue assertions.
3. Linux real-process coverage in `TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup` and its sibling orphan-reaper tests still exercises the production scanner/reaper behavior.
4. `cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent -run '^(TestExecuteLoop_StartupOrphanReaperUsesInjectedScanner|TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without default worker-loop fixtures invoking the production `/proc` scanner.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The normal `internal/agent` race gate performs production host-wide process discovery for every default-suite `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run`. On Niflheim, the active `go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` produced a 2,769,416,192-byte Go test cache log in roughly 14 minutes while repeatedly recording `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`, `/proc/<pid>/stat`, and `/proc/<pid>/cwd` reads. This turns hermetic worker-loop tests into host-size-dependent CPU, I/O, and disk consumers and delays every agent bead validation.
ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1685-1700 unconditionally runs the startup orphan-harness reaper with `newOrphanHarnessProcessScanner()` for every worker loop, including default-suite fixtures. cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper_linux.go:22-45 enumerates every `/proc` directory and cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper_linux.go:48-66 reads cmdline, cwd, and stat for every process. Fixtures such as cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go:87-188 and the `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain` fixture invoke the real startup path without a hermetic process-scanner seam.
PROPOSED FIX
Add an explicit attempt/loop runtime seam for the orphan-harness process scanner (or an equivalently narrow recovery preflight) whose nil/default preserves production recovery behavior. Inject a bounded fake/no-op scanner in default worker-loop fixtures that are not testing orphan recovery. Keep real `/proc` coverage in the Linux orphan-reaper tests. Make the default-suite behavior deterministic regardless of host PID count, and retain an observable production scan when recovery candidates exist.
NON-SCOPE
Changing the production orphan ownership policy, removing Linux real-process integration coverage, weakening provider reaping, or changing queue priority semantics.
PARENT
Discovered while validating the Niflheim provider-test CPU regression parent.
DEPS
None.

Linux real-process coverage in TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup and sibling orphan-reaper tests must still exercise production behavior","coverage":"Child 3 AC1"},{"parent_ac":"4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -race ./internal/agent -run '^(TestExecuteLoop_StartupOrphanReaperUsesInjectedScanner|TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanProcessGroup)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s must pass","coverage":"Child 3 AC2"},{"parent_ac":"5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent must pass without default worker-loop fixtures invoking the production /proc scanner","coverage":"Child 3 AC3"},{"parent_ac":"6. lefthook run pre-commit must pass","coverage":"Child 1 AC3, Child 2 AC4, and Child 3 AC4"}],"child_ids":["ddx-02200b1e","ddx-d9caa6fc","ddx-5011e1a8"],"rationale":"Split the host-scan regression into a seam, fixture migration, and end-to-end guard so each child can land and verify independently without forcing the entire fix through one oversized bead."}

title: Bound default worker-loop tests away from host-wide process scans

acceptance:
1. TestCoordinationContract_Local verifies the new coordination contract against the local implementation using a real bead store and git repository.
2. TestCoordinationContract_Local covers claim contention, tracker transition, landing, and idempotent already-applied outcomes without call-recording mocks.
3. Production coordination code invoked by the local implementation calls existing bead-store claim/transition APIs and the real landing path rather than substituting fake recorders.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCoordinationContract_Local" passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Execution code has no transport-neutral coordination domain contract for claims, lease/transition handling, or landing outcomes, so the later connected and offline implementations cannot share behavioral guarantees.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 construct NewLocalLandCoordinator directly. cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 owns only connection state and an in-memory event buffer, not typed coordination requests, outcomes, or idempotency semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a coordination domain package under cli/internal/agent that defines typed claim/lease, tracker-transition, and landing request/outcome APIs plus a behavioral contract suite. Add the first real local implementation path sufficient for the contract tests to exercise claim contention, tracker transition, landing, and idempotent already-applied outcomes against a real bead store and git repository.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not implement the HTTP coordination client in this bead. Do not implement the durable offline journal or cross-process offline lock here except for interfaces needed by the contract.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.

acceptance:
1. TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot proves claim heartbeat paths avoid raw /tmp when a configured DDx execution/runtime temp root is available.
2. TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping proves two workers for the same project resolve the same claim-liveness root, while different project roots resolve distinct scoped roots.
3. TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot proves existing fallback behavior remains when no configured runtime temp root is available.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot|TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping|TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot" passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Claim heartbeat sidecars currently use raw os.TempDir(), so workers can wedge on /tmp inode exhaustion even when DDx has a configured execution/runtime temp root with available capacity. On 2026-07-07 manual /tmp cleanup restored progress immediately, proving claim liveness depended on the wrong filesystem root.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:31-35 hardcodes claim heartbeat sidecars under `filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), "ddx-claim-heartbeats", ...)` instead of using a DDx execution/runtime temp root when one is configured.
cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:56-64 writes atomic temp files into that raw /tmp-derived directory, so inode exhaustion there fails claims before the worker can use the configured execution root.

PROPOSED FIX
Move claim-liveness heartbeat storage under the configured DDx execution/runtime temp root when available, while keeping a deterministic project-scoped path so workers for the same project on the same machine still observe each other's stale-claim sidecars. Retain the existing raw os.TempDir() fallback only when no configured runtime root exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change bead claim semantics or heartbeat TTL. Do not remove durable worker/evidence history. Do not weaken cross-worker stale-claim protection. Do not change resource threshold values or preflight policy in this bead. Do not add stale tmp cleanup in this bead; that is covered by the cleanup child bead.

acceptance:
1. `ddx doctor` output contains `Plugin Marketplace Skills` and no longer contains `Checking Skill Install Topology`, `Global Install (`, or `Project Install (` in full-scope normal output.
2. A test such as `TestDoctor_ChecksMarketplaceSkillsFromLibraryConfig` creates a package config with required plugins and proves doctor reports configured `ddx`, `helix`, and `easel-skills` entries without hardcoding the normal list.
3. A test such as `TestDoctor_SuggestsMarketplaceInstallCommandsForMissingSkills` proves missing configured plugins print their configured install commands and `ddx doctor` exits 0.
4. A test such as `TestDoctor_UsesVisibleFallbackWhenMarketplaceConfigMissing` proves the fallback includes `ddx`, `helix`, and `easel-skills` and prints a fallback/stale-config warning.
5. Tests such as `TestDoctorScopedToConfigChange` and `TestDoctorScopedSkillValidationIgnoresInstallLayer` assert `ddx doctor --paths .ddx.yml` and skill-file-only scoped runs skip marketplace skill availability checks.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run "TestDoctor_.*Marketplace|TestDoctorScoped|TestDoctorFullScope"` passes.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/...` passes, or any pre-existing provider-orphan deadlock is documented with the narrower passing test set.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doctor` still reports DDx package-layer topology instead of checking whether required marketplace skills/plugins are available. Current code at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:292-298` prints `Checking Skill Install Topology` and calls `reportSkillInstallTopology`; `cli/cmd/doctor.go:432-462` reports `Global Install` and `Project Install` for `.ddx/plugins/ddx`. The new product requirement is for doctor to check configured plugin marketplace skill availability for `ddx`, `helix`, and `easel-skills`, and suggest installing missing ones.

ROOT CAUSE
Doctor is coupled to the old DDx package install model. It has no loader for marketplace requirements from the ddx-library package config and no deterministic availability checker for marketplace-installed skills.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the Skill Install Topology section in `cli/cmd/doctor.go:292-298` with a `Plugin Marketplace Skills` check.
- Load required marketplace plugin requirements from the active ddx library package config added by dependency bead `ddx-7abf3a7a`. Resolve package config from project/global/baked-in DDx package layers, but use those layers only to find config, not as the success signal for skill availability.
- Implement deterministic availability checks from the configured hints, for example scanning explicit skill directories and/or plugin metadata paths declared in config. The exact sources must be documented in the code and tests; do not shell out to `npx` during doctor.
- When config is unreadable or absent, use a small baked-in fallback containing `ddx`, `helix`, and `easel-skills`, and print that fallback requirements are being used so stale config is visible.
- Missing marketplace skills/plugins should be warning-level and exit 0; a broken legacy symlink may remain fatal only if the existing legacy-symlink guard still detects harmful project-local state.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not install marketplace plugins from doctor. Print install commands only.
- Do not hardcode the normal requirement list in doctor when package config is readable.
- Do not remove `ddx install` command registration in this bead; sibling bead `cli: remove top-level ddx install commands` owns that.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-7abf3a7a for typed package config.
title: doctor: check configured marketplace skill availability

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'LandCoordination' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.
- If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, preserve or retry the land/evidence step according to local policy rather than escalating the model.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating, decomposition detection, or power escalation policy in this child.
- Do not reimplement low-level tracker/index contention fixes owned by sibling work.
title: agent: classify land coordination failures as reconciliation

acceptance:
1. TestCoordinationContract_Local verifies claim contention, tracker transition, landing, and idempotent already-applied outcomes against a real bead store and git repository.
2. TestOfflineJournal_OrderedDurableResume verifies crash-safe sequence persistence, acknowledged-through resume, and compaction without dropping an unacknowledged mutation.
3. TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses verifies two real subprocesses cannot overlap the protected mutation window.
4. Production coordination code invokes the existing bead-store claim/transition and real landing paths; no call-recording mock substitutes for them.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(CoordinationContract_Local|OfflineJournal|OfflineCoordinator)" passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
workerprobe knows Connected versus NotConnected and buffers events, but execution code has no transport-neutral coordination abstraction or durable offline mutation journal. Each CLI invocation therefore constructs its own local land coordinator.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 owns only connection state and an in-memory event buffer. cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 construct NewLocalLandCoordinator directly. No durable idempotency-keyed coordination record exists.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a coordination domain package under cli/internal/agent with typed claim/lease, tracker-transition, and landing requests/outcomes.
- Add a durable project-scoped ordered offline journal with monotonic sequence, idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, outcome, and acknowledged-through compaction.
- Add a real local implementation that holds a cross-process project coordination lock and uses existing bead-store/landing implementations.
- Define one behavioral contract suite that runs against the local implementation and the later HTTP implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:dep, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

Deps: ddx-c6317784.

description:
PROBLEM
When a bead in one repo is blocked on work in ANOTHER repo (e.g. niflheim beads blocked on heimq), operators hand-carry 'SKIP these heimq-blocked beads' as prose across sessions and compactions. They wanted to mark them blocked but had to fall back to priority-4 + notes.

CURRENT STATE (from adversarial review — gap is NARROWER than first thought)
- An external-blocker primitive ALREADY exists: cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go has StatusBlocked + a required ExternalBlockerReason, a LifecycleBucketBlockedExternal bucket, an ExternalBlocked() store query (store.go), and a manual recheck workflow (operator reopens when cleared).
- The RESIDUAL gap: the blocker reason is FREE TEXT with MANUAL reopen. There is no STRUCTURED cross-repo reference (<repo>#<bead>) and no AUTOMATED recheck.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (design, not implementation)
- A short design doc specifying: a structured cross-repo blocker reference field (repo + bead id) layered on the existing ExternalBlockerReason, and an automated recheck that flips the bead back to open when the referenced bead closes. Cover: how the recheck observes another repo's tracker (the referenced repo may be local or remote), failure modes, and backward compatibility with free-text reasons.
- Decompose into child build beads with command-based ACs depending on this epic.

NON-SCOPE (for THIS bead)
- No implementation in this bead — design + child beads only.
- Do not replace the existing external-blocker primitive; build ON it.
- No new lifecycle statuses.
title: DESIGN: structured cross-repo blocker ref + auto-recheck

2. TestServiceFinalFailureClassifiesProviderRouteHealth uses fake final events for provider timeout, quota/rate-limit, model unavailable, and harness unavailable; each is normalized into the provider failure taxonomy with retryable vs hard-pin semantics preserved.

5. TestProviderFailureEvidenceNamesFallbackDecision proves the bead/run evidence includes requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, fallback_attempted, and fallback_stop_reason.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestServiceExecuteErrorClassifiesProviderConfigFailures|TestServiceFinalFailureClassifiesProviderRouteHealth|TestUnpinnedWorkerFallsBackAfterTypedProviderFailure|TestPinnedWorkerReportsHardPinExhaustedWithoutWidening|TestProviderFailureEvidenceNamesFallbackDecision' passes.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx/FIZ worker failures can still surface as underspecified execution failures when provider availability, model availability, credentials, endpoint reachability, or harness configuration differs across machines. DDx is the abstraction that should let unattended workers continue through provider variance; a generic provider failure that gives no typed cause, no fallback action, and no durable route-health evidence is a product bug.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:287-290 returns pre-dispatch Fizeau Execute errors as a generic `agent: execute: %w`, leaving the execute loop without a typed provider/model/configuration failure contract.
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:332-376 copies final service error text into Result.Error/ExitCode, but downstream classification still depends on free-text heuristics rather than a required typed route-health outcome for provider unavailable, model unavailable, auth missing, quota/rate limit, endpoint timeout, or harness misconfiguration.
cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:516-544 and :588-612 contain the unpinned route-exclusion/escalation path, but that path only works when the failure is already recognized as provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider; untyped provider/configuration failures can stop or preserve the bead without proving fallback was attempted or intentionally impossible.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define a typed provider failure taxonomy at the DDx/Fizeau boundary: provider_connectivity, provider_auth, provider_rate_limit, provider_quota, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_config_invalid, no_viable_provider, and unknown_provider_failure.
- Normalize both pre-dispatch Execute errors and final failed events into that taxonomy before ExecuteBeadReport construction.
- For unpinned workers, convert retryable provider/model/harness availability failures into route-health evidence and continue fallback/escalation without parking the bead.
- For explicit operator pins, fail loudly with a typed hard-pin exhausted/operator-action report that names the pin and available remediation, without silently widening pins.
- Persist durable evidence on the bead/run showing requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, whether fallback was attempted, and why fallback stopped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not move concrete route selection into DDx; Fizeau still owns routing, aliases, model discovery, and provider catalog behavior. Do not remove explicit operator pins. Do not call real providers in tests.
title: agent: type provider failures and enforce fallback semantics

description:
PROBLEM
Spec saves from the GraphQL API can still overwrite a document when the caller's optimistic concurrency token is stale, and the current mutation schema exposes only path and content. The resolver does rebuild the graph after writes, but there is no explicit stale-write gate or conflict response before the filesystem is mutated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 defines DocumentWrite with only path and content and writes directly with os.WriteFile.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without an expected hash/version input.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:62-89 rebuilds the graph after the write, but only after the filesystem has already been changed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an expected hash/version argument to documentWrite in schema.graphql and thread it into resolver_mutation_docs.go.
- Compare the caller's expected token against the current document before os.WriteFile; if it mismatches, return a conflict instead of overwriting.
- Keep ResolveDocumentPath as the confinement gate for absolute paths, traversal, and symlink escapes.
- Preserve the post-save graph rebuild and return the refreshed document/graph state after a successful write.
- Add focused resolver tests for stale-save refusal, confinement refusal, and graph refresh.

NON-SCOPE
- Hub-to-spoke forwarding is handled by sibling bead ddx-aa88732d's forwarding child and is not part of this child.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql
- cli/internal/server/graphql_mutation_docs_test.go

Out-of-scope (file a separate bead):
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts
title: server: add stale-safe documentWrite version checks and graph refresh

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

description:
PROBLEM
Agents keep tripping the execute-bead one-commit/no-amend contract (the implementation commit is rewritten/amended/rebased after the first commit). Today this is DETECTED post-hoc and the whole attempt is discarded and re-run, wasting long, expensive runs (observed 3047s / 15.3M-token attempts thrown away).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (divergence/ancestry resolution, ~line 187 and ~910-927): a diverged/rewritten candidate is resolved by aborting the claim (detect-then-fail), not by preventing or correcting the rewrite.

PROPOSED FIX
- Prevent or auto-correct the contract violation rather than discarding the attempt: e.g. detect a post-first-commit rewrite before land and reconcile it (or instruct/guard the harness to not amend) so a successful candidate is preserved instead of re-run from scratch.
- This is DISTINCT from the mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale detector in execute_bead.go:~1419 (commit+rationale mismatch). Do NOT target that path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not relax the audit-trail rule (never squash/rebase/filter landed attempt history).
- Do not touch the mixed_commit detector.
- Do not change Fizeau routing.

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Make execute-bead auto-comply with the one-commit/no-amend contract

Plumb the sibling-guard's operator-promotion determination into the entry construction site so operator_override=true is written when readiness skipped a downgrade because the bead was operator-promoted, and false otherwise. description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-8a78ee90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Execute-bead agents are instructed to run `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then run `git commit`. The repository-installed Git pre-commit hook invokes Lefthook again on the same staged tree. During attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4`, a small containment patch ran the full staged `./cmd` plus `./internal/agent` race gate twice explicitly after one test correction, then `git commit` launched a third full pass on the identical final staged tree. The second explicit pass alone spent more than 18 minutes in `go test` output processing. This duplicate gate contract serializes the queue without adding evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2104-2113` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2137-2148` require rich and embedded agents to rerun `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then commit exactly once, without recognizing that the implementation commit invokes the installed hook.
- `lefthook.yml:205-216` maps staged Go files to package-wide race tests, so each duplicate invocation can repeat multi-minute `./cmd` and `./internal/agent` gates.
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit:11-18` invokes Lefthook for ordinary `git commit`, making a separately requested manual invocation redundant when the staged tree is unchanged.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the ordinary implementation commit hook the single authoritative staged pre-commit gate. Update both execute-bead prompt variants and integrity evidence so agents stage the exact commit set, run `git commit` normally, and treat the hook output/exit status from that commit as satisfying the bead’s `lefthook run pre-commit` acceptance criterion. If a workflow explicitly runs the hook before commit, reuse that success only when a content/config fingerprint proves the staged tree and hook inputs are identical; otherwise rerun. A failed hook must abort the commit and remain a failed gate.

NON-SCOPE
Using `--no-verify`, setting `LEFTHOOK=0`, weakening or removing repository hooks, reusing evidence after any staged-tree or hook-config change, caching across attempts, bypassing human commits, or changing which tests the hook selects.

PARENT
None; root worker-throughput regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-2afca9c9` (test-output/runtime headroom) and `ddx-03568f89` (gate evidence); no implementation dependency.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-53df5e2f is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postg
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

description:
PROBLEM
The concrete storage implementations are still publicly importable from `cli/internal/bead/`, so the compiler does not yet enforce the storage boundary TD-027 §21 wants. `cli/internal/bead/store.go:39-140` still defines the public `Store` constructor path, and `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:35-152` still assumes the concrete type lives in the public package.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go:39-140` keeps `Store`, `NewStore`, and `NewStoreWithCollection` in the public package alongside the implementation details for backend selection. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:12-100` is the sibling lifecycle implementation, but this bead only covers the storage side. The result is that `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonBackend`, and the store internals remain reachable outside the package boundary.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move `store.go`, `backend_jsonl.go`, `backend_external.go`, `axon_backend.go`, `claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, and `migrate.go` into `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` with package rename to `storage`.
- Move `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` into the same internal storage package.
- Add `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the public constructor surface for `NewStore` and `NewStoreWithCollection`; keep it delegating to `internal/storage`.
- Add the compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` inside `internal/storage/` so the concrete type proves interface satisfaction after privatization.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller-field narrowing.
- WatcherHub relocation to `internal/lifecycle`.
- Boundary import test.
- Lint analyzer cleanup.
title: BL storage: privatize concrete bead backends behind internal/storage and add public factories

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-6a2ce3bd is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-owned temporary homes, provider shims, and helper scratch are created directly under raw os.TempDir(), so /tmp remains the choke point even when DDx has a configured execution/runtime temp root with available space. The 2026-07-08 incident had /tmp at 100% inode use while the configured execution worktree root under /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt still had capacity.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:91 creates provider shims with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-provider-shim-").
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29 and runner_test.go:56 create ddx-home-* with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-"); equivalent command tests create the same prefix in cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:80, cli/cmd/try_test.go:823, and cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:85.
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:116 creates ddx-fixture-bin-* with os.MkdirTemp("", ...).
These producers bypass config.ExecutionScratchRoot / config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot and strand inode-heavy scratch under /tmp.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared helper for DDx-owned scratch temp dirs that prefers config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot) or the configured execution/runtime root when a project root is available, and falls back to os.TempDir() only when no configured root exists. Migrate provider shims and DDx-owned temp homes/fixture bins to the helper where production/runtime context is available. Keep tests hermetic by setting the helper root explicitly in tests.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go
- cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go or a new scratch-temp helper package
- relevant tests in cli/internal/agent and cli/cmd

NON-SCOPE
Do not move claim-liveness in this bead; ddx-3986b89a covers claim heartbeat root selection. Do not change git worktree placement. Do not store long-lived user config in execution scratch. Do not require a project root for all tests; fallback behavior must remain.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestProviderShimUsesDDXScratchRoot proves EnsureProviderShimOnPATH can create ddx-provider-shim-* under the configured scratch root rather than raw /tmp.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestDDXScratchTempDirUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot|TestDDXScratchTempDirFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutConfiguredRoot|TestProviderShimUsesDDXScratchRoot" passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. Audit report committed at `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/work-try-nomenclature-audit.md` listing every remaining `work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead` hit under `cli/`, `docs/`, `.agents/skills/`, and `.claude/skills/`, classified as renamed, retained-compat, durable-key, historical-doc, generated, or follow-up.
2. `TestTerminology_WorkTryPublicSurface` (new or renamed in the appropriate cmd/server test package) verifies public help and server/UI-facing labels prefer `ddx work` / `ddx try`, while legacy `ddx agent work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` are described only as compatibility aliases.
3. `TestTerminology_DurableLegacyKeysDocumented` verifies retained durable keys such as `work-retry-after`, `work-last-status`, and `work-last-detail` have explicit comments/docs explaining why they remain legacy-named.
4. All non-compatibility production symbols introduced for the FEAT-010 path are renamed from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary, or each exception is listed in the audit report with a compatibility reason.
5. Related open beads ddx-387a0178, ddx-29058e2a, and ddx-1a9cc01f are updated, superseded, or explicitly cross-referenced so no open bead still instructs future work to create new `ExecuteLoop*` names without a compatibility rationale.
6. `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead" cli docs .agents/skills .claude/skills` returns only audit-approved retained compatibility/durable/historical hits documented in `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/work-try-nomenclature-audit.md`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestTerminology|TestWork|TestTry|TestExecuteLoop|TestExecuteBead" -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This plan retains ddx agent compatibility aliases and legacy cli/internal/agent package targets, so it must not dispatch.
title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

description:
PROBLEM
Even once intake.blocked entries carry rule_fingerprint and operator_override, operators cannot see those fields because `ddx bead show` does not render them in the intake.blocked list section.

ROOT CAUSE
- The intake.blocked rendering path in `ddx bead show` (cli/cmd/bead_show.go and its formatter helpers) prints only outcome and detail strings per entry; it does not read or print rule_fingerprint or operator_override.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the `ddx bead show` formatter for intake.blocked entries to read and render rule_fingerprint and operator_override per entry. Keep the rendering compact and stable so existing snapshot/golden tests can be updated deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Defining or computing rule_fingerprint (sibling child bead).
- Defining or propagating operator_override (sibling child bead).

- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.

- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on both sibling child beads (fingerprint+dedup, operator_override) so the fields exist on entries to render.

description:
PROBLEM
Git lock recovery can remove a replacement lock after another contender already won stale cleanup and acquired it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:141-189 separates stale inspection from pathname deletion. The path can be replaced between those steps. Phase 0 WB-5 requires the same atomic stale-break rule at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:124-132.

PROPOSED FIX
Atomically rename the observed stale lock to a contender-owned tombstone, remove only that tombstone after validation, and restart acquisition on a lost rename. Preserve live-owner refusal and current diagnostics.

NON-SCOPE

title: gitlock: make stale index-lock breaking single-winner

acceptance:
1. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_WorkerStartStopRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued start/stop for a spoke project creates/stops exactly one spoke-local worker and no hub-local phantom worker.
2. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_BeadMutationRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued bead create/edit for a spoke project mutates the spoke project and not the hub project.
3. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_DocumentWriteRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves hub-issued `documentWrite` for a spoke `docs/helix/**` path writes only under the spoke root and preserves/returns forwarding metadata where available.
4. At least one live write path includes an offline-spoke refusal assertion with no phantom hub-local row.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Federation|FanOut|RouteMutation|ForwardMutation|Spoke' -count=1` passes.\n6. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` passes, or the new sibling e2e file named in this bead passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The federation suite proves hub/spoke registration, federated reads, offline badges, restart recovery, and mocked UI mutation states, but it does not yet prove the most important live owner-targeted write contract: commands issued from a hub for a spoke-owned project must execute on the spoke and must not create hub-local phantom state.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:34-39 says FEAT-026/ADR-007 require owner-targeted federation writes and that hub/spoke writes must never broadcast.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarding for `startWorker`, `stopWorker`, bead mutations, and `documentWrite`, with the hub never creating local fallback state for a spoke write.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:335-337 requires spoke worker start/stop forwarding and offline refusal.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:350 and :363 require federated bead and document writes.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307 currently exercises live two-node reads/offline/restart but not live owner-targeted write persistence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the live multi-process federation coverage, preferably in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or a sibling e2e file, to start one hub and one spoke with isolated temp roots and per-node XDG state.
- From the hub, issue a worker start/stop for a spoke project and assert the worker exists in the spoke project worker registry, not the hub registry.
- From the hub, create or edit a bead in the spoke project and assert the spoke `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes while the hub project does not receive a phantom local bead.
- From the hub, write a `docs/helix/**` document in the spoke project and assert the file changes on the spoke root only and origin/forwarding metadata is observable where the API exposes it.
- Include an offline-spoke refusal assertion for at least one write path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add real ts-net/MagicDNS coverage; loopback hub/spoke is the autonomous local gate.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028; this bead covers FEAT-026 inbound spoke owner writes only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on the stale-bead reconciliation bead so the implementation target is narrowed before coding.
title: federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke

acceptance:
1. TestBeadAutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock (or equivalent) runs two or more concurrent bead update/create operations with auto_commit=always against the same repo and proves all complete without .git/index.lock errors.
2. The same regression asserts commit history is audit-correct: each auto-commit either contains only the operation named in its subject or is an explicitly named combined commit that lists every included tracker mutation.
3. Existing stale-lock recovery tests TestBeadCreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, TestBeadUpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, and TestBeadCloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBead(AutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock|CreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|UpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|CloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock)' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/gitlock/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.

4. `ddx bead recheck-blockers --id <bead-id> --json` and the no-flag (whole-store) form are wired in cli/cmd/bead.go and covered by TestBeadRecheckBlockersCommand in cli/cmd/bead_test.go, asserting the reopened bead's status is "open" and Extra no longer carries the ref or reason after a successful recheck.

description:
PROBLEM
A bead marked status=blocked with a structured cross-repo ref (ddx-438d7ee3) never auto-reopens; an operator must remember to check the other repo and run `ddx bead update <id> --status open --manual-reopen`.

ROOT CAUSE
No code path resolves a lifecycle-cross-repo-blocker-ref against another local repo's bead store and no command triggers the check. cli/internal/bead/store.go:2560 (ExternalBlocked) only lists blocked beads; it does not resolve their refs.

PROPOSED FIX (see docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-040-cross-repo-blocker-recheck.md §4 for full contract)
- New cli/internal/bead/recheck.go: a function that, given a Store and a known-repos config, iterates Store.ExternalBlocked(), parses each bead's lifecycle-cross-repo-blocker-ref (ddx-438d7ee3), and for `path:`-type known-repos entries opens a Store rooted at the resolved path's .ddx/ directory and calls Store.Get(bead) on the referenced ID.
- If the resolved target bead's status satisfies bead.LifecycleStatusSatisfiesDependency (i.e. == StatusClosed), transition the blocked bead blocked -> open via the existing ManualReopen path (lifecycle.go:113, ValidateLifecycleTransition with ManualReopen: true) with Actor "system:cross-repo-recheck" and Source set to the resolving repo name, clearing ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason and the ref exactly as today's manual reopen clears the reason (store.go:778). Append an event recording the source bead/repo and observed remote status.
- If the repo is unresolvable (not in known-repos, path missing, or target bead ID not found), leave the bead blocked and report the specific reason; do not retry-loop or auto-cancel.
- New CLI command cli/cmd/bead.go `ddx bead recheck-blockers [--id <bead-id>] [--json]` that runs the above over the whole store or a single bead.
- Node/project (federation) known-repos entries are out of scope here; skip them with an "unresolvable: federation not yet supported" result (child bead 3 of ddx-3b5ab8aa implements that path).

NON-SCOPE
- Federation-backed (node/project) resolution — separate child bead.
- Wiring into the `ddx work` drain loop pre-pass — file as a follow-up once this command lands and is validated standalone.
