description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
- `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
- Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).
title: perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation

acceptance:
1. `cli/internal/server/server.go` `New()` constructs a `WorkerSupervisor` alongside `WorkerManager` and stores it on the Server struct.
2. `ListenAndServe()` (or a `Start()` helper called by it) launches a background goroutine that ticks `supervisor.Reconcile()`. Tick interval defaults to 10s and is overridable via `DDX_SUPERVISOR_TICK` (duration string).
3. `Shutdown()` cancels the supervisor context and blocks on goroutine exit before returning.
4. New file `cli/cmd/worker.go` exposes `ddx worker set/status/enable` subcommands. `set` and `enable` write `.ddx/workers/desired.json` via `supervisor.SaveDesiredState`. `status` prints desired count + current running managed workers.

Test `TestServer_SupervisorTickTriggersReconcile` in `cli/internal/server/server_supervisor_test.go` (or extension of existing supervisor test file) proves: (a) a Server started with `desired_count=1` and no running worker calls `Reconcile` within one tick and starts a worker; (b) after `Shutdown`, no supervisor goroutine remains (verify via `runtime.NumGoroutine` delta or a `sync.WaitGroup` count).
6. Test `TestWorkerCmd_SetPersistsDesiredState` in `cli/cmd/worker_test.go` proves `ddx worker set --project <tmpDir> --count 2` writes valid `.ddx/workers/desired.json` that round-trips through `LoadDesiredState`.
7. Existing tests `TestWorkerDesired*`, `TestWorkerReconcile*`, `TestWorkerRestart*` in `cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go` remain green unchanged.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run 'Worker|worker|Supervisor|Reconcile' -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
# PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` is currently a foreground process bound to the operator's terminal. When the terminal detaches (SIGHUP), the operator Ctrl-C's, or a transient git-index race trips the durable-audit commit, the worker dies and NOTHING restarts it. Queue drain stalls until the operator manually restarts. Observed live 2026-07-02: two consecutive worker deaths in one session, 17 ready beads stalled ~2h waiting.

The P0 epic `ddx-9d1af129` (server: supervise project workers from durable desired state) documents this. Phase 1 of `docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md` requires: desired-state model + persistence + reconcile loop.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:38-533` — the entire Phase 1 code exists: `WorkerDesiredState` model (line 38), `Validate` (line 109), `WorkerSupervisor` struct (line 147), `NewWorkerSupervisor` (line 157), `SaveDesiredState`/`LoadDesiredState` (line 174/204), `Reconcile`/`ReconcileAt` (line 229/234), `restartBackoffDelay` (line 437), `pruneTimeWindow` (line 468). All unit-tested at `cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go` (316 lines).

But `Reconcile()` is NEVER CALLED in production. `rg 'Reconcile\(\)' cli/ | grep -v _test` returns zero hits. `cli/internal/server/server.go:175` constructs a `WorkerManager` but never wraps it in a `WorkerSupervisor`. No background reconcile goroutine exists. No CLI writes `desired.json`.

# PROPOSED FIX
Wire the existing supervisor into server lifecycle:

1. **Bootstrap**: in `cli/internal/server/server.go` `New()` (after `workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)` at line 175), construct `supervisor := NewWorkerSupervisor(workers)` and hold it on the Server struct.

2. **Reconcile goroutine**: in `ListenAndServe` (line 311) or a new `Start` helper, spawn a background goroutine that calls `supervisor.Reconcile()` on a `time.Ticker` (10s default, configurable via env `DDX_SUPERVISOR_TICK`). Guard with a context cancelled by `Shutdown`.

3. **Shutdown integration**: in `Shutdown()` (line 285), cancel the reconcile context and wait for the goroutine before returning.

4. **CLI surface**: new `cli/cmd/worker.go` with subcommands:
   - `ddx worker set --project <path> --count N [--harness X] [--model Y] [--restart-enabled=true]` — writes `.ddx/workers/desired.json` via `supervisor.SaveDesiredState`. Works standalone (no server needed) since save is filesystem-only.
   - `ddx worker status [--project <path>] [--json]` — reads `desired.json` + queries running workers.
   - `ddx worker enable [--project <path>]` — shortcut for `--count 1 --restart-enabled=true`.

5. **Tests** (see AC).

# NON-SCOPE
- Subprocess boundary + process-group cleanup (`ddx-1c865bdb`, `ddx-24a3da84`, `ddx-096129b9`) — Phase 2, separate beads.
- GraphQL/UI wiring (`ddx-cf0cafef`, `ddx-2b15342f`) — Phase 3.
- Docker leak-proof (`ddx-6ae5134b`) — Phase 4.
- Dogfood migration (`ddx-f6609c33`) — Phase 5.
- ADR-022 amendment — track separately.

# IMPACT
After this bead lands and the operator sets `desired_count=1` via `ddx worker enable` and restarts `ddx server`, worker death (crash, SIGKILL, transient git race, terminal detach) is followed by automatic restart within one supervisor tick, honoring `WorkerRestartPolicy` backoff. The user no longer has to manually restart.
title: server: wire WorkerSupervisor reconcile loop into server lifecycle (Phase 1 of ddx-9d1af129)

description:
PROBLEM
When pre_claim_intake produces a warn-only rejection with the same fingerprint repeatedly (for example a readiness schema mismatch), the worker logs a warning and goes idle every cycle (pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle). With many ready beads and one daemon, none get claimed and the worker looks alive while making zero progress, leaving the queue idle indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE
- warn-only policy mode masks the failure: the loop emits pre_claim_intake.warn at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1859, :1982, :2009, :2034, :2073, each carrying a fingerprint computed in cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-24, and then goes loop.idle (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1637-1649) with no guard counting consecutive identical fingerprints across distinct bead IDs to break the silent loop.
- The threshold knob exists at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:76-79 (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero means DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) and the escalation message format at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1141.

PROPOSED FIX
After N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason "preclaim_warn_repeated", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

DEPENDENCIES: no deps.

description:
PROBLEM: `ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>` can create `child -> parent` or `child -> ancestor` edges, which deadlocks decomposed parents because the parent is waiting on children while children wait on the parent chain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2848` implements `Store.DepAdd` with existence, self-edge, and dependency-cycle checks only; it never walks `Bead.Parent` ancestry before appending the dependency at `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2888`. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328` wires `ddx bead create` and accepts `--parent` at `cli/cmd/bead.go:355`, but creation-time dependency handling does not share a parent-ancestor invariant with `DepAdd`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a central tracker validation helper that builds the parent chain for the dependent bead or pending create request and hard-rejects any dependency whose target is the bead's parent or any ancestor. Use the helper from `Store.DepAdd` and from `ddx bead create --depends-on ... --parent ...` handling. Error text must name the offending ancestor and include the parent chain in stable order. Preserve normal dependency cycle detection for non-parent graph cycles.

NON-SCOPE: Do not change decomposition semantics for valid `parent -> child` edges. Do not auto-repair existing tracker rows in this bead; repair belongs to the doctor bead. Do not change work-loop retry policy or root-mutation logging. Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:63` defines decomposed parent state; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:182` defines the decomposition-first transition sequence.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

First-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus "lazy-resolves-to-global" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the "Global Install (...) — <status>" and "Project Install (...) — <status>" lines (doctor.go:447-448).
- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.
3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing" and "Project Install (<projectRoot>/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global".
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).
title: init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting

description:
PROBLEM
Server shutdown must clean every server-owned managed worker process tree, not only background hubs and land coordinators. A graceful server shutdown can currently leave ddx work or harness descendants running.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:269-283 documents Shutdown as closing background services, then calls s.beadHub.Close and s.workers.LandCoordinators.StopAll. It does not stop WorkerManager work workers. cli/internal/server/workers.go:218-220 stores live worker handles, but there is no manager shutdown pass that iterates those handles and applies the Stop/reap process-tree cleanup path.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an idempotent WorkerManager shutdown method that snapshots server-owned managed work handles and on-disk running server-owned records, invokes the shared managed process-tree cleanup path, releases any claimed bead for those workers, and persists terminal stopped status. Call it from Server.Shutdown before or alongside LandCoordinators.StopAll so server shutdown cleans all managed worker process groups.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.
title: server: shut down all server-owned managed worker process trees

description:
PROBLEM
  Server-managed workers can remain absent even when .ddx/workers/desired.json has desired_count=1. On 2026-07-07, /home/erik/Projects/ddx had desired_count=1 and /home/erik/Projects/niflheim had desired_count=1, but ddx work status --all-projects --json showed only axon and helix workers. Both ddx and niflheim had older status=operator_attention worker records, while the current desired state still asked the supervisor to run one worker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:364-374 records every restart-blocking terminal worker into blockedTerminals. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:417-419 then makes canStartMore return false whenever blockedTerminals is non-empty. There is no expiry, no check that the underlying dirty/root condition has been resolved, and no operator command/state transition that clears the block. Because snapshotWorkers reads historical terminal records from .ddx/workers, the block can survive server restarts and suppress a fresh desired worker indefinitely.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:364-419: distinguish active unresolved operator-attention stops from stale historical records. A clean project root with a current desired state must be able to start again after the operator resolves the condition.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-61f6c57d is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",
    "detail":"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string",
    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",
    "suggested_action":"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry",
    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }
- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.
- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 5
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx governing documentation still describes decomposed parent beads as open, execution-ineligible containers that depend on generated children. That contradicts the desired lifecycle contract: a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition, while children remain separately executable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- The same contract is referenced by docs/beads.md, docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md, and FEAT-010 decomposition text where present.

PROPOSED FIX
Update docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and matching FEAT-010 decomposition text so they state one normative rule: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Preserve traceability guidance through child Parent metadata and decomposition events, but state that Parent is not a dependency gate and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent. Explicitly describe historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers as legacy/backfill cases only.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab.
Governing artifacts: docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, FEAT-010.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Deps: No deps. The related ddx-130cf9ab work can land independently; this bead must preserve the no-parent-dependency invariant in docs.
title: docs: make decomposition contract close lossless parents

Verify whether claim-time enforcement exists and whether the roll-up path skips validation.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed command and the upgraded writing skill.
title: try: attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts

description:
PROBLEM
store.go:Reopen (lines 1664-1665) and store.go:Unclaim (lines 1065-1067) maintain parallel loops that delete the same set of claim metadata Extra keys. This duplication is error-prone: if ClaimMetadataExtraKeys is extended, both loops must be updated.

ROOT CAUSE
store.go:Reopen clears claim fields in isolation; store.go:Unclaim does the same separately. Both iterate ClaimMetadataExtraKeys but the operations are not factored into a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a private helper func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) that deletes all entries in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys from the Extra map.
2. Update store.go:Reopen:1664-1665 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
3. Update store.go:Unclaim:1064-1069 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
4. Verify both methods also call RemoveClaimHeartbeat as before (Unclaim does; Reopen should also).

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the set of keys being cleared. Do not refactor heartbeat cleanup.

2. TestBuiltinDDxPackageResolvesFromDefaultPluginFallback proves registry consumers can resolve built-in DDx resources through the embedded defaultplugin fallback without .ddx/plugins/ddx.
3. TestRegistryDistinguishesLocalOverlayFromMarketplacePackage proves explicit local overlays remain supported and are not confused with marketplace/cache-backed generated adapter behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
The registry/defaultplugin layer still exposes .ddx/plugins/ddx as if it were the forward install target for the built-in DDx package. That makes compatibility metadata look like the normal marketplace/cache-backed topology and leaves tests asserting the wrong package shape.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27 says generated adapters and payload copies are not forward repository state.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go:75-82 and cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/package.yaml:14-22 still expose install.root.target=.ddx/plugins/ddx for the built-in ddx package, while registry comments treat it as compatibility-only metadata.
- cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:60-68 asserts the legacy .ddx/plugins/ddx target.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust registry/defaultplugin package metadata and tests so .ddx/plugins/ddx is either removed from forward assertions or explicitly classified as local-overlay/legacy compatibility metadata. Add tests that prove the built-in DDx package resolves through plugin lock/cache/defaultplugin fallback and that normal registry behavior does not require a project payload copy.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for explicit local development overlays. Do not remove the embedded default plugin fallback needed for offline ddx init. Do not change HELIX marketplace packaging.

PARENT
ddx-602ae31a

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27
title: registry: separate built-in DDx overlays from forward install metadata

description:
PROBLEM: After `ddxroot.Path($HOME)` stops returning `~/.ddx/`, existing incorrectly-created home state such as `~/.ddx/server/` and `~/.ddx/tsnet/` becomes orphaned. Operators need an explicit startup signal so stale TLS or tsnet state does not keep confusing client/server trust debugging.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` previously allowed `~/.ddx/` to act as a project state root. High-risk server consumers include TLS cert/key state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:326` and tsnet state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:503`. Once the resolver falls through to XDG, those old home directories are no longer read by the server path helpers.

PROPOSED FIX: Add startup detection in the server path that checks for legacy `JoinHome("server")` and `JoinHome("tsnet")` directories when the active project state root resolves elsewhere. Emit a clear warning naming the orphan source and the active XDG/project state destination. Keep the behavior warning-only unless the implementation already has an established safe migration helper.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not silently delete legacy home-state directories. Do not move unrelated user-global state such as `installed.yaml` or `plugins/`.

acceptance: See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.
description:
**The bug**

`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in
one of two locations based on whether `<projectRoot>/.ddx/` exists as a
directory:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
        return inTree    // <projectRoot>/.ddx/
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>
}
```

The `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named
`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home
directory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's
where user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the
parallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made
with `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`
override), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a
project root.

Result: the same logical state files appear at two different paths
depending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.
The concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS
cert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`
(via systemd) wrote its cert at
`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate
invocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote
`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same
filename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some
other client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got
`tls: bad certificate`.

**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**

`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites
across the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The
highest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT
diverge between locations):

- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,
  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,
  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)
- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)
- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)
- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)
- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)
- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)
- `workers/<id>/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,
  `executions_mirror.go:211`)
- `executions/<attempt-id>/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,
  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)
- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)
- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,
  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)
- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)
- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)
- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)
- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)

Anything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`
arguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.

**Proposed fix (structural)**

Tighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but
not sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization
marker: `<projectRoot>/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by
`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir
        return inTree
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root
}

func isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {
    info, err := os.Stat(dir)
    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
        return false
    }
    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "config.yaml"))
    return err == nil
}
```

`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.

This makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to
`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>` (since `~/.ddx/` has no
`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.

**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**

A surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`
tests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare
`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old "any `.ddx/` = project root"
contract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`
run:

- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,
  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)

Files that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`
in the `.ddx/`:

- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)
- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`
- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`
- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`
- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)

A central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)
that creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.

**Migration**

Existing user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.
gets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`
returned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition
is graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but
operators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot
migration. Recommend:

1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the
   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.
2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host-id>/server/`
   on first server start under the new resolver.

**Operational note**

The original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the
entire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir
cert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.
That's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.

**Severity: P1**

The bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as
"server unreachable" rather than "bead tracker corrupted" is that
nobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files
recently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses
`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test
   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`
   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.
2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write
   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).
3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.
4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /
   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.
5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.

**Out of scope**

- Renaming `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/` (separate cleanup).
- Multi-project federation collision (different concern, different
  layer).
- The TLS-renew schedule / rotation logic (orthogonal — the bug is
  not "the cert was rotated"; it's "two carts exist").

description:
PROBLEM
DDx receives harness identity from Fizeau inventory but rewrites selected known names through a DDx-owned branding registry. That makes display stale for new harnesses and gives DDx a second concrete harness catalog. Inventory names must remain opaque Fizeau facts and appear verbatim on provider summary/detail presentation surfaces.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/inventory_service.go:10-19,37-45 defines the Fizeau-only inventory seam and cli/internal/server/providers.go:111-135,450-468 lists it. Summary/detail builders at providers.go:247-273 and 276-310 preserve canonical info.Name in Harness but fabricate DisplayName. providers.go:709-733 hard-codes concrete branding and providers_test.go:424-446 locks that registry behavior into tests. Fizeau HarnessInfo exposes Name, not a separate DDx display label.

PROPOSED FIX
Set summary and detail DisplayName directly from info.Name and delete harnessDisplayName plus its concrete registry. Test arbitrary future names and mixed-case/whitespace-bearing values to prove DDx performs no lookup, branding, canonicalization, trimming, or fallback. Exercise the production provider summary/detail data path shared by REST, MCP, and the frontend. Keep GraphQL provider-model identity unchanged because it already uses canonical Fizeau inventory names.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change inventory membership, identity, defaults, eligibility, RouteStatus facts, GraphQL provider-model behavior, Fizeau names, routing, execution constraints, pricing, billing, cost, quota, or evidence caps. Do not infer capability or eligibility from displayed strings.

PARENT
ddx-1b3272a6.

DEPS
ddx-d7800ef7 establishes that server inventory membership and identity come only from Fizeau listings.
title: server: display Fizeau harness inventory names verbatim

description:
CONTEXT
This is an explicit follow-up, not part of the first executable slice. Once `ddx doc prose --changed` has landed and advisory noise is known, DDx can expose prose findings inside bead review or a review hook.

IN-SCOPE FILES

- docs updates for review integration

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR

- Findings remain advisory by default.
- Review output must distinguish prose-quality findings from acceptance/correctness findings.
- The integration must reuse the same checker/rule/config path as `ddx doc prose`.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Making prose findings globally blocking.
- Changing work selection or closure semantics based only on prose findings.
title: Integrate prose findings into bead review as opt-in advisory output

description:
PROBLEM
The CLI entry points that should consume the broadened classifier already exist, but the repo only proves them with explicit worktree-shaped candidates. `ddx cleanup --apply` goes through `runCleanup` in `cli/cmd/cleanup.go:43-87`, and startup pre-claim cleanup goes through `defaultStartupProcessCleanup` in `cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go:17-37`, yet neither path has an end-to-end test for a reparented descendant under the execution worktree root.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:76-120` and `cli/cmd/work_startup_cleanup_test.go:122-176` cover ordinary `.execute-bead-wt-*` fixtures only. There is no assertion that the broadened scanner from the sibling agent bead is actually invoked by both surfaces.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cleanup integration coverage that writes a cwd-only descendant and proves `ddx cleanup --apply` removes it.
- Add startup pre-claim coverage that runs `work` once and proves the same descendant is reaped before claim.
- Keep the command behavior unchanged aside from the broadened reap.
- Depend on the classifier bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change provider launch behavior.
- Do not add a new worker status scanner.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-037 prohibits numeric retry floors on beads. Retry escalation currently stores numeric `MinPower` floors in bead `Extra` through `TriagePowerHintKey`, and later reads them as dispatch input.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:290` defines `numericPowerFloorHint` and reads bead `Extra`.
- `cli/cmd/try.go:290` applies numeric bead floors to `requestMinPower`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3563`, `4119`, and `4189` write numeric retry floors through `TriagePowerHintKey`.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete `numericPowerFloorHint` and every call to it. Remove numeric floor writes from no-changes retry, route-exclusion retry, ladder-exhaustion handling, and any related tests. Keep retry escalation as execution policy/evidence inside the current attempt path; do not replace the removed behavior with another bead `Extra` key.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_route_exclusion_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_repair_escalation_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the new `triage.estimated_difficulty` parser beyond using it as the initial difficulty input.
- Do not add any new bead-level retry key.
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or concrete provider/model fallback.
title: work: remove numeric retry floors from bead metadata

description:
PROBLEM: FEAT-004 §Queue Semantics For Epics (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:134-136,158-161) requires the ddx work idle-path remediator to fire a "closure cascade" auto-action that closes epics whose children are all terminal — and forbids the loop from skipping/exiting while ready epics remain. Today the loop only REPORTS EpicClosureCandidates (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3432,3461; cmd/work.go:345) and SKIPS them as epic_container (execute_bead_loop.go:3694-3695). The recovery hook (internal/agent/recovery_hook.go:71-94) dispatches reframe/decompose/park but has NO closure action. So an all-children-closed epic is reported and skipped, never closed — a FEAT-010 reliability-contract violation.

ROOT CAUSE: no idle-path step invokes the closure path; recovery_hook.go lacks a closure-cascade remediation.

PROPOSED FIX: Add an idle-path remediation step (before declaring "no execution-eligible beads") that calls Store.EpicClosureCandidates(ctx) (store.go:1702) and closes each via Store.Close (which already emits the epic_auto_close audit event and runs the walk-up, including the cancelled-terminal + all-cancelled-cancels refinement from the CloseWithEvidence bead). Idempotent: closed epics drop out of EpicClosureCandidates so a second pass closes nothing new. Record closures in the work-log snapshot (reuse EpicClosureCandidatesCount, execute_bead_loop.go:3461).

NON-SCOPE: the CloseWithEvidence hook (separate bead); reconcile; recursion.
title: epic-autoclose: add idle-path closure-cascade remediation to ddx work

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0. Fizeau just shipped v0.14.1 with three routing-reliability fixes that together resolve the queue-stall problems we hit all session:

1. Bounded TCP dial timeout (5 s) for openai-compat clients — closes the gap that v0.14.0 left open (the IsDialError short-circuit was wired but the dial itself was still ~30s). This addresses fizeau-b5757624.

2. Policy-aware scoring under MinPower/MaxPower bounds — `--min-power 8` now correctly prefers cheaper in-bounds subscription candidates over metered higher-power candidates. Without this, ladder-aware routing in ddx-8a7a6843 (landed) does not consistently pick the right provider when both are eligible.

3. Proactive endpoint aliveness probing + ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — startup TCP-connect probe and background re-probe loop record per-provider reachability. The routing gate filters known-unreachable providers with FilterReasonEndpointUnreachable instead of dialing them every request. PersistRouteHealth (optional, path-based) persists probe results across ddx process boundaries, so a fresh ddx-try does not re-discover that bragi is dead. This addresses fizeau-7cd66e6b.

Until this bump lands, autorouting on this machine still pays ~5s per dead-provider dial on every new ddx-try, the cheap subscription is not always picked when in-power-bounds, and provider health is rediscovered fresh on each invocation. With the bump, the routing chain is end-to-end correct.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/go.mod line: `github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0`. Needs to be v0.14.1.

PROPOSED FIX
- `go get github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.14.1` from cli/, regenerate go.sum.
- `go build ./...` green.
- `go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Cooldown|Retry|Routing|Provider|Land|Lock" -count=1` green (smoke).
- Single commit, `chore(deps): bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1`.
- DOES NOT wire ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — that requires the DDxRoot epic (ddx-602ae31a) to settle on a path. Follow-up bead can wire it once DDxRoot lands. The bump itself unblocks the dial-timeout and policy-scoring fixes immediately; PersistRouteHealth defaults to off in v0.14.1 so the bump is safe without DDx-side wiring.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring PersistRouteHealth.
- Any DDx-side routing/escalation changes.
- Changes to provider configuration.

DEPS
None. This is a pure dependency bump.

TOP PRIORITY because every running drain on this machine pays the v0.14.0 dial-timeout cost on every dead-provider attempt, and the in-flight epic (ddx-602ae31a) child beads will reference the new behaviour as the baseline.
title: deps: bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1 — dial timeout, policy-aware scoring, endpoint probing

3. A fixture representing an existing valid tracker graph passes `ddx bead doctor` without `--fix` and without regression findings. Test: TestBeadDoctorValidGraphSnapshotClean.

4. Verification gate passes: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestBeadDoctorDetectsParentAncestorBackEdge|TestBeadDoctorRepairsParentAncestorBackEdge|TestBeadDoctorValidGraphSnapshotClean' -count=1`.

description:
PROBLEM: Existing trackers can already contain `child -> parent` or `child -> ancestor` dependency edges. Without a diagnostic and repair path, those trackers remain wedged even after future writers stop producing bad edges.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `ddx bead doctor` is wired in `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:31` and delegates normal scan/fix to `bead.BeadDoctor` / `bead.BeadDoctorFix` at `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:110`, but `cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:34` only scans oversized fields. It never builds a parent graph or reports dependency edges that intersect the parent chain.

PROPOSED FIX: Extend bead doctor with a `parent_ancestor_in_deps` check that scans all bead rows, walks each bead's parent chain, and reports any dependency target that equals the direct parent or any ancestor. JSON output must be stable and scriptable. `--fix` must remove only offending dependency edges, preserve unrelated deps and events, and remain idempotent. Keep existing oversized-field and dangling-success behavior intact.

NON-SCOPE: Do not modify decomposition prompts. Do not change `ddx work` retry behavior. Do not manually edit tracker storage outside the doctor implementation. Do not remove valid `parent -> child` dependencies.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:75` makes AC mapping load-bearing; this diagnostic preserves existing child work without lossy tracker rewrites.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.
title: Detect and repair parent ancestor deps in bead doctor

acceptance:
1. readiness_checks JSON schema is defined at cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json with verdict as oneOf boolean|string|null, and is the single source of truth both producer and consumer reference; verified by TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go.
2. The consumer decoder references the shared schema path constant (readinessChecksSchemaPath, cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21) and drift between the code contract and the schema fails TestReadinessChecksSchema.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Schema drift between the readiness-check producer (prompt/harness) and the pre_claim_intake consumer caused the verdict=bool failure that idled the entire fizeau queue (Fizeau 2026-05-18). One side emitted verdict as bool, the other parsed it as string. Without a single source of truth referenced by both sides and a CI gate, the same class of silent drift recurs.

ROOT CAUSE
Producer and consumer historically encoded the readiness_checks contract independently; there was no shared schema enforced across both. The consumer decoder lives in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go and the verdict field is decoded at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:77-125; nothing pins the producer to the same contract.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix B)
Define the readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared location at cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json (readiness_checks as a JSON array; each item has reason, verdict as oneOf boolean|string|null, evidence, checkable_before_attempt). Reference it from the consumer via a constant readinessChecksSchemaPath at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21 and from the producer prompt. Add a test that validates representative payloads (verdict bool, string, and null) against the schema and fails on drift between the code contract and the schema file.

DEPENDS ON
The pre-claim intake decoder child (verdict coercion to a canonical readinessVerdict type) must land first, because the schema's canonical verdict forms are defined there.

NON-SCOPE
- Decoder coercion implementation (sibling decoder child; this is its dependency).
- Warn-fingerprint escalation (sibling child).
- claim-rate operator signal (sibling child).
- README documentation (sibling child).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads. Adjacent silent-failure bugs: ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf.

title: Lock readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared location with a CI drift gate

acceptance:
1. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonDryRunRoutesToImporter covers --to-axon --dry-run --json calling Migrator.MigrateToAxon with DryRun true and without requiring --apply.
2. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonDryRunRoutesToImporter asserts JSON output includes bead, event, attachment, dry-run, and limit fields from the returned stats.
3. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonApplyRoutesToImporter covers --to-axon --apply --limit 7 --json passing Limit 7 and CopyAttachments true into the migrator.
4. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonApplyRoutesToImporter asserts human or JSON output exposes bead, event, attachment, dry-run, and limit values from the returned stats.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonDryRunRoutesToImporter|TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonApplyRoutesToImporter' passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-53df5e2f requires ddx bead migrate --to-axon to call the Axon importer with correct dry-run, apply, limit, attachment-copy, and JSON stats options. This routing should be verified independently from help text, validation, verify mode, and storage fidelity.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The CLI reads migration flags at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:44 and routes --to-axon into Migrator.MigrateToAxon at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:55. The relevant flags are registered at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:202, and the stats shape returned by the importer is defined at cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:609. Without explicit dry-run and apply routing tests, operators can run --to-axon --dry-run or --to-axon --apply --limit N --json and receive output from a command that silently passed incorrect Migrator options.

PROPOSED FIX
Update cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go and cmd tests so --to-axon --dry-run --json calls Migrator.MigrateToAxon with DryRun true and no apply requirement, and --to-axon --apply --limit 7 --json passes Limit 7 and CopyAttachments true. Ensure JSON and human output expose bead, event, attachment, dry-run, and limit values from the migrator stats for these modes.

NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder; deferred until first v1-to-v2 breaking change.
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres; separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres.
- Storage importer internals except as needed to satisfy the Migrator interface; sibling children own importer apply/fidelity behavior.
- New backend selection policy outside the existing Migrator/NewStore construction path.
- Do not claim or edit parent bead ddx-53df5e2f tracker data manually; tracker mutations must go through ddx bead commands.
- Help text, invalid flag-combination rejection, and verify mode are owned by sibling child beads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
TD-027.

DEPENDENCY TO PRESERVE WHEN FILING
Parent dependency ID ddx-3d96c6f9.

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBead_FailedDirtyAttemptSkipsSynthesizeCommit` uses a runner that writes a tracked edit and exits non-zero, then proves `SynthesizeCommit` and its commit-hook runner are never called and the result remains `task_failed`.
2. `TestExecuteBead_FailedDirtyAttemptPreservesRescuePatch` proves the failed dirty attempt records a durable bundle-relative rescue patch containing the implementation edit after its isolated worktree is removed.
3. `TestExecuteBead_SuccessfulDirtyAttemptStillSynthesizesCommit` proves a zero-exit dirty attempt without a no-changes rationale still receives the normal synthesized implementation commit.
4. `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized` remains green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestExecuteBead_FailedDirtyAttemptSkipsSynthesizeCommit|TestExecuteBead_FailedDirtyAttemptPreservesRescuePatch|TestExecuteBead_SuccessfulDirtyAttemptStillSynthesizesCommit|TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized)$" -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When an implementation provider exits non-zero or otherwise fails after leaving a dirty worktree, execute-bead still synthesizes an implementation commit. The synthetic `git commit` runs the full repository pre-commit hook even though DDx already knows the attempt failed. Live attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4` first failed during the agent-owned commit hook, then DDx launched a second `chore: execute-bead iteration` commit and repeated the same multi-minute race hook against unsafe partial code. This wastes CPU, delays lease release, and risks turning a failed partial patch into commit-shaped evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1314-1377` gates synthesis only on unchanged HEAD plus a dirty worktree. It records `ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed` in the preliminary result when `exitCode != 0`, but proceeds to `SynthesizeCommit` anyway.
- `cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:148-179` implements `SynthesizeCommit` with ordinary `git commit`, so repository hooks run for this already-failed attempt. The synthesis error is not used to avoid the expensive call because failure classification happens later.

PROPOSED FIX
Classify a non-zero provider exit or provider execution error before dirty-worktree synthesis. For failed attempts, do not call `SynthesizeCommit` or any commit hook. Preserve the dirty implementation diff as a durable bundle-relative rescue patch, record its path in the result, and continue normal `task_failed` cleanup and retry handling. Keep synthesis unchanged for successful zero-exit dirty attempts that provide no no-changes rationale.

NON-SCOPE
Do not bypass hooks for successful implementation commits, automatically land or commit failed partial work, weaken successful-attempt verification, change retry limits, or fold the valid-rationale behavior owned by `ddx-232aa2c0` into this bead.

PARENT
None; root execute-bead failure-path regression observed during the Niflheim queue drain.

DEPS
None. Related to `ddx-232aa2c0` for rationale-before-synthesis ordering and `ddx-8a78ee90` for duplicate successful-path hook execution.

acceptance:
1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the plugin dir present under BOTH `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>` and `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>`, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer=="project" and the project path.
2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the project copy absent and only the global copy present, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer=="global" and the path returned by `GlobalPluginDir(name)`.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Runtime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `"baked-in"` with an empty path only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>`.
- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `"project"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).
- Guarantee it returns layer `"global"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.
- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name=="ddx"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.
- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.

description:
# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:axon, area:migration, kind:feature, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore must exist), MIG bead (Migrator interface must exist).
