acceptance:
1. Add TestWorkStatus_CorruptGitWorkerNotReportedHealthy proving a server-managed worker stopped for project_git_corrupt is visible as operator attention or failed, not plain running with zero activity.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestTrackerSync_PreClaimCorruptGitClassifiesOperatorAttention|TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimCorruptGitStopsServerManagedWorker|TestWorkStatus_CorruptGitWorkerNotReportedHealthy" passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-99a4284e is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
  A server-managed worker can remain listed as running while doing no work when the project Git repository is corrupt. On 2026-07-07, /home/erik/Projects/axon had many zero-byte objects and git status failed with fatal: bad object HEAD. The live worker worker-20260707T040945-73a3 continued to appear as running in ddx work status --all-projects --json, with last_activity_at at the zero time and no active bead. Its log repeatedly printed tracker sync (pre_claim): fetch_failed and fatal: bad object refs/heads/master, then continued with local state.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go:27-29 treats pre-claim fetch/merge failure as a warning by calling emitTrackerSyncAttention with reason tracker_sync_merge_failed, but the loop continues. cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go:58 emits fetch_failed warnings for git fetch failures. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2301-2304 logs repeated pre-claim idle manual-intervention messages, but the server-managed worker remains state=running and ddx work status does not surface the project_git_corrupt/preclaim_sync_failed condition as operator attention.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go:24-60: classify unrecoverable Git repository corruption during pre_claim sync separately from transient fetch errors. Invalid HEAD, bad object, invalid sha1 pointer, and did not send all necessary objects should produce an operator-attention stop or structured preclaim blocker after a short repeat threshold.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2301-2304: convert repeated identical pre-claim tracker-sync corruption into a durable loop.operator_attention event with project root, reason, and the Git diagnostic.
  - cli/internal/server/workers.go or worker status adapter: ensure a server-managed worker that stops for project_git_corrupt is no longer reported as a healthy running worker.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync_test.go and execute loop tests: add coverage for corrupt Git diagnostics.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not attempt to repair .git/objects automatically; repo repair/reclone is an operator action.
  - Do not change ordinary transient network fetch failures into terminal stops.
  - Do not change provider/model routing.

PARENT / DEPS
  Root operational bug. No deps; related to ddx-8b5a0bac for status visibility.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Add TestWorkStatus_CorruptGitWorkerNotReportedHealthy proving a server-managed worker stopped for project_git_corrupt is visible as operator attention or failed, not plain running with zero activity.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestTrackerSync_PreClaimCorruptGitClassifiesOperatorAttention|TestExecuteLoop_PreClaimCorruptGitStopsServerManagedWorker|TestWorkStatus_CorruptGitWorkerNotReportedHealthy" passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance: 1. Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f3c3b38a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Decomposition can still produce child beads whose deps include the parent when agent-facing instructions or structured decomposition payloads request `child -> parent` edges. That recreates the deadlock even if the direct `dep add` path is fixed only later or if another writer bypasses the CLI.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: The execute-bead prompt snapshots instruct agents to run `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` at `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt:18` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt:16`. The preclaim readiness payload model includes suggested child `Parent` and `Deps` fields at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:259`, but the decomposition application path at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4678` only creates children and wires parent deps, with no golden assertion that children exclude the parent from deps.

PROPOSED FIX: Update live prompt construction and checked-in prompt snapshots so decomposition instructions create children with `parent=<parent-id>` and wire only `parent -> child` dependencies. Add validation in the programmatic decomposition path that rejects or strips any suggested child dep equal to the parent or an ancestor before tracker mutation. Add a golden e2e test that decomposes a bead into N children and asserts every child has `Parent == parent.ID` and no dependency target equal to the parent.

NON-SCOPE: Do not implement the global `DepAdd` invariant here except where needed by decomposition writer tests. Do not add doctor repair. Do not change post-attempt remediation or operator-attention behavior. Do not relax AC-map completeness rules.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:65` requires child beads to link back via `parent`; `docs/triage/decomposition.md:72` requires dependency edges in the parent-to-child direction; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:193` requires executable child beads with dependency edges.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Stop decomposition writers from creating parent back-edges: part 1

acceptance:
1. TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTierAndAgentLinks asserts `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. TestInstallProject_InTreeMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>.
3. TestInstallProject_ConventionMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx install` targets the project path unconditionally and has no `--global` surface. Operators who want one Claude Code install per machine must repeat the install per project, and project installs do not correctly distinguish in-tree vs convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and writes agent-tier links only for the project. There is no `--global` flag and no branch for convention-mode project paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx install`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx plugin show`. When `--global` is set: resolve target via the GlobalPluginDir resolver from the dependency bead (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/), write the plugin content there, and create symlinks (or copies where the OS requires) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read — ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name> (and ~/.codex/skills/<name> where the agent registry defines it). Skill discovery from a fresh shell must find the skill via the agent-tier path.
2. Without `--global`: write to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In in-tree mode the links point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>; in convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) they point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>.

NON-SCOPE
- The runtime lookup precedence itself (owned by the registry dependency bead).
- `ddx init` / `ddx update` changes (sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + precedence) — install consumes GlobalPluginDir.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-777f4a09 ddx-validate pre-commit hook: skill validation must still scope to staged skill files regardless of install location — do not regress that scoping.
title: install: add --global flag and honor convention vs in-tree project install paths

acceptance: tests/helix-loop-integration.sh runs with exit code 0 on ci.sh and covers the seven enumerated DDx --json response shapes above. Adding a row to CONTRACT-001-audit.md with resolution='code-link:<path>' triggers a harness test linking to that path; harness fails if the link is broken or the linked behavior is absent. Unknown-field tolerance and missing-required-field strictness are each asserted by at least one fixture with specific stderr/exit-code expectations documented in the test.
description:
Create tests/helix-loop-integration.sh (or equivalent under tests/) that drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx emitting scripted execute-loop --json output. Coverage requirements:
(a) no_ready_work: helix run exits cleanly with no cycle counted
(b) single success: one results[] entry with status=success, HELIX applies post-cycle to that bead
(c) mixed success/failure in one drain
(d) retry-after cooldown: results[] entry with retry_after set; HELIX does not unclaim or blame
(e) preserve_ref present: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land
(f) tolerant parsing: unknown top-level or per-result fields MUST be ignored without error
(g) strict required fields: missing bead_id or status MUST cause a clear structured error, not a silent pass

CI exercise of the contract matrix produced by the audit bead: each DDx-owned item with a code-link resolution has a harness test asserting the linked behavior; each new-DDx-bead resolution is tracked as skipped-pending; each HELIX-backport resolution has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.

---
Mirrored from helix-3fdba193 on 2026-05-20 per cleanup decision: HELIX repo PRD non-goals exclude tracker/queue/execution-engine implementation. Original description below is from the pre-collapse helix-cli era and references paths that no longer exist (scripts/helix, internal/helix/, tests/helix-cli.sh) or pre-collapse modules. DDx maintainers should reinterpret against current DDx surface before acting on it.
title: helix-ddx integration harness with contract-matrix exercise and tolerant-parse tests

acceptance:
1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd/init_test.go or init_functional_test.go) asserts `ddx init --global` makes ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.
2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd/init_test.go) asserts plain `ddx init` creates `<project>/.ddx/` and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology (cli/cmd/doctor_plugins_test.go) asserts `ddx doctor` output contains a "Global Install (...) — ok|missing" line and a "Project Install (...) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global" line, and that the project line reads `lazy-resolves-to-global` when only the global install is present.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

First-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `<project>/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).
- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (<globalPath>) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (<projectPath>) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.
2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, land/reconcile status, and any review status available for the land coordination scenarios.
2. Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, land/reconcile status, and any review status available for the land coordination scenarios.
- Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
  The DDx skill tree is still managed through parallel bootstrap copies instead of a single package-owned source. The governing plan at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:16-23 requires the default `ddx` plugin package to own the DDx skill, requires `ddx init` and `ddx plugin install ddx` to share one package installer path, and keeps home-directory skill installs out of scope.

ROOT CAUSE
  Current state is split across several bootstrap paths: cli/cmd/init.go:256-287 copies embedded skills with `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, registers `.ddx/skills/ddx` separately, then installs the default plugin through `registry.InstallPackage`; cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 handles package skills by calling `skills.Install` directly instead of applying manifest skill mappings; cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 has a separate local symlink implementation; cli/Makefile:65-84 keeps copied skill mirrors in sync. The plan requires these paths to collapse into package install semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
  Break the migration into child beads that first make package install semantics correct, then route init through the embedded package installer, then move the skill source into the default package layout, and finally update docs/specs/help.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not execute the child implementation work in this epic bead.
  - Do not restore global or home-directory skill installs.
  - Do not change public plugin CLI names beyond the separate CLI rationalization work.

PARENT + DEPS
  Root epic. No deps.

acceptance: `ddx install --force --local <path>` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.
description:
## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

`ddx install <plugin> --force --local <repo>` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.

## Reproduction

1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)
2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)
3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`
4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`

Repro on HELIX:
```
ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix
# Error: validating package structure:
#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...
```

Workaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.

## Expected behavior

`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v <path>` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.

## Related

- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.
- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.
title: ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` still reports readiness decode failures and then fail-opens into execution. Two observed shapes are now known:
- `waivers_applied` may be emitted as a flat string list, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied of type agent.preClaimReadinessWaiver`.
- `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` may be emitted as a single string, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.suggested_child_beads.acceptance of type []string`.

Both failures make the readiness model unavailable even though the model produced a semantically usable classification. This keeps readiness from contributing route/difficulty signals and produces noisy fail-open logs during normal `ddx work --watch` operation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:60-72` decodes the readiness classification payload directly into strict Go structs. `preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.SuggestedChildren` is `[]preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild`, while `preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.Acceptance` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:148-152` requires `[]string`. `WaiversApplied` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:71` requires structured objects. `decodeReadinessClassificationPayloadWithMode` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:584-588` fails the whole readiness classification on any JSON shape mismatch. The prompt text at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:424-431` asks for `suggested_child_beads` and `waivers_applied` but does not give enough defensive handling for common model scalar/list variants.

PROPOSED FIX
Add small JSON normalization types for readiness model-only fields so decoding accepts the observed variants without creating new execution paths. Specifically: normalize `waivers_applied` from omitted/null/empty array/flat string list/object list, and normalize `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` from either a string or a string array. Keep the internal readiness result behavior unchanged unless existing code already consumes these fields. Tighten the readiness prompt so it documents the preferred shapes and explicitly says scalar fallbacks are tolerated only by the decoder.

NON-SCOPE
Do not persist readiness waiver or child-bead suggestion data to bead metadata. Do not add numeric difficulty/score metadata. Do not implement automatic decomposition behavior here. Do not change the easy/medium/hard routing contract.
title: readiness: accept model-output waiver and child-acceptance variants

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose), ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle), ddx-04e89823 (RC-2 cascade), ddx-f500c75b (RC-3 walk-up), ddx-7b88e3a7 (RC-6 auto-decompose handler).

acceptance:
1. TestLockMetrics_EmitsAcquireAndReleaseEvents asserts that acquiring and releasing the wrapped lock produces two JSON events with required fields (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation).
2. TestLockMetrics_DurationMatchesElapsed asserts duration_ms equals released_at minus acquired_at within 5ms tolerance.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has no visibility into how long workers hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock. Operators diagnosing tracker-lock-timeout errors must infer from filesystem mtimes and ps output instead of structured logs. Lock-lifetime cap enforcement (separate child) depends on having this signal first.

ROOT CAUSE
Lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/run.go, and cli/internal/bead/store.go log nothing structured around acquire/release. Executing agent must locate the wrappers introduced in [[scope-locks-to-mutation-window]] (or the existing call sites if that child has not landed yet) and wrap them in an instrumented helper.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small instrumented-lock helper in cli/internal/lockmetrics/ (or extend an existing util package) that wraps acquire/release and emits one JSON-line event per acquire and release containing: lock_name, acquired_at (RFC3339Nano), released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation (e.g. 'execute-bead.stage-commit', 'bead.update'). Events go through the existing ddx logs/event stream (consistent with .ddx/attachments/ event conventions).

NON-SCOPE
- Enforcing a hold-time cap (separate child)
- Refactoring lock acquisition scope itself (separate child)
- Metrics for non-git locks (worktree locks, etc.)
- Dashboards or aggregation tooling.
title: emit structured lock acquire/release events with duration for index.lock and tracker.lock

2. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd/update_test.go) asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and does not modify the project install or project state.

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Operators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).
- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.
- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name> --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).
2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.
3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.
4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works".
5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-013-multi-agent-coordination.md states one-claim-wins semantics and losing concurrent attempts do not create terminal evidence.
2. docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md no longer contradict the no-history-rewrite merge policy for execute-bead branches.
3. Any "7 reliability principles" reference in repo guidance/docs is updated to P1-P10 or otherwise made accurate.
4. `rg -n "last writer wins|7 reliability principles|rebase.*fast-forward" docs AGENTS.md` has no stale contradictory hit, or each remaining hit is explicitly scoped as historical/future-only.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The governing docs still disagree on parts of multi-worker execution. SD-013 describes concurrent claims as serialized with "last writer wins", while TP-021 requires one claim to win and losing concurrent `ddx try` attempts to exit without terminal-looking evidence. API-001 and FEAT-012 still describe rebase+fast-forward landing, while repository merge policy forbids history rewriting on execute-bead branches.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-013-multi-agent-coordination.md:49 uses stale claim-race language.
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:223-239 describes rebase+fast-forward and push behavior that conflicts with current no-history-rewrite policy.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:224-239 and :410-416 need to align with the bounded mutation-window contract introduced by TP-021.

PROPOSED FIX
- Amend SD-013 to say concurrent claim attempts are atomic and at most one runner owns the bead.
- Amend API-001/FEAT-012 landing language to distinguish local merge/preserve semantics from any future multi-machine coordination, and remove stale rebase-as-normal wording where it conflicts with no-history-rewrite policy.
- Update reliability-principle references that still say there are 7 principles when the doc now defines P1-P10.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change code behavior in this docs-only bead.
- Do not redesign multi-machine coordination; file separate beads if SD-020 needs implementation changes.
title: docs: align multi-worker claim and landing contracts

acceptance:
1. A diagnostic or health check marks an unreachable Vidar endpoint unavailable before DDx/Fizeau selects it for work.
2. A provider_connectivity failure records actionable route facts including provider, model, endpoint, and timeout class.
3. A retry/fallback path avoids repeatedly selecting the same unreachable Vidar route for the same bead attempt.
4. Focused tests for provider connectivity failure handling pass.
5. Repository pre-commit or equivalent local gate passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A Niflheim ddx work attempt for bead niflheim-27416d39 reached execution and then failed after DDx/Fizeau routed the continuation to provider vidar model Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit. The route failed with provider_connectivity: openai POST http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions dial tcp 100.108.162.80:1235 i/o timeout.

IMPACT
The project queue remains worker-ready, but attempts can fail after useful work if the default/cheap route selects an unreachable Vidar endpoint. This is infrastructure, not a Niflheim acceptance failure.

EVIDENCE
Niflheim attempt id: 20260518T190003-d738a271. Worker output ended with: provider_connectivity, provider=vidar, model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions, i/o timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
Make provider health or route selection avoid unreachable Vidar endpoints, or mark Vidar unhealthy before dispatch so DDx falls back to a reachable provider. Add regression coverage for provider-connectivity failures so a failed route is recorded and retried/falls back without consuming the useful attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Niflheim beads. Do not mask genuine task failures as provider failures.
notes: 2026-05-21 repro: Niflheim bead niflheim-439a49ae failed attempt 20260521T080548-f4200852 after auto-routing selected `policy=cheap` / `provider=vidar` / `model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit` for a medium validation task. Route failed with `connect: connection refused` to `http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions`. Bead event recorded `work-failed-routes` for vidar, but the once-worker exited after one failed route instead of retrying/falling back to a reachable route. This confirms the bug remains live and should block further Niflheim burn-down until route failure exclusion/fallback is fixed or Vidar health is marked unavailable before selection.
title: Avoid unreachable Vidar route after provider_connectivity failure

acceptance:
1. `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` exists, declares package `lifecycle`, and contains the concrete hub plus per-project watcher internals formerly in `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:23-224`.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer defines `StoreFactory`, `WatcherHub`, `NewWatcherHub`, `projectWatcher`, or `genLifecycleEventID`; the public `bead` package still defines `LifecycleEvent` and the `LifecycleSubscriber` interface.
3. `TestWatcherHub_SubscribeLifecycle` and `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` pass from `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/...` against the relocated implementation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:23-100` defines and exports `StoreFactory`, `WatcherHub`, and `NewWatcherHub` from the public `bead` package, while `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:109-224` keeps the polling `projectWatcher` internals in the same public package. TD-027 §21 requires the concrete lifecycle polling hub to sit behind an internal boundary, but callers can still import the public bead package and construct the hub directly.

ROOT CAUSE
The concrete watcher implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:23-224` instead of `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`, so the concrete type and constructor are part of the public `bead` package API. The public interface boundary already exists at `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:144-147`, but the implementation is not separated from it.

PROPOSED FIX
Move the concrete watcher implementation from `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` with package name `lifecycle`. Keep `LifecycleEvent` in the public `bead` package, update the internal lifecycle implementation to reference `bead.LifecycleEvent`, and keep `LifecycleSubscriber` as the public interface. Move or rewrite watcher tests under `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` so behavior is proven against the relocated implementation.

Parent: ddx-74452926. Governing artifact: TD-027 §21. No additional parent dependency IDs were supplied.

NON-SCOPE
- Storage backend relocation.
- Caller-field narrowing.
- Boundary import test.
- Lint analyzer cleanup.

1. TestCmdTestsDoNotSpawnRealProviderCLIs wraps representative cmd tests and proves no descendant process named real codex, claude, or gemini remains after the test.
2. TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified passes with PATH containing fake harness binaries and without reading the user's real Fizeau discovery cache.
3. TestWorkDoesNotSpawnProviderAfterUnderSpecifiedRouting passes without launching a real provider CLI.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified|TestWorkDoesNotSpawnProviderAfterUnderSpecifiedRouting|TestCmdTestsDoNotSpawnRealProviderCLIs'` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/...` no longer spawns real codex/claude/gemini processes or loops on `/home/*/.cache/fizeau/discovery/*.refreshing`.

description:
PROBLEM
During ddx-0be85773 dogfood on 2026-06-13, `go test ./cmd/...` and the pre-commit race package loop spawned real provider CLI children (`codex --no-alt-screen`, `claude`, and `gemini`) from cmd tests. The test log showed repeated reads of `/home/erik/.cache/fizeau/discovery/*-subscription.refreshing` and real auth/provider discovery paths. Automated DDx tests must be hermetic; they cannot depend on or leave behind live provider processes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cmd tests run through real routing/discovery code paths without a fake Fizeau/provider discovery layer. Observed tests included TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified, TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim, and TestWorkDoesNotSpawnProviderAfterUnderSpecifiedRouting. cli/cmd/testutils_test.go and cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_test.go set some hermetic env, but they do not block provider CLI discovery or force fake harness binaries for all cmd tests.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cmd-test-wide provider isolation guard that removes real provider CLI directories from PATH or points codex/claude/gemini discovery at harmless fake binaries.
- Force Fizeau discovery/cache paths into each test temp root and disable subscription refreshes/network calls in tests.
- Add a leak assertion around cmd tests that fails if a test leaves a descendant provider process alive.
- Update zero-config and work resource tests to use fake harness/service routes instead of real provider discovery.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production provider discovery. Do not skip the affected tests. Do not kill unrelated interactive provider sessions as part of test cleanup.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.

description:
PROBLEM
The docker-clone attempt backend is unusable on a remote, rootless, or de-nested Docker daemon (e.g. OrbStack on this machine, docker info Name=orbstack). Every attempt fast-fails in 17-39ms before any agent work with: docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist: <host path>. Outcome is classified execution_failed / transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:612 dockerRunArgs() constructs the container with five classes of HOST bind-mount (--mount type=bind,src=<hostpath>):
  - :622 src=ws.WorkDir            dst=/work               (cloned repo)
  - :623 src=exe                   dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx  (ddx binary, os.Executable())
  - :636 src=ws.DockerRun          dst=/ddx-runtime        (HOME/cache/tmp + injected creds)
  - :641-642 gocache, work-tmp                              (Go build/test cache)
  - :644-649 dockerToolMounts() -> claude/codex/gemini      (agent CLIs via exec.LookPath)
A de-nested/remote daemon's filesystem namespace does not include this machine's /home/erik/... , so every src path 'does not exist' from the daemon's view. Confirmed: 4 attempts on ddx-b5816493 failed this way; failing paths included /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx and /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145. A short -v probe 'succeeded' only because docker silently auto-created an empty dir (the classic -v footgun), proving the source was not visible.

PROPOSED FIX (decomposed into child tasks)
Adopt the standard create -> cp -> start -> cp-out -> rm pattern used for remote/rootless daemons, plus bake static tooling into the image and use daemon-managed named volumes for caches. Only the daemon's own storage (image layers + named volumes) is reliably reachable; everything dynamic is copied in/out.
  1. Bake agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image; drop their bind-mounts.
  2. Keystone: move workspace + ddx binary in via docker create/cp instead of bind.
  3. Convert Go caches + runtime dir to named volumes.
  4. Inject credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind.

NON-SCOPE
- The worktree and local-clone backends (unaffected; worktree is the working default).
- Routing/provider selection.

NOTE: filed from an operator session after switching the live queue drain to --attempt-backend worktree to work around this. Child tasks carry their own file:line scope and AC.
title: docker-clone attempt backend: eliminate host bind-mounts for remote/de-nested daemon compatibility

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock.*Section|TestTrackerLock.*Sink' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test asserts withMainGitLock with section="durable_audit" yields a TrackerLockSample with Section=="durable_audit".
3. A unit test asserts the worker-installed sink emits both a loop.tracker_lock event and a plain log line per acquire/release with all four fields (section, wait, hold, retries).
4. A grep `grep -n 'withMainGitLock\|withTrackerLock' cli/internal/agent/*.go cli/cmd/*.go` shows every call site passes a non-empty section string (no positional-only call remains).
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:151-156` defines
`TrackerLockSample{LockDir, Wait, Hold, Retries}` and a global
`TrackerLockMetricsSink func(TrackerLockSample)` (line 161). The hook fires
on every acquire+release cycle at line 232. **No production code wires the
sink to anything operator-visible** — the sample is computed and dropped on
the floor. Similarly, `lockmetrics.Instrument("tracker.lock",
"tracker.commit", fn)` at line 231 always passes the literal label
"tracker.commit" regardless of which of the five call sites
(`durable_audit.go:50`, `execute_bead.go:694`, `execute_bead.go:1493`,
`execute_bead_land.go:839`, `execute_bead_land.go:857`,
`execute_bead_land.go:1210`) is holding the lock — so the metric cannot
distinguish a slow durable-audit commit from a slow Land or a slow
pre-dispatch worktree creation.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28: a niflheim drain with two concurrent `ddx work`
processes saw worker B (pid 681801) time out on the tracker lock with
`tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 424293)` after
the 30s `MaxElapsed` budget defined in `DefaultLockRetryPolicy`. We have
no log of how long A held the lock or which call site was holding —
diagnosis required reading the source to enumerate the call sites and
infer plausible candidates (pre-dispatch worktree creation, Land merge).
The metric to answer "was that a 31s hold or a 2.9s hold blocked by a
30s queue" is computed but never logged.

ROOT CAUSE: Two coupled gaps —
  1. `withTrackerLock` / `withMainGitLock` (line 145 and 128) take no
     section/caller identifier, so the sink receives only `LockDir`
     plus timings. There is no way to attribute a long hold to a
     specific call site from the metric alone.
  2. No worker startup code installs a `TrackerLockMetricsSink`. The
     hook is a dead extension point in production.

PROPOSED FIX:

A) Plumb a section identifier through the lock API:
   1. Extend `withMainGitLock` and `withTrackerLock` to accept a
      `section string` parameter (e.g. "durable_audit", "pre_dispatch",
      "land", "preserve_dirt", "ancestry_local").
   2. Extend `TrackerLockSample` with `Section string`.
   3. Pass `section` to `lockmetrics.Instrument` (replacing the hardcoded
      "tracker.commit") so cap- and metrics-level views also distinguish
      sites.
   4. Update all six call sites listed above to pass a stable section
      string.

B) Wire the sink to the worker log in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`:
   1. In `execute_bead_loop.go` near where the runtime / log is
      constructed (search for `runtime.Log` usage near `emit(...)`
      callsites), install a sink that, on every release, emits a
      structured line. Use the existing `emit` event stream when
      available (`emit("loop.tracker_lock", map[string]any{...})`) so
      the data shows up in the same channel as `loop.idle`,
      `loop.operator_attention`, etc. Always also write a plain-text
      log line for terminal monitors:
        `tracker_lock section=<x> wait=<dur> hold=<dur> retries=<n>`
   2. Make the install per-worker and idempotent (the sink is a package
      global today; if multiple workers run in the same process the
      sink must serialize or use sync/atomic). For per-process worker
      isolation as it stands, idempotent install at worker start is
      sufficient.

C) Surface lock samples in `ddx work status`:
   - Out of scope for this bead. Track separately if useful — the log
     wiring is the immediate need.

NON-SCOPE:
- Narrowing the lock-held region itself — tracked separately in the
  sibling bead about pre-dispatch and Land lock-surface narrowing.
- Changing the retry policy (30s MaxElapsed, 200 MaxRetries, 1s ceiling).
- Adding a new metric backend or storage; emit through existing
  `loop.*` event stream and `runtime.Log`.
- Adding telemetry to non-execute-bead lock sites (e.g.
  `cmd/bead.go:167`).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (add Section field, plumb param)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (pass "durable_audit" section)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (pass "preserve_dirt" at 694,
  "pre_dispatch" at 1493)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (pass "ancestry_fetch" at 839,
  "ancestry_local" at 857, "land" at 1210)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (install sink at worker start)
- cli/cmd/bead.go (pass a section identifier at line 167 —
  "cmd_bead_write" or similar)
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (extend tests for Section
  field and sink behavior)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package
- cli/internal/lockmetrics — already accepts the label parameter; only
  change is in callers
- ddx work status command (separate bead if surfaced there later)
title: execute-bead: wire TrackerLockMetricsSink to worker log with section identifier (current sink is unread, lock hold/wait times invisible)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. TestDDXScratchTempDirUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot proves a DDx scratch temp helper creates ddx-home/provider-shim style directories under the configured execution/runtime root when configured.
2. TestDDXScratchTempDirFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutConfiguredRoot proves fallback behavior remains when no project/config root is available.
3. TestProviderShimUsesDDXScratchRoot proves EnsureProviderShimOnPATH can create ddx-provider-shim-* under the configured scratch root rather than raw /tmp.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestDDXScratchTempDirUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot|TestDDXScratchTempDirFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutConfiguredRoot|TestProviderShimUsesDDXScratchRoot" passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx-owned temporary homes, provider shims, and helper scratch are created directly under raw os.TempDir(), so /tmp remains the choke point even when DDx has a configured execution/runtime temp root with available space. The 2026-07-08 incident had /tmp at 100% inode use while the configured execution worktree root under /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt still had capacity.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:91 creates provider shims with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-provider-shim-").
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29 and runner_test.go:56 create ddx-home-* with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-"); equivalent command tests create the same prefix in cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:80, cli/cmd/try_test.go:823, and cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:85.
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:116 creates ddx-fixture-bin-* with os.MkdirTemp("", ...).
These producers bypass config.ExecutionScratchRoot / config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot and strand inode-heavy scratch under /tmp.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared helper for DDx-owned scratch temp dirs that prefers config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot) or the configured execution/runtime root when a project root is available, and falls back to os.TempDir() only when no configured root exists. Migrate provider shims and DDx-owned temp homes/fixture bins to the helper where production/runtime context is available. Keep tests hermetic by setting the helper root explicitly in tests.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go
- cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go or a new scratch-temp helper package
- relevant tests in cli/internal/agent and cli/cmd

NON-SCOPE
Do not move claim-liveness in this bead; ddx-3986b89a covers claim heartbeat root selection. Do not change git worktree placement. Do not store long-lived user config in execution scratch. Do not require a project root for all tests; fallback behavior must remain.

status=blocked
reason=Host /tmp is at ENOSPC (no space left on device) in this execution environment, which breaks the Bash tool itself — even its own working-directory bookkeeping mkdir under /tmp/claude-501/... fails before any shell command can run. Verified independently in the main session and in a freshly spawned subagent (both hit the identical ENOSPC on the same path), so this is a host/container-level condition, not scoped to one conversation. `go build`, `go test`, `git`, and `lefthook` all require the Bash tool, so none of them can be invoked. This bead's own AC requires running `cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` before a commit — neither is executable right now. Writable alternatives exist (Write tool succeeds against the repo tree and against /dev/shm), but /tmp itself rejects even a 6-byte file write, confirming this is genuine disk/inode exhaustion on /tmp rather than a sandbox path quirk. This is an instance of the exact class of problem ddx-6a2ce3bd is meant to fix (DDx scratch under /tmp choking out), but the fix cannot be authored, built, or tested from inside an environment where /tmp is already full — that requires either an operator clearing /tmp on this host, or dispatching this bead in a worker whose /tmp has headroom. No source changes were made; retry once /tmp has free space/inodes.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

acceptance:
1. scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh or TestIntegration_ServerManagedWorker_NoProcessLeaks builds/runs an isolated Docker scenario from current source.
2. The scenario verifies explicit stop leaves no managed ddx work, fake claude, fake codex, shell, or sleep descendants running.
3. The scenario verifies watchdog reap and server shutdown leave no managed worker or fake agent descendants running.
4. The scenario calls stop twice and proves the second call is a no-op with no duplicate bead event and no unrelated process kill.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/integration/... ./internal/server/... -run 'ServerManagedWorker|NoProcessLeaks' passes when Docker is available and skips with a clear message when Docker is unavailable.
6. scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh passes on a Docker-enabled host if implemented as a script.
7. make install is run after the code change lands.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The feature is only credible if an automated isolated test proves server-managed worker cleanup leaves no ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind. Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient for process-tree cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214 Stop and :1423 reapWorker are process-cleanup entry points, but existing tests do not run a full server plus managed worker plus fake agent child tree in an isolated environment and assert post-cleanup process state. docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md Testing Strategy requires a Docker-enabled no-leak scenario.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a Docker-backed integration test or scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh. Build DDx from current source in the container, create a fixture repo with a deterministic script/fake harness, put fake claude and codex binaries first on PATH, start ddx server, set desired_count=1 through server API or ddx worker CLI, wait for fake children, then exercise explicit stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown. Assert no managed ddx work, fake claude/codex, or descendant shell/sleep process remains after each cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require real Claude/Codex credentials. Do not rely on host tmux or host process state. Do not make the test mandatory on developer machines without Docker; skip clearly there, but provide a CI/release command that runs when Docker is available.
title: integration: prove server-managed workers leave no process leaks in Docker

acceptance: 1. cli/internal/bead/merge.go strips dependency edges whose target is absent from the merged set (active union archive) after union; covered by a new merge test that feeds a bead with a dangling dep and asserts the merged output has it removed. 2. cli/internal/bead/import.go strips (not just warns about) dangling deps; test asserts the imported bead has no dangling dep and the reported count is accurate. 3. detectPrefix never returns a prefix that fails ValidateID (no leading '.', no '/'); test feeds a worktree-style workingDir and asserts the prefix falls back to DefaultPrefix. 4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... green. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: ddx accumulated ~132 phantom dependency edges (dependencies[].depends_on_id pointing to bead IDs absent from beads.jsonl AND beads-archive.jsonl), which permanently blocked ~29 beads from readiness and stalled the live queue (readyFiltered/classifyLifecycleQueue treat a missing-target dep as unsatisfied forever). The REPAIR + one write-path guard already landed (merge to main: ddx bead doctor gained a 'dangling_dep' finding that prunes such edges archive-aware; Store.updateBead now rejects NEWLY-introduced dangling edges). This bead closes the REMAINING source paths so phantoms stop appearing.

ROOT CAUSES still open (from investigation):
1. cli/internal/bead/merge.go MergeTrackerConflictJSONL set-unions the dependencies list via mergeObjectLists(dependencyMergeKey,...) with NO referential-integrity check (merge.go:~162). A parent's dep edges merge in while child records on a dropped branch do not -> dangling. PRIMARY accumulation path for ddx-internal merges (ddx bead import / sync).
2. cli/internal/bead/import.go:~281 detects dangling deps but only WARNS ('dangling dep (skipped)') and appends the bead WITH the dangling dep intact. The '(skipped)' wording is false.
3. cli/internal/bead/store.go detectPrefix (~:3070-3087) returns filepath.Base(workingDir)/filepath.Base(cwd) when 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' is unavailable; run inside an execute-bead worktree this yields a prefix like '.execute-bead-wt-...', producing malformed child IDs (one such ID is live in the tracker). ValidateID should have rejected a leading '.', so these bypass Create/GenID validation via raw WriteAll/import/merge.

PROPOSED FIX:
- merge.go: after the union, drop any dependency edge whose depends_on_id is not present in the merged bead set UNION the archive sibling (mirror doctor.go loadArchiveBeadIDs). Add a referential-integrity pass in MergeTrackerConflictJSONL (or ValidateTrackerJSONLUnique).
- import.go: actually strip dangling deps (filter them out of the bead's Dependencies) and report count; keep the warning but make it true.
- detectPrefix: reject a derived prefix that fails the ValidateID charset (or contains '.'/'/'); fall back to DefaultPrefix. Optionally validate GenID output against ValidateID before returning.
NON-SCOPE: the doctor 'dangling_dep' finding + updateBead guard are already landed; do not redo them. Do not change readiness semantics here (separate decision).

CONTEXT: the durable cross-machine convergence works today because doctor --fix bumps updated_at and the supervisor union resolver keeps newest-updated_at, so pruned records propagate. This bead is about stopping NEW phantoms at the ddx write/merge/import layer.
title: Close the remaining phantom-dep write paths: merge union, import, and worktree-prefix ID contamination

acceptance:
1. `TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsStaleAttemptDescendantProcessesBeforeClaim` (or equivalent) proves `ddx work` startup/pre-claim cleanup runs the same process pass before claiming the next bead and emits a structured cleanup observation when it reaps or preserves a process group.
2. A preservation regression proves live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, and other-project processes survive the widened startup pass.
3. Existing startup cleanup coverage, including `TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees` and `TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees`, remains green with the new process pass wired in.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'WorkStartupCleanup|AttemptDescendant|Cleanup.*Process' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` startup cleanup can already reap stale worktrees and stale worker-sidecar directories, but it does not terminate or report the underlying DDx-owned process group when a stale sidecar corresponds to an already-orphaned attempt descendant. That leaves the queue controller looking healthy while the host still burns CPU or holds ports.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:97-126` only reads run-states and then calls `scanWorktrees`, `scanWorkerDirs`, and `scanExecutionDirs`; there is no process-cleanup pass in the pre-claim path.
`cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:218-267` only reaps stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` directories after liveness checks. It does not consult process-group evidence or emit a structured observation when a stale attempt descendant is reaped or preserved.

PROPOSED FIX
- Wire the same conservative attempt-process pass used by the cleanup manager into startup housekeeping before the next bead claim.
- Emit a structured cleanup observation when startup reaps or preserves a process group so operators can see why the host was or was not cleaned.
- Keep the existing worktree, worker-dir, and execution-dir retention logic intact, including the cleanup lock/jitter behavior already in place for long-lived workers.
- Add command tests that prove startup cleanup runs the process pass before claim and preserves live run-state / worker-sidecar / registered-worktree / other-project processes.

NON-SCOPE
- `ddx cleanup` JSON/text shaping is covered by the sibling cleanup-command bead.
- The process-census/reaper logic itself lives in the sibling agent bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No change to worker modes, queue selection, or retention-policy semantics.

acceptance:
1. TestInitProject_MaterializesBuiltinAdaptersWithoutPluginPayload still passes and is extended or paired with a new TestDefaultDDxPackageDoesNotRequireProjectPayload proving plain ddx init/plugin sync leaves .ddx/plugins/ddx absent while .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx work.
2. New or updated registry/config tests prove DefaultNewConfig/ApplyDefaults no longer teach .ddx/plugins/ddx as the normal project payload path for the built-in DDx package.
3. Registry/defaultplugin tests distinguish compatibility/local-overlay install.root metadata from marketplace/cache-backed generated adapter behavior and stop asserting .ddx/plugins/ddx as the forward install target.
4. CLI help and frontend/e2e fixture docs no longer instruct users to rely on .ddx/plugins/ddx for normal marketplace installs.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/frontend/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
HELIX is now available as a marketplace plugin and demonstrates the intended npx-style install model: projects record plugin intent in .ddx/plugins.lock.yaml, payloads live in the shared XDG cache, and only generated adapters appear under .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/. The built-in DDx default package still has residual implementation and fixture drift that can teach users and tests that full payloads belong under .ddx/plugins/ddx.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27 says the forward behavior is cache-backed and npx-like and generated adapters/payloads are not forward repository state.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:623-672 still defaults library.path to .ddx/plugins/ddx in DefaultNewConfig and ApplyDefaults.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go:75-82 and cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/package.yaml:14-22 still expose install.root.target=.ddx/plugins/ddx for the built-in ddx package, while registry comments treat it as compatibility-only metadata.
- cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:60-68 asserts the legacy .ddx/plugins/ddx target, and cli/cmd/skills.go:27-32 advertises checking .ddx/plugins/ddx.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md:85-105 describes the e2e fixture as containing .ddx/plugins/ddx resources, which conflicts with generated-adapter/cache topology.

PROPOSED FIX
Finish the default DDx package topology so marketplace and built-in DDx package behavior match HELIX: config defaults and docs should point to generated adapters/cache-backed resolution rather than project-payload copies; compatibility install.root metadata should be clearly local-overlay/legacy only or removed from forward assertions; tests should prove ddx init/plugin sync do not create .ddx/plugins/ddx for normal installs and that consumers resolve built-in resources through plugin lock/cache/defaultplugin fallback.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for explicit local development overlays. Do not remove the embedded default plugin fallback needed for offline ddx init. Do not change HELIX marketplace packaging.
title: plugin: finish npx-style DDx default package topology

description:
Part of EPIC ddx-42893255. Read ADR-029 first. Depends on hub endpoints (ddx-48c55db2) + identity binding (ddx-26ae40ed).

ROOT CAUSE: ddx work/try run in a separate process from the server and cannot see federation role, node_id (s.state.Node.ID set only in EnableSpokeMode, cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go:50), or hub URL. So the worker cannot decide to acquire, nor reach the hub with correct identity/TLS.

PROPOSED: Add a worker-facing claim layer routed through the LOCAL server (self-signed cert -> newLocalServerClientTimeout skip-verify, cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go):
- GET /api/federation/self -> {federated:bool, node_id, role}. Worker queries once; non-federated => hard no-op (zero overhead single node).
- Local claim acquire/renew/release/validate endpoints that the local server services directly when it is the hub, or forwards to the hub (like handleFederationForwardMutation, federation_hub.go:340) when it is a spoke, injecting its own authenticated node_id.

AC:
1. GET /api/federation/self returns correct {federated,node_id,role} for hub, spoke, and non-federated servers; TestFederationSelf_ReportsRole passes in cli/internal/server.
2. A spoke's local claim call is forwarded to the hub with the spoke's node_id; a hub services locally; TestFederationClaimProxy_HubLocalAndSpokeForward passes.
3. Non-federated server returns federated:false and the worker path becomes a no-op (TestFederationSelf_NonFederatedNoop).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'FederationSelf|FederationClaimProxy' green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: federation: local-server claim proxy + GET /api/federation/self

description:
PROBLEM
Successful landing deletes the canonical project-root execution bundle after evidence commits were intentionally retired. This loses manifest, result, and checks evidence. Three server tests expose the loss, but two also retain obsolete assertions that evidence must appear in Git or produce a trailing audit commit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1218-1248 defines local-only evidence finalization with no evidence commit.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1382-1403 cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy deletes the project-root bundle and is still invoked at 1692-1704 after successful landing.
- cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go:399-451 requires a trailing evidence/audit commit even though no such commit now exists.
- cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:1112-1116 requires .ddx/executions evidence in Git, contradicting the local retained evidence policy.
- TestWorker_RequiredGatePass_LandsViaCoordinator fails because checks.json has been deleted from the canonical bundle.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the obsolete project-root evidence cleanup while continuing to clean only temporary finalization worktrees. Add a production landing regression proving manifest.json, result.json, and checks.json survive locally but remain untracked and EvidenceCommitSHA stays empty. Align the server fixtures with production .gitignore and update revision assertions so the implementation commit differs from the merge commit while LandedRev equals the final target tip when no later audit commit exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not reintroduce evidence commits, alter evidence retention/mirroring policy, remove candidate/implementation/landed revision fields, or weaken repository gates.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-8b22914e. Deps: none.
title: agent/server: retain local execution evidence and reconcile landing contracts

acceptance:
1. TestWorkPinnedRouteDoesNotLaunchUnrequestedProviderChild uses fake codex/gemini/claude binaries and proves a pinned `ddx work --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini` run never executes the fake gemini/claude binaries in readiness, lifecycle, review, or execute phases.
2. TestLifecycleClearRoutingPinsRequiresExplicitAutonomousRouteEvidence fails if a lifecycle AgentRunRuntime clears routing pins while the worker rcfg has explicit harness/provider/model constraints and no explicit autonomous-routing evidence hook.
3. TestPinnedWorkerProviderChildEvidence records requested_harness, requested_model, selected_harness, selected_model, pid, command, and phase for every DDx-owned provider subprocess; the test asserts a mismatch is visible before cleanup.
4. The implementation preserves DDx's provider-independence layering: DDx forwards passthrough constraints and enforces no-escape invariants, while Fizeau remains responsible for model catalog, aliasing, fallback, and concrete route choice.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkPinnedRouteDoesNotLaunchUnrequestedProviderChild|TestLifecycleClearRoutingPinsRequiresExplicitAutonomousRouteEvidence|TestPinnedWorkerProviderChildEvidence' passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A pinned DDx worker still launched a non-pinned provider child during the 2026-06-13 dogfood run. The worker command was `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 30s --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.4-mini --request-timeout 20m` for bead ddx-aef128f7. At 2026-06-13T16:10:51Z, worker pid 4105977 still owned `codex exec ... -m gpt-5.4-mini` pid 4116435, but also owned `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gemini` pid 4130588. That violates the DDx/Fizeau provider-independence contract: explicit passthrough constraints must apply to every DDx-owned lifecycle, review, and execution subprocess, and DDx must not silently widen to a different harness/provider.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:70-82 and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:208-224 preserve rcfg harness/provider/model only at the AgentRunRuntime dispatch boundary. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1456-1459 records route evidence after the provider returns, but there is no worker-level invariant or test that all DDx-owned child processes created during a pinned worker run match the requested harness/model or have explicit lifecycle routing evidence. Existing hidden lifecycle callers still use ClearRoutingPins in cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:89, cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:118, cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:132, and cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:556; any one of these can create a provider child that escapes the operator pin.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an execution-level regression that starts a pinned worker/attempt with fake codex and fake gemini/claude executables on PATH and fails if any DDx-owned child process invokes an unrequested provider binary.
- Audit every AgentRunRuntime with ClearRoutingPins=true in lifecycle/review/intake/reframe paths and make each caller either preserve explicit worker passthrough constraints or record an explicit, operator-approved autonomous route decision.
- Add worker process-tree attribution/evidence for provider child commands during lifecycle and execution phases so future failures name pid, phase, requested route, selected route, and command.
- Ensure the fix does not implement a DDx model catalog or provider fallback policy; DDx should pass constraints and enforce invariants, while Fizeau owns concrete route resolution.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for Gemini, Claude, or other harnesses. Do not kill unrelated interactive provider sessions. Do not make DDx choose replacement models. Request-timeout process cleanup is covered by sibling bead ddx-aef128f7; this bead is specifically about preventing non-pinned provider children from launching in the first place.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: same dogfood run also proved request-timeout/process-tree cleanup failure; this bead supplies the provider-escape invariant that cleanup alone cannot solve.
- ddx-34d96048 and ddx-3d80038f: prior fixes covered pinned route preflight and decomposition routing, but this dogfood run shows another provider escape remains in execution/lifecycle subprocess ownership.
title: work: prevent pinned workers from launching unrequested provider children
