description:
PROBLEM
`ddxroot.Path()` in `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:38-83` decides whether the active DDx root is in-tree or convention-mode, but the current coverage is split across feature-specific tests like `cli/cmd/install_test.go:107-182`, `cli/cmd/install_global_test.go:94-184`, `cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:13-88`, and `cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:22-80`. There is no single parameterized matrix that proves the same scenario suite behaves identically in both modes.

ROOT CAUSE
The test surface validates individual features one mode at a time instead of asserting parity across both anchors. That leaves mode drift invisible for path anchoring, worktree registry behavior, auto-commit landing, and attempt artifact placement.

PROPOSED FIX
Add `cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go` with deterministic Convention and InTree fixtures, a table-driven matrix over the nine parent scenarios, and the shared-XDG two-clone subscenario. Keep the package doc comment that states: `Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression.` Use existing command helpers so the suite exercises bead lifecycle, bead execution, plugin resolution, persona binding, worker drain, meta-prompt sync, auto-commit routing, attachments, and index-lock recovery under both modes.

NON-SCOPE
- No doc edits.
- No new user-visible behavior beyond the test harness.
- No migration tooling for existing in-tree repos; this repo remains in-tree.

Governing deps: `ddx-06cbaa90`, `ddx-937de9fc`.
In-scope files: `cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go` and any small test helpers it needs.

description:
PROBLEM
When the pre-claim decomposition model returns invalid or empty output, the deterministic fallback can create malformed children that are not independently executable. On 2026-07-13 it split ddx-e6abf4a6 into two partial implementation beads plus a verification-only child, omitted sibling dependencies, duplicated and contradicted the parent context, and failed to place the required package and lefthook gates on every implementation child. An operator had to rewrite and rewire the queue manually.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:480-539 chooses child count from the raw number of numbered AC lines and chunks those lines without distinguishing implementation criteria from shared verification gates or dependency order. cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:587-603 embeds a truncated parent description beneath newly generated PROBLEM and ROOT CAUSE headings, preserving stale PARENT/DEPS text and creating contradictory child prompts. cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:606-619 adds go test and lefthook only when those strings are absent anywhere in the parent acceptance, so existing gates become their own chunk instead of being copied to each executable child. cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:456-477 validates only non-empty title, description, and acceptance and therefore accepts children that fail the repository bead-authoring floor.

PROPOSED FIX
Make deterministic fallback fail safe. Separate implementation ACs from shared build gates, copy required go test and lefthook gates to every implementation child, generate standalone child context without nested or contradictory parent headings, and validate every proposed child against an execution-readiness contract before writing any tracker rows. Never create a verification-only child. If the fallback cannot prove standalone scope, complete AC coverage, and safe sibling ordering, do not apply it: leave or park the parent with a durable operator_attention/decomposition event explaining why manual refinement is required. Keep writes atomic so a rejected fallback creates no partial children.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go
- The nearest shared bead-readiness validator if reuse avoids duplicating the authoring rubric

NON-SCOPE
Do not change model/provider routing, decomposition timeout budgets, worker lifecycle visibility, or generic bead lint policy. Do not infer semantic dependencies from AC ordering when the evidence is ambiguous; use the operator-attention lane instead. Do not repair previously created children in migration code.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: none; root queue-integrity regression. Depends on ddx-2afca9c9 because this internal/agent change must pass the restored package-runtime gate.
notes:
Live deterministic-decomposition failures observed on Niflheim, 2026-07-13:
- ddx-9c3e703b was split into two implementation slices plus verification-only ddx-f3d774d3. The implementation children omitted required full-package/lefthook gates and dependencies. The operator rewrote ddx-c4497391 and ddx-03568f89, wired their dependency, and cancelled ddx-f3d774d3.
- ddx-20e2148b split fixture code from regression tripwires but omitted the dependency and full-package gate from ddx-7326dd54. The operator restored the gate and made ddx-e03399c8 depend on it.
- ddx-69672921 contained only three verification commands as its acceptance slice. A managed worker spent 14m26s and 4,137,315 tokens proving its focused regression green before the unrelated full suite timed out. The bead produced no implementation and reopened, demonstrating the direct throughput cost of verification-only children.
These are concrete production examples for TestFallbackPreClaimDecompositionRejectsVerificationOnlyChildren, TestFallbackPreClaimDecompositionProducesExecutionReadyChildren, and atomic rejection of invalid fallback output.
title: decomposition: refuse non-executable deterministic fallback children

description:
PROBLEM
Queue-control startup and pre-claim cleanup can remove stale worker sidecar directories, but it does not run the conservative stale attempt-descendant process pass before claiming more work. A worker can therefore continue draining while orphaned DDx-owned processes from older attempts still burn CPU or hold ports.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:218-267` removes stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` sidecar dirs, but it does not terminate or report the underlying stale DDx-owned process tree when a stale sidecar corresponds to an orphaned attempt descendant.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` owns cleanup of stale DDx execution artifacts, but work startup/pre-claim cleanup does not invoke the same conservative process pass before queue claim.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` handles startup reaping for orphaned top-level harnesses only and does not cover shell, cargo, benchmark, or test-server descendants left under stale execution worktrees.

PROPOSED FIX
Wire the same conservative stale attempt process cleanup pass into work startup/pre-claim cleanup before the next bead is claimed. Preserve existing cleanup lock and jitter behavior so multiple workers do not race to kill the same process group. Emit a structured cleanup observation whenever startup/pre-claim cleanup reaps, preserves, or cannot safely reap a stale process group. The startup path must use the same ownership and liveness rules as `ddx cleanup --apply`: current-project execution root, stale or missing run-state liveness, dead owner PID, no live worker sidecar, and no active registered worktree.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go` and existing work-startup cleanup tests.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` only as needed to reuse the process cleanup pass.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.
title: workers: run stale attempt process cleanup before bead claim

description:
PROBLEM

Dogfood on 2026-06-14 reproduced a false ddx sync failure immediately after successfully pushing HEAD to origin/main. The worktree was on dogfood/provider-failure-20260613 with HEAD and origin/main both at c0b72bd30028e8daa8f9775adc6d57f540f12ac7, but `ddx sync` fetched, merged origin/main, then failed twice with non-fast-forward. This makes the required sync-after-land step unreliable whenever the operator works from a non-main branch that tracks origin/main.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- cli/cmd/sync.go:198-200 documents and executes `git push origin main`. In git, that pushes the local branch named `main`, not the current HEAD. When the current branch is a dogfood/work branch and local `main` is stale, sync pushes stale local main and gets a legitimate remote rejection even though the checked-out branch is already aligned with origin/main.
- cli/cmd/sync.go:201-210 treats any rejected/non-fast-forward output as retryable, then as terminal after the second identical stale-branch push, without comparing current HEAD to origin/main after fetch.
- cli/cmd/sync_test.go:188-221 covers double push failure, but does not cover the non-main-branch success case where current HEAD equals origin/main and local main is stale.

PROPOSED FIX

Change sync push semantics to publish the checked-out commit to origin/main explicitly, e.g. `git push origin HEAD:main`, or otherwise resolve the current HEAD ref and push that SHA/ref to main. Before retrying or aborting on non-fast-forward, fetch and compare `rev-parse HEAD` with `rev-parse origin/main`; if already equal, treat sync as success and clear any failure marker. Add regression coverage using fake git responses for stale local main plus aligned HEAD/origin/main.

NON-SCOPE

Do not introduce force-push, rebase, squash, or no-verify behavior. Do not change execute-bead merge policy. Do not make sync manage arbitrary non-DDx dirty files.
title: sync: push current HEAD to origin/main from non-main branches

description:
PROBLEM
The unattended `ddx work` drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on the next pre-claim found `.crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter` dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard rather than continue. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. This breaks the unattended-drain goal: a stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message built at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (.ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, …) — added by ddx-df77e668. Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are NOT covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (pick one; implementer/operator decides)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink that has been materialized into a directory whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink) instead of implementation dirt.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills) in the cleanliness check, analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these tool-materialized skill paths so they never read as dirty (note: they are currently committed as symlinks, so this is a deliberate structural change).
Recommend A (most precise; only neutralizes the spurious symlink->dir case, preserves the guard for genuine edits).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits.
- Do not change what materializes the symlink (external skill tooling) — make ddx resilient to it.

CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668; same unattended-drain-resilience theme.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.
- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.
- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx update`. When set, update only the global plugin tree under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>; do not touch the project tree.
2. Without `--global`, `ddx update` updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree <project>/.ddx/plugins or convention-mode XDG project plugins).
3. `ddx doctor` reports both layers: a "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" line and a "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global" line, using the tier-resolution info exposed by the registry bead.
4. Land the spec/docs updates: file-or-update the skill-install-topology ADR (ADR-025 or the equivalent skill-topology record), document install locations in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", document agent-tier paths in library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md if that file exists, and document the install precedence in the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section.

NON-SCOPE
- install / init `--global` flags (sibling beads).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + tier reporting) — update and doctor consume it.
- install sibling bead — doctor's project-tier reporting assumes the install layout it produces.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-06cbaa90 spec updates (this bead's spec edits are in addition to those).
- ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" precedence note.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:archive, area:interchange, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: A: Archive + Interchange — add ctx, satisfy BeadArchive/InterchangeR/W; signature-only (TD-027 §6)

description:
PROBLEM: Two issues mask the intended design system (`.stitch/DESIGN.md`: warm-paper brutalist). (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. Operator decision: load Inter (do NOT introduce a new display font — that would be a spec change).

PROPOSED FIX: Regenerate `design-tokens.json` from `.stitch/DESIGN.md` (warm palette: #3B5B7A lever-steel, #F4EFE6 cream, etc.) so the export pipeline is the source of truth and carries the correct values; KEEP the `semanticColors` alias layer in tailwind.config.js (do not delete it; ensure it reads the regenerated tokens rather than shadowing stale ones). Add Inter via `@fontsource-variable/inter` (`bun add`), import in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/app.css`, so all non-mono roles render Inter instead of system fallback.

NON-SCOPE: introducing a distinctive display font (operator chose Inter-only); off-palette utility migration (separate bead); deleting the semanticColors layer.

2. A regression test simulates lefthook output where every hook skips because there are no staged files and asserts it is not accepted as pre-commit gate evidence for a code-changing bead.
3. Successful execute-bead attempts still pass when they stage specific files, run the staged gate meaningfully, create one implementation commit, and leave a clean worktree.
4. The result JSON or bead event explains the validation failure clearly enough for an operator to distinguish implementation failure from DDx infrastructure validation.

description:
PROBLEM
During Niflheim ddx work monitoring, attempt 20260518T021035-da282d6e for bead niflheim-bc47ed66 violated the execute-bead contract while still landing as success. The prompt told the implementer to run gates before committing, rerun lefthook after staging when staged files are required, commit exactly once, and stop after the commit.

Observed behavior:
- The agent ran lefthook before staging, so every hook skipped with no matching staged files.
- The agent committed 1551674d, then a later post-commit lefthook/clippy run found compile errors.
- The agent edited after the commit and amended to e7ae0424.
- A later cargo check found more missing shard_id initializers; the agent edited after the second final summary and amended again to 66beb39b.
- After those amendments, lefthook evidence still included a no-staged-files run, not a meaningful staged pre-commit gate.
- DDx accepted the attempt as success and landed it with result.json status=success/outcome=merged.

This makes the execution audit trail less trustworthy: a successful attempt can contain post-commit mutation, multiple amended implementation commits, and gate output that did not actually test the staged commit set.

PROPOSED FIX
- Teach execute-bead result validation to detect when the implementation commit was amended or replaced after the first successful commit event, and either reject the attempt or record a blocking validation error.
- Treat lefthook runs that report no matching staged files/no staged files as non-evidence for pre-commit acceptance when the prompt requires staged-file gates.
- Add a structured post-agent validation step that compares the first implementation commit event, final implementation_rev, dirty state, and evidence commands before marking success.
- Preserve successful retry behavior by allowing the orchestrator to rerun the bead in a fresh attempt instead of letting an in-attempt agent rewrite its own commit history.

EVIDENCE
Niflheim repo attempt: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/executions/20260518T021035-da282d6e
Final DDx result: implementation_rev=66beb39ba598dfe12301dec195f0673e750e1c98, status=success, outcome=merged.
Agent log shows commits 1551674d, e7ae0424, and 66beb39b in the same attempt.
title: execute-bead: reject post-commit mutation and empty gate evidence

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_HookFailureAbortsCommit` proves a nonzero hook blocks the commit, records failed gate evidence, and never retries with `--no-verify` or `LEFTHOOK=0`.
2. `TestExecuteBeadPreCommitEvidence_InvalidatedByStagedMutation` proves any staged content or relevant hook-config change invalidates prior green evidence and requires a new hook run.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-8a78ee90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Execute-bead agents are instructed to run `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then run `git commit`. The repository-installed Git pre-commit hook invokes Lefthook again on the same staged tree. During attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4`, a small containment patch ran the full staged `./cmd` plus `./internal/agent` race gate twice explicitly after one test correction, then `git commit` launched a third full pass on the identical final staged tree. The second explicit pass alone spent more than 18 minutes in `go test` output processing. This duplicate gate contract serializes the queue without adding evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2104-2113` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2137-2148` require rich and embedded agents to rerun `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then commit exactly once, without recognizing that the implementation commit invokes the installed hook.
- `lefthook.yml:205-216` maps staged Go files to package-wide race tests, so each duplicate invocation can repeat multi-minute `./cmd` and `./internal/agent` gates.
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit:11-18` invokes Lefthook for ordinary `git commit`, making a separately requested manual invocation redundant when the staged tree is unchanged.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the ordinary implementation commit hook the single authoritative staged pre-commit gate. Update both execute-bead prompt variants and integrity evidence so agents stage the exact commit set, run `git commit` normally, and treat the hook output/exit status from that commit as satisfying the bead’s `lefthook run pre-commit` acceptance criterion. If a workflow explicitly runs the hook before commit, reuse that success only when a content/config fingerprint proves the staged tree and hook inputs are identical; otherwise rerun. A failed hook must abort the commit and remain a failed gate.

NON-SCOPE
Using `--no-verify`, setting `LEFTHOOK=0`, weakening or removing repository hooks, reusing evidence after any staged-tree or hook-config change, caching across attempts, bypassing human commits, or changing which tests the hook selects.

PARENT
None; root worker-throughput regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-2afca9c9` (test-output/runtime headroom) and `ddx-03568f89` (gate evidence); no implementation dependency.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_HookFailureAbortsCommit` proves a nonzero hook blocks the commit, records failed gate evidence, and never retries with `--no-verify` or `LEFTHOOK=0`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The embedded-dispatch runner tests isolate provider configuration by changing HOME to a raw ddx-home-* directory. On Niflheim, an abandoned ddx-home-3567057526 contained a 468 MB private Go module cache with read-only module directories, and later gates recursively walked it. The current fixture pattern can leave behind the same kind of scratch tree and silently suppress cleanup failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29-34 and cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:56-61 call os.MkdirTemp with an empty root, change HOME, and discard os.RemoveAll errors. Changing HOME also changes the default GOPATH and GOMODCACHE for any Go subprocess or embedded tool that does not receive explicit values.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the raw os.MkdirTemp fixtures with a shared test helper backed by t.TempDir so cleanup failures fail the test. Before changing HOME, preserve an explicit stable Go module-cache/GOPATH policy for child work so the isolated provider-config HOME cannot become a second module cache. Keep the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch tests using the helper and leave the no-real-provider/no-exec-path assertions intact.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production runner routing, make live provider calls, delete existing host scratch, or change production cleanup ownership/budgets. ddx-f914dddd and ddx-de1ff4b4 own those cleanup changes.

PARENT
ddx-6a2ce3bd.

DEPS
None.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go
- nearest internal/agent test helper if shared setup is warranted

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx worker status` reports the persisted desired state, but when `desired_count=1` and no live worker exists after the last server-managed worker exited from fd exhaustion, the operator still cannot see why the desired worker is missing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/worker.go:121-158 loads `.ddx/workers/desired.json` and prints only project, desired_count, restart state, default spec, and updated_at.
cli/cmd/worker_test.go:81-96 only covers the absent-desired-state case, so there is no regression coverage for a missing worker with a terminal fd-exhaustion reason.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the worker status report with missing-worker count and an fd-exhaustion diagnosis when desired workers are absent, using the new supervisor helper rather than brittle string matching. Preserve the existing absent-state message and the current desired-state fields in both text and JSON output.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change `ddx worker enable`, desired-state persistence, or `ddx work status`. Do not weaken byte/inode resource guards. Do not alter the stale operator-attention fixes tracked by ddx-b17cad6d and ddx-4d92f2cf. Do not introduce a second fd classifier.

Governing artifact: ddx-9c53e551.

acceptance:
1. TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_AppliesToProviderDescendants launches a real bounded helper whose harness root forks at least four CPU-busy descendants, including one that calls `setsid` and reparents, and proves the controller still contains the complete attempt tree with an effective default quota of 200 percent.
2. TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_ManualAndManagedParity runs the shared executor configuration through manual/offline and server-managed construction paths and proves requested/effective quota semantics are identical; the managed path still carries the existing parent-death behavior.
3. TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_DisabledIsExplicit proves a configured quota of 0 leaves the attempt unbounded and records that explicit policy rather than a controller failure.
4. TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_DegradedFallbackIsObservable proves an unavailable platform controller lowers scheduling priority, keeps the attempt runnable, and emits structured evidence with requested quota, effective mode, and fallback reason.
5. TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_CleanupRemovesController proves normal exit, timeout, cancellation, and provider-start failure do not leak controller state or descendants, including a session-changing/reparented tool child.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... -run "TestAttemptCPUEnvelope_(AppliesToProviderDescendants|ManualAndManagedParity|DisabledIsExplicit|DegradedFallbackIsObservable|CleanupRemovesController)" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/integration/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A single DDx attempt can monopolize the entire server-owned CPU allowance. On 2026-07-13, TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace launched five Codex providers whose aggregate use reached about 350 percent CPU, nearly saturating the installed server CPUQuota=400 percent. The aggregate service cap protected the host but provided no fairness between attempts, and a manual/offline worker outside ddx-server.service has no equivalent cap at all.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/server_service.go:98-102 exposes only an aggregate CPU quota and weight for the complete server-owned worker/provider/build tree. cli/internal/service/service.go:15-22 defines that policy explicitly as aggregate. cli/internal/agent/executor.go:177-188 starts each provider directly, while cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 configures only a process group and parent-death signal; no attempt-scoped CPU controller contains the provider and all descendants. Consequently one nested test/provider fanout can consume the whole managed-service budget, while the same manual worker path is unbounded.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add one attempt-scoped CPU-envelope abstraction at the provider execution boundary so the harness root and every descendant share a bounded controller. Membership must survive process-group/session changes, double-forking, and reparenting; cleanup and cancellation must act on controller membership rather than ancestry alone.
- Apply the same default and configuration to server-managed and manual/offline workers without requiring a live DDx server. Preserve the existing parent-death behavior so managed provider trees still die when their worker/server dies.
- Use a conservative default of 200 percent of one core per active attempt, allow an explicit 0 value to disable the per-attempt cap, and keep the existing aggregate server service cap as the outer ceiling.
- Emit structured attempt evidence containing requested/effective quota, controller kind, descendant membership, and fallback reason. When the platform cannot enforce a hard quota, continue with lowered scheduling priority and an operator-visible degraded-control event rather than silently claiming enforcement.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go and adjacent platform-specific executor/resource-controller files
- cli/internal/config execution settings and validation
- cli/cmd/work.go and cli/cmd/try.go only for shared configuration plumbing
- cli/internal/integration for a real descendant-fanout proof

NON-SCOPE
Do not replace or remove the aggregate ddx-server.service CPUQuota/CPUWeight policy. Do not throttle arbitrary external cargo, Go, provider, or user processes that are not descendants of the current DDx attempt. Do not require the coordination server for manual/offline execution. Do not change desired worker count, queue ordering, provider routing, or the process-group/Pdeathsig ownership contract. ddx-aa6cc320 remains responsible for removing live providers from the default test fixture.
notes: Provider-descendant CPU containment belongs to the Fizeau harness/runtime boundary, not DDx attempt execution.
title: agent: enforce a per-attempt CPU envelope for provider descendants

acceptance:
1. TestExpensiveReplayDetector_EquivalentFailedCommandAcrossWrappersCancels proves direct, timeout-wrapped, redirected/background, and setsid-wrapped forms of the same failed go test command share one semantic fingerprint and the second launch cancels before another suite runs.
2. TestExpensiveReplayDetector_WorktreeOrConfigMutationAllowsOneRevalidation proves a changed tracked-worktree or relevant environment/config fingerprint permits exactly one new validation and records why the prior output was invalid.
3. TestExpensiveReplayDetector_DistinctAndCheapCommandsRemainAllowed proves different test packages, changed flags, and cheap read-only shell commands do not trip the replay circuit.
4. TestExpensiveReplayDetector_ReapsDetachedReplay proves cancellation removes a setsid/reparented tool child from the attempt controller and emits operator_attention with bead ID, attempt ID, prior/current fingerprints, outcomes, and reason.
5. Existing TestLoopDetector_FiresAfterRepeatedIdenticalCommands and TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion remain green.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "^(TestExpensiveReplayDetector_(EquivalentFailedCommandAcrossWrappersCancels|WorktreeOrConfigMutationAllowsOneRevalidation|DistinctAndCheapCommandsRemainAllowed|ReapsDetachedReplay)|TestLoopDetector_FiresAfterRepeatedIdenticalCommands|TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During attempt 20260713T145533-c006900a, the model ran cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s, received the same host-scratch traversal failure, reran it after operator termination, and finally launched the equivalent suite through setsid -f with redirected output. The detached run entered a new session and continued consuming CPU/I/O until the operator killed it. The prompt explicitly prohibited an unchanged long-command replay, but DDx did not intervene.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2088-2094 expresses the command-fingerprint rule only as model instructions.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:39-76 keys loop detection on tool name, raw input JSON, error text, and result output. The same expensive command with a different termination message, redirection, timeout wrapper, or setsid wrapper therefore produces a different key.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:268-271 and 399-415 require four identical call-result pairs in an eight-entry window, so two or three costly replays can consume tens of minutes without firing.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:32-39 cannot reap a replay that changes session/process group; ddx-7df6b12a owns the attempt controller needed after cancellation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an expensive-command replay detector over normalized service tool-call events. Canonicalize shell wrappers that do not change command semantics (cd into the same worktree, timeout, setsid, output redirection, foreground/background launch) while retaining command args, environment/config inputs, cwd, and a worktree/config fingerprint. After an expensive command fails, times out, or ends incomplete, cancel the attempt before a second equivalent replay unless the normalized args/environment/config or worktree fingerprint changed and the event records why the previous evidence is invalid. Persist a structured operator_attention event containing both fingerprints, outcomes, and the cancellation reason. Cancel through the ddx-7df6b12a attempt controller so detached/session-changing children are reaped.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go and adjacent replay-detector helpers/tests
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go for correlation/evidence plumbing
- attempt-controller integration introduced by ddx-7df6b12a
- execute-bead event/operator-attention mapping for replay cancellation

NON-SCOPE
Do not block distinct commands, cheap read-only commands, one legitimate validation after a code/config fingerprint change, or manual operator commands outside an attempt. Do not infer success from a killed or incomplete command. Do not lower the generic four-pair loop detector for non-expensive tools.

PARENT AND DEPS
Root reliability incident. Depends on ddx-7df6b12a for controller-backed cancellation. Related to ddx-45ea2e36, which owns the independent hard attempt deadline.
notes: Shell-wrapper semantic replay policing belongs inside the selected harness; DDx reacts to typed attempt outcomes.
title: agent: stop equivalent expensive command replays across shell wrappers

description:
PROBLEM
DDx evidence limits vary by concrete harness name even though harness selection belongs to Fizeau and can change independently of the task. The existing resolved cap is also not wired into all production prompt roles. Evidence policy must resolve only from the DDx semantic invocation role: implementer, reviewer, or lifecycle.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/config/types.go:191-224 exposes EvidenceCapsConfig.PerHarness; cli/internal/config/evidence_caps.go:5-30 and cli/internal/evidence/caps.go:64-77 resolve concrete harness overrides. cli/internal/config/resolved.go:85-114 couples sealing to r.harness and lines 330-333 expose one harness-bound result. cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:636-679 advertises per_harness. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:146-171 does not wire caps to production reviewer construction, so cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:26-42 and execute_bead_review.go:1034-1055 fall back to defaults. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1921-1936,2323-2413 lacks implementer caps and cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go:658-670 hardcodes repair defaults. FEAT-022 lines 79-100 still govern per-harness overrides.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace evidence_caps.per_harness with strict per_role keys implementer, reviewer, and lifecycle. Reject every legacy per_harness declaration, including empty/null, through the pre-schema migration hook with an actionable typed error. Expose EvidenceCapsForRole(role) and remove cap resolution against harness/provider/model. Thread implementer caps into execute/repair prompt assembly, reviewer caps through CLI and server reviewer construction, and lifecycle caps through pre-claim intake, lint, and post-attempt triage. Update schema, clone/default/resolution tests, examples, migration docs, and FEAT-022 to define route-neutral role calibration.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau routing, model context windows, provider catalogs, evidence ranking/redaction/truncation/floors, add role-to-route mappings, use caps for routing/retry/escalation/close policy, or retain per_harness as a supported alias.

PARENT
ddx-1b3272a6.

DEPS
ddx-e2c3453b removes legacy concrete provider configuration before this remaining route-coupled schema migration.
title: evidence: replace harness-keyed caps with DDx semantic roles

description:
PROBLEM
DDx can correctly classify an attempt as no_evidence_produced or execution_failed inside the execution engine, then overwrite that typed outcome as no_changes at the ddx try and ddx work command boundaries merely because ResultRev equals BaseRev. A harness that returns no commit and no rationale is therefore reported as a legitimate no-change attempt, hiding execution failures and bypassing the no-evidence retry/operator-attention path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1529-1584 distinguishes clean no-evidence from rationale-backed no_changes, and populateWorkerStatus at lines 1803-1816 records no_evidence_produced. cli/cmd/try.go:389-399 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:476-485 later normalize solely from ResultRev == BaseRev and overwrite the typed status/outcome. docs/execute-bead-self-commit-contract.md:3-17 requires a commit or explicit no_changes_rationale; absence of both is a harness failure, not no_changes.

PROPOSED FIX
Normalize try and work results from the typed worker status/outcome first. Preserve no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, and other authoritative non-success outcomes even when revisions are equal. acceptance:
1. TestCaptureLifecycleProjectStatus_CancellationKillsGitStatus installs a blocking git-status shim, cancels the caller context, and proves the snapshot returns a deadline/cancellation error within one second with no surviving shim process.
2. TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation_BoundsStatusSnapshot proves the pre-claim intake hook preserves project-root mutation rejection while a blocked before, after, or restored snapshot is bounded and reported as PreClaimIntakeError rather than hanging the package.
3. Production before, after, and restored lifecycle snapshots use the caller context plus an explicit per-snapshot deadline no greater than five seconds; RepairKnownConfigCorruption and internalgit.Command receive that derived context.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(CaptureLifecycleProjectStatus_CancellationKillsGitStatus|LifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation_BoundsStatusSnapshot)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The normal internal/agent gate timed out after 10 minutes while TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation waited for captureLifecycleProjectStatus. One unbounded project-status subprocess can consume the complete package budget, fail unrelated beads, and force the queue to retry work whose focused acceptance tests are already green.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:95-117 accepts no context. At line 105 it calls RepairKnownConfigCorruption with context.Background, and at line 110 it runs git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all with context.Background. The before, after, and restored snapshots at cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:47, 144, and 157 therefore outlive caller cancellation and have no per-operation deadline. In attempt 20260713T104022-f6d5cb82, the package timeout stack showed TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation blocked in internalgit.Command(... status ...).CombinedOutput at lifecycle_dispatch.go:110.

PROPOSED FIX
Thread the lifecycle dispatch caller context through every project-status snapshot and repository-health repair. Derive a short per-snapshot deadline, capped by the caller deadline, and ensure cancellation terminates and waits for the Git subprocess. Return a typed lifecycle snapshot error that preserves deadline/cancellation diagnostics so the intake hook fails promptly instead of holding the package gate.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go or a focused lifecycle dispatch test file
- nearest internal Git test helper only if needed for a blocking git shim

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken project-root mutation detection, skip pre/post/restored snapshots, change queue retry policy, increase the package timeout, or alter unrelated Git locking and tracker serialization. Do not rerun or close ddx-69672921 in this bead.

PARENT
None; root lifecycle reliability regression.

DEPS
None.

1. TestRunAgentViaService_HonorsServiceRunFactory installs a passthroughTestService through SetServiceRunFactory, invokes the legacy RunAgent/runAgentViaService path, and proves the override receives the requested workdir and Execute request without constructing a live Fizeau service.
2. runAgentViaService calls resolveService; with a nil override, resolveService continues to call NewServiceFromWorkDir so production routing behavior is unchanged.
3. The test restores SetServiceRunFactory(nil) with t.Cleanup and does not use t.Parallel or leak the process-global override into neighboring tests.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^TestRunAgentViaService_HonorsServiceRunFactory$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The legacy Runner embedded-provider path cannot be isolated with the package test factory. Tests that only need to prove lmstudio/openrouter dispatch reaches RunAgent instead construct a real Fizeau service, start discovery/background state work, and race t.TempDir cleanup. Attempt 20260713T114250-0509adf3 repeatedly failed cleanup with late files under HOME/.cache/fizeau and XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/projects/local until it patched the production seam.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:112-123 calls NewServiceFromWorkDir directly inside runAgentViaService.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:22-80 defines SetServiceRunFactory and resolveService specifically so internal callers honor a hermetic test service while production falls back to NewServiceFromWorkDir. RunWithConfigViaService uses that resolver, but the legacy RunAgent path bypasses it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Change runAgentViaService in cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go to obtain its service through resolveService(wd), preserving the nil-factory production fallback.
- Add TestRunAgentViaService_HonorsServiceRunFactory in cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service_test.go. Install a passthroughTestService factory, record the supplied workdir/request, invoke the legacy RunAgent path, and restore the global factory with t.Cleanup.
- Keep the factory override test-only; do not introduce a production configuration path or alter route/provider selection.

NON-SCOPE
Do not refactor the HOME/XDG fixture itself; child/consumer bead ddx-7326dd54 owns that. Do not change Fizeau discovery cancellation or process reaping; ddx-a751a7b1 and ddx-93d8b7c8 own those fixes. Do not add ignored RemoveAll cleanup.

PARENT
ddx-7326dd54.

DEPS
No deps.

acceptance:
1. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded calls testing.Short before newLandTestRepo or any git/bootstrap work and skips with a diagnostic that identifies the dedicated load matrix.
2. TestLandingChaosShortModeSkipsPreservationLoadMatrix invokes the package test binary in short mode for only TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded and proves it reports SKIP within 30 seconds without creating a landing repository/worktree fixture.
3. Running TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded without -short still executes the original deterministic four-worker, ten-cycle matrix and retains the preservation-rate, parentage, reachability, and status assertions.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestLandingChaosShortModeSkipsPreservationLoadMatrix)$" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
5. cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent passes with the preservation load matrix reported as skipped and without recursive provider-launch descendants.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes on the final staged tree.
description:
PROBLEM
The mandatory short agent-package validation is still CPU- and I/O-heavy after provider-shim recursion is removed. During attempt 20260713T195457-44c4a97d, one non-recursive agent.test process spent more than 16 minutes in TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded, grew the Go action log beyond 600 MB, and repeatedly created real repositories, commits, and worktrees. This makes every code edit and pre-commit hook pay a chaos/load-test cost even when invoked with -short, slowing queue throughput and obscuring actual regressions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go:175-183 starts TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded without checking testing.Short and fixes the matrix at four concurrent workers across ten cycles. cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go:189-243 then creates four commits and calls concurrent real Land operations in every cycle, including temporary worktrees, bootstrap repositories, merge attempts, and preservation refs. The test is intentionally a load/chaos matrix but is currently included in cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent.

PROPOSED FIX
Classify the preservation-rate matrix as a long-running test and skip it before repository setup when testing.Short is true. Add a subprocess regression test proving short mode reports the load matrix as skipped without creating its landing fixture. Preserve the full non-short matrix and its deterministic seed so dedicated or nightly validation retains the original concurrency coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not reduce the worker/cycle matrix in non-short mode.
- Do not weaken preservation-rate assertions or landing correctness.
- Do not remove the mandatory short race gate.
- Do not change provider-shim behavior owned by ddx-5653688f or the broader attempt CPU controller owned by parent ddx-7df6b12a.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent ddx-7df6b12a. No implementation dependencies; this is an independently green test-runtime fix.

acceptance:
1. `TestBeadQueueTopPrecedesExistingRankZero` seeds an older rank-zero bead, tops a newer bead, and proves the topped bead has a strictly lower numeric rank and is first in ReadyExecution order.
2. `TestOpsQueue_TopRepeatedCallsStrictlyReorder` tops at least three beads successively and proves each most-recent target becomes uniquely first with deterministic ordering of prior targets.
3. `TestBeadQueueTopPreservesOtherPriorityBucket` proves top leaves every rank and row in an unrelated priority bucket unchanged.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/ops/queue/... -run '^(TestBeadQueueTop(PrecedesExistingRankZero|PreservesOtherPriorityBucket)|TestOpsQueue_TopRepeatedCallsStrictlyReorder)$' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-07-13 Niflheim queue drain, the operator ran `ddx bead queue top` on four newly discovered P0 reliability beads. Each command succeeded and committed, but all four beads received `queue-rank: 0`; `ddx bead ready` continued to put the older retry bead `ddx-415c8b95` first. The command therefore does not fulfill “move to the front” once any bead in the priority bucket already has rank zero.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go:25-32` implements `Top` as a direct `QueueSetTop{}` mutation without reading the priority bucket.
- `cli/internal/bead/operation.go:8-17` hard-codes every `QueueSetTop` result to rank zero.
- `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go:34-75` already has the correct bucket-aware `Move(..., position=0)` machinery, including sparse negative ranks and renormalization, but `Top` bypasses it.
- `cli/cmd/bead_queue_test.go:48-71` tests top only against unranked beads and misses existing-rank-zero and repeated-top cases.
This regresses the ordering semantics specified and previously closed by `ddx-d0c656b0`.

PROPOSED FIX
Make `Top` use the same bucket-aware positioning contract as `Move` so the target strictly sorts before the current first bead, including when one or many entries already have rank zero. Preserve priority and avoid rewriting unrelated priority buckets. Add command- and ops-level repeated-top coverage.

NON-SCOPE
Changing priority values, global cross-priority ordering, clearing operator ranks, changing ready eligibility, or manually editing tracker JSONL.

PARENT
None; root queue-ordering regression found while prioritizing Niflheim reliability work.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestReleaseWorkflowResolvesManualAndTagEventToSameImmutableTag parses release.yml and proves tag push uses github.ref_name, manual dispatch uses inputs.tag, every job checks out the resolved tag, build metadata uses the resolved tag commit, and action-gh-release receives that exact tag_name.
2. TestReleaseWorkflowDoesNotUseAmbientRefForReleaseIdentity fails if version, changelog range, prerelease decision, release name/tag, final URL, or build commit uses ambient github.ref_name/github.sha outside the one tag-push resolver.
3. TestReleaseChecklistNamesNineAssetAndSmokeProofs proves docs/releasing.md names four archives, four sidecar checksums, aggregate checksums.sha256, workflow success, checksum validation, binary version smoke, explicit prerelease install selection, tag SHA, workflow URL, and Go/No-Go evidence.
4. TestContributingReleaseGuidanceUsesDurableChecklist proves CONTRIBUTING.md links docs/releasing.md and contains no .ddx/skills/ddx-release or /ddx-release invocation.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'Test(ReleaseWorkflowResolvesManualAndTagEventToSameImmutableTag|ReleaseWorkflowDoesNotUseAmbientRefForReleaseIdentity|ReleaseChecklistNamesNineAssetAndSmokeProofs|ContributingReleaseGuidanceUsesDurableChecklist)$' -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The release workflow advertises manual tag dispatch but ignores its tag input, and contributor guidance points to a deleted release skill instead of a durable checklist. A tag-push release works, but the alternative entry point can build/release the wrong ref and the repository has no checked-in operator proof contract for the nine expected assets and post-release smoke checks.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .github/workflows/release.yml:7-12 accepts workflow_dispatch.inputs.tag, but lines 35-54 and 324-337 read github.ref_name; manual dispatch from main validates, builds, names, and links the branch rather than the requested tag. Checkout steps also omit the requested ref and build metadata uses github.sha instead of the resolved tag commit.
- CONTRIBUTING.md:138-149 directs operators to nonexistent .ddx/skills/ddx-release/SKILL.md and /ddx-release.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-007-release-readiness.md:174-186 requires a repository release checklist covering tag, CI, artifacts, site, and installer verification, but no current checklist exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Resolve one immutable release tag and commit for both tag-push and manual-dispatch events. Propagate that value through every checkout, version, changelog, build metadata, prerelease decision, release tag/name, and final URL. Add structural tests that fail if any release consumer falls back to github.ref_name/github.sha for release identity. Add docs/releasing.md with pre-tag gates, annotated tag/push, expected four archives + four sidecars + aggregate checksum, workflow/release verification, checksum and binary-version smoke checks, prerelease installer behavior, evidence fields, and rollback/no-history-rewrite rules. Point CONTRIBUTING.md at that checklist.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim bit-reproducible artifacts while Bun, runner images, actions, and timestamps remain floating; do not pin those dependencies in this bead, change release asset formats, publish a tag, rewrite an existing tag, or bypass the full Go/pre-commit/release workflow gates.

PARENT
None; release infrastructure repair discovered during alpha105 audit.

DEPS
ddx-305e8ddb, which establishes the verified frontend artifact consumed by this workflow.
title: release: make tag identity and operator proof checklist truthful

description:
PROBLEM
DDx can persist only a free-text external blocker reason or a structured cross-repo bead reference. Local resource failures therefore cannot be distinguished safely from manual blockers after the host recovers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:761-825 defines ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason and ExtraLifecycleCrossRepoBlockerRef, but no typed local blocker representation. cli/internal/bead/store.go:852-870 clears cross-repo metadata on transition out of blocked but has no equivalent local-resource marker to validate or clear.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a typed local blocker marker in cli/internal/bead/local_blocker.go with a closed enum containing local_resource_exhaustion, plus optional resource roots/fingerprint needed for recheck evidence.
- Add parse/validation helpers matching the CrossRepoBlockerRef pattern.
- Store the marker under a named Extra key and clear it on every transition out of blocked.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/bead/local_blocker.go
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/local_blocker_test.go

NON-SCOPE
- Do not classify agent text or run resource probes.
- Do not modify cross-repo blocker behavior.

PARENT
- ddx-5958e8a4.

DEPS
No deps.

3. `TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_HookFailureAbortsCommit` proves a nonzero hook blocks the commit, records failed gate evidence, and never retries with `--no-verify` or `LEFTHOOK=0`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadPreCommitEvidence_InvalidatedByStagedMutation` proves any staged content or relevant hook-config change invalidates prior green evidence and requires a new hook run.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "^(TestExecuteBeadPrompt_UsesImplementationCommitAsSinglePreCommitGate|TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_RunsPreCommitOnceForUnchangedIndex|TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_HookFailureAbortsCommit|TestExecuteBeadPreCommitEvidence_InvalidatedByStagedMutation)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-bead agents are instructed to run `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then run `git commit`. The repository-installed Git pre-commit hook invokes Lefthook again on the same staged tree. During attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4`, a small containment patch ran the full staged `./cmd` plus `./internal/agent` race gate twice explicitly after one test correction, then `git commit` launched a third full pass on the identical final staged tree. The second explicit pass alone spent more than 18 minutes in `go test` output processing. This duplicate gate contract serializes the queue without adding evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2104-2113` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2137-2148` require rich and embedded agents to rerun `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then commit exactly once, without recognizing that the implementation commit invokes the installed hook.
- `lefthook.yml:205-216` maps staged Go files to package-wide race tests, so each duplicate invocation can repeat multi-minute `./cmd` and `./internal/agent` gates.
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit:11-18` invokes Lefthook for ordinary `git commit`, making a separately requested manual invocation redundant when the staged tree is unchanged.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the ordinary implementation commit hook the single authoritative staged pre-commit gate. Update both execute-bead prompt variants and integrity evidence so agents stage the exact commit set, run `git commit` normally, and treat the hook output/exit status from that commit as satisfying the bead’s `lefthook run pre-commit` acceptance criterion. If a workflow explicitly runs the hook before commit, reuse that success only when a content/config fingerprint proves the staged tree and hook inputs are identical; otherwise rerun. A failed hook must abort the commit and remain a failed gate.

NON-SCOPE
Using `--no-verify`, setting `LEFTHOOK=0`, weakening or removing repository hooks, reusing evidence after any staged-tree or hook-config change, caching across attempts, bypassing human commits, or changing which tests the hook selects.

PARENT
None; root worker-throughput regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-2afca9c9` (test-output/runtime headroom) and `ddx-03568f89` (gate evidence); no implementation dependency.

notes:
Second live reproduction in attempt 20260713T163832-72b324a1: an explicit lefthook run pre-commit passed on the final staged tree after 27m0.869s. The immediately following ordinary git commit launched the identical internal/agent race hook again; within 31 seconds the cached replay was already consuming about 1.55 GiB RSS and one CPU core. The operator terminated only the duplicate hook group and committed the already-verified unchanged index with hooks disabled for that invocation so the valid patch could land. The product fix must make this operator workaround unnecessary and retain fail-closed invalidation on staged changes.

Attempt ddx-02200b1e / 20260713T174439-e938b97a adds the complementary pre-stage case: its explicit full race gate passed in 22m30.061s and generated a 625,717,248-byte testlog. The bead still required staged lefthook and an ordinary commit, which would replay that same package gate at least once and potentially twice on an unchanged tree. The model stalled before staging; the operator checkpointed c6dd2b61b and stopped the attempt rather than permit the duplicate. Prompt, bead-acceptance, and implementation-commit evidence must share one content-fingerprinted gate result so these three syntactic requirements do not become three executions.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx cannot be trusted to drain work while live lock deletion, unsafe stale-lock breaking, non-hermetic gates, ambiguous close evidence, and uncontrolled scratch/process cleanup remain possible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The governing recovery plan identifies eight unfinished work packages at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:80-165. The current tracker covers isolated symptoms but has no phase-level owner for the intake freeze, operator reclamation, scratch ownership, observe-only lock caps, atomic stale-lock breaking, truthful gates, evidence-owned attempt outcomes, and weekly A/B baseline.

PROPOSED FIX
Track and complete WB-1 through WB-8 from the governing plan. Child beads must preserve the DDx/Fizeau boundary: DDx owns work, attempts, evidence, review, retry/escalation policy, landing, and closure; Fizeau owns route selection and provider process trees. The phase exits only on the mechanical measurements at lines 54-67.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add DDx provider routing, provider-tree supervision, model selection, or harness-specific recovery. Do not declare the phase complete from closed historical beads whose behavior is absent from the current tree.

PARENT
None; root recovery epic.

DEPS
None.
title: Epic: stop DDx from damaging work and host state

2. TestReleaseChangelogIncludesCommitsBehindUnpublishedTags proves the generated alpha102..alpha105 range includes commits associated with unpublished alpha103 and alpha104 as well as later untagged commits, while excluding alpha102 history.
3. TestReleaseWorkflowUsesPublishedReleaseBaseline proves .github/workflows/release.yml invokes the tested selector with RELEASE_COMMIT and published GitHub Release metadata, never git describe alone, and fails clearly when no baseline can be proven.

description:
PROBLEM
The release workflow derives changelog history from the nearest local tag, not the previous published GitHub Release. Tags v0.6.2-alpha103 and v0.6.2-alpha104 exist but have no published releases, while v0.6.2-alpha102 is the latest published release. An alpha105 changelog based on alpha104 would silently omit every unpublished alpha103/alpha104 commit from user-visible notes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
.github/workflows/release.yml:71-80 runs git describe --tags --abbrev=0 on the release parent and exports that tag as previous_tag without checking GitHub Release publication. .github/workflows/release.yml:288-299 then logs only PREVIOUS_TAG..RELEASE_COMMIT. cli/internal/server/release_workflow_test.go:143-161 asserts immutable release identity and the range endpoint but does not exercise a repository containing unpublished intervening tags.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a deterministic baseline selector used by release.yml that chooses the newest published, non-draft GitHub Release tag reachable from RELEASE_COMMIT; for alpha105 the current evidence requires v0.6.2-alpha102.
- If GitHub Release metadata is unavailable, fail closed or choose a documented deterministic range that includes all commits since the last provably published release; never silently use the nearest tag.
- Put selection/range logic in a testable script or helper and keep RELEASE_COMMIT as the immutable upper bound.
- Add hermetic tests with local commits/tags alpha102 through alpha105 and injected/stubbed release metadata; tests must not call the network.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not publish alpha103 or alpha104 retroactively.
- Do not change tag identity, artifact build inputs, prerelease classification, or release asset naming.
- Do not infer publication from tag existence alone.

PARENT
None; this is a root alpha105 release-correctness blocker.

DEPS
ddx-8976c5cc must remain closed because it establishes immutable tag identity and release proof. No alpha105 release bead exists as of filing.
title: release: derive changelog baseline from the previous published release

description:
PROBLEM
DDx correctly intends to request a stronger reviewer, but today it queries Fizeau profile metadata, selects a profile, clears the primary operator envelope, and may set review-specific concrete routes. DDx must express review strength only as abstract MinPower while Fizeau independently chooses the harness, provider, model, and internal policy.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:819-866 builds reviewer requests with HarnessOverride and ProfileOverride and clears routing pins, profile, and MaxPower.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:869-939 calls LoadProfileSnapshot and SelectStrongestProfile or SelectStrongestProfileAbove.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:941-949 and 1222-1248 apply review-specific model/profile pins.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:1375-1388 turns concrete provider pairing into an escalation signal.
- cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:70-84 and service_run.go:226-277 prove these fields reach the public Fizeau Execute request.

PROPOSED FIX
Build reviewer requests from the immutable primary-attempt operator passthrough envelope, preserved byte-for-byte including MaxPower and any explicit Harness, Provider, Model, or public Policy constraint. Add only a stronger abstract MinPower than the implementer actual power. Delete reviewer profile/catalog loading, review-specific concrete pins, implementer route correlation used as policy, and provider-pairing escalation. Keep the Fizeau-returned concrete route in execution audit evidence only.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change primary-attempt difficulty mapping, reviewer cardinality, typed transport retry, gate repair, or Fizeau routing internals.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-b8cacbeb. Depends on ddx-666456c8 and ddx-af65a486 so review-tier and global difficulty/profile cleanup land before the overlapping reviewer request rewrite.
title: review: dispatch stronger reviewers with minimum power only

description:
PROBLEM
A server-managed ddx work process can be terminated mid-attempt even while its bead lease and worker heartbeat are fresh. The parent regression incident recorded worker worker-20260711T204017-68e9, PID 303304, emitting a fresh heartbeat for ddx-69591a68 at 2026-07-13T05:47:15Z, then receiving cancellation and exiting 130 at 2026-07-13T05:47:45Z. This regresses ddx-57faa17c because that fix preserved the bead claim but did not protect the live process.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1447-1449 calls cleanupManagedWorkerProcessTree for every live PID named by a disk record before cli/internal/server/workers.go:1451-1467 asks staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim whether the fresh lease is safe to reclaim. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1586-1614 correctly refuses claim release for fresh or live same-machine leases, but that decision arrives after the process has already been killed. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1470-1482 can still rewrite the record stopped after that premature cleanup decision.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor stopStaleDiskEntry so it classifies whether a stale disk record is reclaimable before sending any signal or rewriting its state. When the disk record names a live same-machine PID and staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim refuses release because the bead lease or heartbeat is fresh, preserve the process, preserve the in_progress claim, and leave the worker record out of the stopped path. Add a structured lifecycle/supervisor diagnostic for this preservation path containing worker ID, PID, bead ID, and preservation reason.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker count, generic process cleanup, provider parent-death behavior, or manual/server coordination owned by ddx-635ae251. Do not weaken cleanup of dead PIDs or genuinely stale leases. Do not implement supervisor duplicate-prevention behavior beyond the direct stopStaleDiskEntry preservation path.

PARENT
Split from root regression bead "server: preserve live managed PIDs during stale-record cleanup".

None.

description:
PROBLEM
The DDx execute loop returns from lifecycle model discovery after the two-second deadline, but the PTY probe can keep running as a direct worker child and accumulate CPU after the caller has moved on.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:519-533` gives `svc.ListModels(modelCtx, modelFilter)` a two-second context but does not prove probe teardown before returning to the queue loop.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a DDx call-site containment wrapper around lifecycle model discovery that binds probe lifetime to the worker/caller context, waits for the spawned PTY session/process group to exit on success, error, timeout, or cancellation, and adds a regression harness for the queue-drain path.

NON-SCOPE
Disabling model discovery, extending the two-second caller timeout, killing manual Codex sessions or provider processes that predate the call, changing route selection, or restarting the DDx server.

PARENT
None; root reliability/performance regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-092f0d34` (preclaim lifecycle containment) and regression of `ddx-403e9a23`; do not touch unrelated provider processes.
notes:
Prior attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4` failed after 68 minutes and was discarded. Do not repeat its broad containment design: it snapshotted every descendant of the worker, matched provider basenames plus `/model`, and killed all new process groups. That races concurrent legitimate providers, can target the worker process group, uses unbounded `ps`, ignores cleanup errors/survivors, and was not portable off Unix. It also passed live contexts into preflight service variants whose canceled quota context intentionally suppresses background probes. Required tests must cover concurrent provider ownership, worker-PGID safety, bounded enumeration failure, cleanup errors, portability, and preservation of intentional preflight cancellation; containment must be scoped to the lifecycle discovery call, not the whole worker descendant tree.

Concurrent attempt `20260713T125244-84b1d082` landed commit `020853315` on main with the two named tests; the focused AC command passed again on 2026-07-13 in 0.786s. The bead remains open because the attempt recorded `required_exec_summary: skipped` and no closing SHA. Treat the landed code as the starting point: verify/fix it and produce required gate evidence rather than creating a second parallel wrapper. Ownership and bounded-cleanup hardening is queued as `ddx-e1e229f5`.

description:
PROBLEM
A watch worker can prove its temp/evidence roots are healthy on every dispatch, yet beads previously blocked by local resource exhaustion remain outside candidate selection forever. This required manually reopening fifteen beads after the July 11 ENOSPC incident.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2352-2363 performs an idle-path auto-reopen only for retryable provider-connectivity proposals before nextCandidate. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2492-2576 reports and sleeps on no ready work without rechecking blocked local resources. cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:208-300 already supplies the authoritative root health probe, but the idle lifecycle path never applies it to typed blocked beads.

PROPOSED FIX
- Before candidate selection in watch mode, list blocked beads carrying the typed local_resource_exhaustion marker from ddx-c4cbf253.
- Run the configured ExecutionResourceChecker once per idle pass. Reopen all matching beads through LifecycleTransitionOptions{ManualReopen:true} only when the probe is healthy.
- Append durable local_resource_blocker_recheck evidence containing before/after root checks and emit a loop event.
- Leave typed blockers blocked when the probe is unhealthy and leave every untyped/manual blocker untouched.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go or one focused companion test

NON-SCOPE

- ddx-c4cbf253 persists the typed marker.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still chooses or biases concrete Fizeau routes in production even after removing the obvious run/try/work preflight. Local harness-name dispatch, lifecycle profile discovery, server defaults, project-model inheritance, and dead exported route-control APIs let DDx select harness/provider/model/profile policy it does not own. Removing preflight also left the advertised 60s route-resolution lease guard disconnected.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:90-135, cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:36-67, and cli/internal/agent/runner.go:84-114 route virtual/script names into DDx-local runners instead of forwarding an operator harness constraint to Fizeau.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:477-494,559-579 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:237-247 call ListPolicies/ListModels and select concrete lifecycle profiles.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:73-100, workers_supervisor.go:23-106, graphql/resolver_feat008.go:89-120, and the workers frontend seed default/smart profiles; server workers.go:363-372 and agent/run_dispatch_spec.go:103-127 leave opaque passthrough false, so config/resolved.go:73-80 can inject project agent.model into an unpinned request.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:6935-7533, service_run.go:461-636, cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:104-379, internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go, and model_discovery_containment.go retain exclusion, resolver, catalog-ladder, historical-route-seeding, and discovery-control capabilities.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:88-91,215-228 plus work/try/server flag plumbing retain route-resolution-timeout after deleting its resolver call, making the documented lease-release guard a no-op.

PROPOSED FIX
Route every production agent and lifecycle invocation through Fizeau. Preserve explicit operator Harness, Provider, Model, Policy/Profile, MaxPower, and other public passthrough byte-for-byte, but never synthesize concrete route fields. Express DDx-owned difficulty or escalation only through abstract MinPower. Remove local script/virtual name dispatch from production, lifecycle catalog/profile selection, default/smart profile injection, project-model inheritance for opaque execution entry points, route exclusion/catalog/seed machinery, and dead exported control APIs. Retain the 60s wedge guard by timing only Fizeau's opaque route stage from Execute dispatch until its first routing_decision event; on expiry cancel the operation, release the lease, and append operator_attention, and permanently disarm the timer once routing_decision arrives.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove read-only provider/model inventory or UI display of Fizeau-returned route evidence, change Fizeau internals, reject explicit operator passthrough, implement typed-result policy that requires a newer Fizeau release, or implement reviewer cardinality/stronger-review policy owned by dependent beads.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
None; Phase 1 WB-2a is start-immediately and this bead is the execution-boundary prerequisite for difficulty and review work.

Forward opaque harness/profile/model/provider/policy passthrough byte-for-byte (no agent->fiz alias rewrite or trimming). Abstract MinPower escalation must honor MaxPower and stop without dispatch when no strictly higher valid bound exists. Implement exact TestEscalationRaisesOnlyMinPowerWithoutCatalogLookup with whole-attempt tripwires for ListHarnesses, ListModels, ListPolicies, ResolveRoute, and profile selection; strengthen try delegation to assert command success.
