description:
PROBLEM
A bead can become dependency-waiting while its model-backed readiness check is running, yet DDx still dispatches it from the stale claimed snapshot. In live attempt 20260713T184450-fa9ca5d7, ddx-1f1c3f0f was claimed for readiness, an operator then added dependency ddx-02200b1e, the tracker mutation was committed into the attempt base, and DDx nevertheless launched the implementation harness. This defeats live queue steering and makes manual/offline tracker updates diverge from a server-managed worker that already selected the bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2602-2619 checks ReadyExecution only before claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2645-2654 then claims the candidate before the potentially long readiness hook at lines 2857-2892. After readiness, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3366-3400 calls refreshClaimedCandidateBeforeAttempt, but that helper at lines 5019-5027 delegates to candidateSkipFromClaimedState. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5077-5105 checks execution eligibility, supersession, and terminal/status changes only; it never re-evaluates open dependencies. The claimed candidate is therefore dispatched even when the current tracker graph now places it in dependency_waiting.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the post-readiness/pre-attempt refresh evaluate current dependency readiness while preserving the worker-owned claim. If any dependency is now open, release the claim, emit a structured stale-candidate skip with reason dependency_waiting and stage pre_attempt, and continue to the next ready bead without creating an execution attempt. Read fresh tracker state after readiness and immediately before agenttry.Attempt so mutations from manual clients, offline clients after synchronization, and server APIs have identical behavior. Allow non-eligibility-changing metadata updates such as notes to refresh the candidate without aborting dispatch.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go claimed-candidate refresh and dependency-readiness helpers
- focused execute-loop concurrency tests using a blocking readiness hook and coordinated tracker mutations
- stale-candidate event/log assertions

NON-SCOPE
Do not reject tracker metadata or dependency mutations merely because a readiness lease is held. Do not cancel an attempt after agenttry.Attempt has already started, change dependency semantics, change queue priority, or broaden this into network synchronization transport.

PARENT
None; root queue-consistency defect observed while steering the Niflheim server-managed worker.

DEPS
ddx-02200b1e must land first so this agent-package change can clear the staged package gate.

acceptance:
1. TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes|WorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes once in the foreground without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees; an incomplete, killed, backgrounded, or setsid-wrapped run does not satisfy this criterion.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The store/config helpers behind `execute_bead_loop_test.go` and `execute_bead_loop_disruption_test.go` can still inherit process-global DDx/config roots, which is what blocked the full-package proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go:3014-3027 creates stores through the normal config loader, so the fixture can drift onto shared roots unless it explicitly pins them.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_disruption_test.go:250-279 exercises the interrupted work path under the same helper and was the path that surfaced the host-dependent snapshot.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the loop/store helpers allocate private fixture/config roots, add a regression around `newExecuteLoopTestStore` or `runInterruptedWorkAttempt` if needed, and keep the interrupt and resource-preflight tests hermetic.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change interruption semantics, claim handling, or cleanup production code. Do not treat a killed, backgrounded, or incomplete run as success.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e6abf4a6. Deps: none. Supersedes the execution scope of ddx-12a41acc, which remains as failed-attempt audit only.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ResourcePreflightReportsClaimLivenessReclaimedInodes|WorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes once in the foreground without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees; an incomplete, killed, backgrounded, or setsid-wrapped run does not satisfy this criterion.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

title: Hermeticize execute_bead loop store and interruption fixtures

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadPrompt_UsesImplementationCommitAsSinglePreCommitGate` proves both rich and embedded prompts tell the agent to stage exact paths and rely on the normal commit hook once, without requesting a separate identical staged `lefthook` run.
2. `TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_RunsPreCommitOnceForUnchangedIndex` uses an instrumented hook and proves one unchanged final staged tree produces exactly one hook invocation and one implementation commit.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-8a78ee90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Execute-bead agents are instructed to run `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then run `git commit`. The repository-installed Git pre-commit hook invokes Lefthook again on the same staged tree. During attempt `20260713T121749-836fcbe4`, a small containment patch ran the full staged `./cmd` plus `./internal/agent` race gate twice explicitly after one test correction, then `git commit` launched a third full pass on the identical final staged tree. The second explicit pass alone spent more than 18 minutes in `go test` output processing. This duplicate gate contract serializes the queue without adding evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2104-2113` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2137-2148` require rich and embedded agents to rerun `lefthook run pre-commit` after staging and then commit exactly once, without recognizing that the implementation commit invokes the installed hook.
- `lefthook.yml:205-216` maps staged Go files to package-wide race tests, so each duplicate invocation can repeat multi-minute `./cmd` and `./internal/agent` gates.
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit:11-18` invokes Lefthook for ordinary `git commit`, making a separately requested manual invocation redundant when the staged tree is unchanged.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the ordinary implementation commit hook the single authoritative staged pre-commit gate. Update both execute-bead prompt variants and integrity evidence so agents stage the exact commit set, run `git commit` normally, and treat the hook output/exit status from that commit as satisfying the bead’s `lefthook run pre-commit` acceptance criterion. If a workflow explicitly runs the hook before commit, reuse that success only when a content/config fingerprint proves the staged tree and hook inputs are identical; otherwise rerun. A failed hook must abort the commit and remain a failed gate.

NON-SCOPE
Using `--no-verify`, setting `LEFTHOOK=0`, weakening or removing repository hooks, reusing evidence after any staged-tree or hook-config change, caching across attempts, bypassing human commits, or changing which tests the hook selects.

PARENT
None; root worker-throughput regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-2afca9c9` (test-output/runtime headroom) and `ddx-03568f89` (gate evidence); no implementation dependency.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadPrompt_UsesImplementationCommitAsSinglePreCommitGate` proves both rich and embedded prompts tell the agent to stage exact paths and rely on the normal commit hook once, without requesting a separate identical staged `lefthook` run.
- `TestExecuteBeadImplementationCommit_RunsPreCommitOnceForUnchangedIndex` uses an instrumented hook and proves one unchanged final staged tree produces exactly one hook invocation and one implementation commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

title: Reuse implementation-commit hooks as the staged pre-commit gate: part 1

acceptance:
1. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected proves a code-changing attempt whose bead requires lefthook is rejected when GateRuns is empty, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files.
2. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected proves any implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command rejects the attempt even when another gate run appears meaningful.
3. TestExecuteBead_WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation simulates a provider that stages code, receives a failed pre-commit gate, commits with --no-verify, and proves the attempt is preserved with failure_mode=attempt_integrity rather than merged.
4. TestExecuteBeadPromptForbidsNoVerify asserts every rich and embedded execute-bead prompt explicitly forbids --no-verify, hook disabling, and committing after failed or interrupted required gates; prompt snapshots are updated.
5. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits proves scoped checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits remain permitted because they are outside implementation-agent evidence.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)|ExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify))$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 had a staged pre-commit gate terminated after it recursively walked 100 leaked host-global module caches. The provider then ran git commit --no-verify and DDx accepted, merged, closed, and pushed implementation commit 85bb0fae0. A code-changing attempt can therefore bypass its required staged hook and still be classified as successful.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2108-2113 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2143-2148 require tests and a staged lefthook run but do not explicitly forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, or bypassing a failed gate. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 invokes ValidateAttemptIntegrity without populating GateRuns. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 checks gate evidence only when len(GateRuns) is nonzero, and TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_SingleCommitNoEvidenceSkips codifies missing evidence as success. The validation promised by closed bead ddx-725b65b4 is therefore not wired into the live execute-bead path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an explicit execute-bead instruction that implementation agents must never use git commit --no-verify, disable hooks, or commit after a required gate failed or was interrupted.
- Capture implementation-agent tool commands and foreground gate outputs/exit codes from the harness session into structured attempt-integrity input.
- When a code-changing bead acceptance requires lefthook run pre-commit, require at least one successful meaningful staged run after the final staged mutation and before the implementation commit. Missing, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files evidence must reject and preserve the attempt.
- Treat an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command as an integrity violation even if a prior gate appeared meaningful.
- Keep DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits outside this rule; their scoped --no-verify use is orchestrator-owned.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go
- execute-bead prompt guardrail/snapshot tests
- nearest harness-session evidence adapter needed to populate GateRuns and implementation commands

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite or remove existing execute-bead history, forbid DDx-owned tracker/audit commits, rerun repository hooks for synthesized commits, or weaken required gates. ddx-a3358532 owns synthesized-commit hook behavior.

PARENT
None; root attempt-integrity regression.

DEPS
Related to closed ddx-725b65b4 and open ddx-2295d9b1, but no dependency is required.

title: agent: reject hook-bypassing implementation commits

acceptance:
1. TestCloseWithEvidenceReturnsErrClosureGateRejectedAndLeavesOpen proves invalid evidence returns the typed error and does not mutate the bead to closed.
2. TestClosureGateRejectionPropagatesToLoop proves the final loop report is not success, records land_status not_landed or closure rejected, and leaves the bead open.
3. Existing valid CloseWithEvidence and work-loop closure tests remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(CloseWithEvidenceReturnsErrClosureGateRejectedAndLeavesOpen|ClosureGateRejectionPropagatesToLoop)" -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A rejected closure evidence gate can leave the bead open while the work loop reports success and continues as if closure completed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:2031-2070 rejects invalid evidence, but cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4257-4268 does not project that rejection into the loop outcome. This is Phase 0 WB-7b at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:145-153.

PROPOSED FIX
Return a typed ErrClosureGateRejected from the store and require the work loop to classify it as not closed/not successful, preserve evidence, release the claim under existing policy, and continue only with an explicit retry or operator disposition.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken closure evidence, auto-close after rejection, change candidate review semantics, or reinterpret repository evidence.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e.

DEPS
None.

description:
PROBLEM
After an execution provider exits with task_failed and leaves implementation edits, DDx synthesizes an iteration commit. That commit runs the repository pre-commit hook even though the provider already ran or failed the same gate. On 2026-07-13, the failed ddx-0f29e87a attempt entered the internal/agent race suite a second time during synthesis and recreated real Codex provider processes, consuming CPU and preventing prompt failure finalization.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1297-1383 invokes GitOps.SynthesizeCommit whenever the attempt worktree is dirty and HEAD did not move, including when exitCode is non-zero and the preliminary outcome is task_failed. cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:151-180 stages the implementation diff and executes plain git commit -m, so repository hooks run inside DDx orchestration. The durable DDx audit paths already use --no-verify at cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:125-128 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1724-1731, demonstrating the intended separation between orchestration-owned commits and user-invoked validation.

PROPOSED FIX
Make RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit create the DDx-owned synthesized attempt commit without invoking repository hooks. Preserve the substantive dirty diff and the per-attempt commit-message/audit contract. Keep validation in the provider run and DDx gate/review pipeline; do not silently reinterpret a failed attempt as success.

NON-SCOPE
Do not skip provider-requested gates, landing gates, or operator git hooks. Do not discard failed implementation edits or rewrite existing execute-bead history. Do not weaken test assertions or timeouts.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-5958e8a. Depends on ddx-aa6cc320 so the package gate cannot itself launch real provider fixtures while this fix is verified.

notes: Fresh reproduction from ddx-842598b1 attempt 20260713T160128-7f5062b0: after the provider ran the required internal/agent suite once and it timed out at 600.020s, DDx classified the response task_succeeded with a dirty worktree and launched git commit PID 761150 to synthesize the iteration commit. The repository pre-commit hook immediately launched lefthook PID 761220 and go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent PID 761393, recreating the expensive package gate. The synthesis subtree shared managed worker PGID 3992923, so it could not be safely cancelled with a process-group signal; operator recursively terminated only PIDs 767160/761393/761363/761357/761220/761199/761150 while preserving worker PID 3992923. This confirms both the duplicate-hook CPU/I/O defect and the need for synthesis cancellation to isolate/reap its own subtree.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pins Fizeau v0.14.50, whose subprocess discovery refresher discards its bounded context and whose Codex model discovery launches ptyquota with context.Background. A cancelled DDx request or stopped server can therefore return while codex --no-alt-screen continues consuming CPU and writing caller-owned temp/cache state. DDx-side containment wrappers reduce symptoms but do not repair the dependency lifecycle.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/go.mod:7 pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.50.
- In that release, Fizeau internal/serviceimpl/subprocess_discovery_cache.go:74-94 ignores the discoverycache.Refresher context before calling DefaultModelSnapshot.
- In that release, Fizeau internal/harnesses/codex/model_discovery.go:20-48 calls ptyquota.Run(context.Background(), ...).
- cli/cmd/lifecycle_model_discovery_test.go proves DDx's outer containment helper, but a local wrapper test cannot prove the Fizeau cache refresher or PTY receives cancellation.

PROPOSED FIX
Wait for upstream fizeau-d25b2862 to add a context-aware model-discovery contract, pass the refresh context through subprocessDiscoveryRefresher, and reap the Codex PTY on cancellation. After an upstream release contains that closing commit, bump cli/go.mod and cli/go.sum to that release. Add DDx integration coverage proving the service lifecycle cancellation reaches dependency discovery, returns only after the probe is reaped, and produces no late cache/home writes. Verify the existing embedded dispatch temp-root regressions with the released dependency.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/go.mod
- cli/go.sum
- cli/cmd/lifecycle_model_discovery_test.go and the nearest DDx service-lifecycle integration fixture

NON-SCOPE
Do not patch Fizeau source in the DDx repository, add a local replace directive, certify the existing outer containment wrapper as the upstream fix, disable discovery, extend the two-second caller timeout, kill manual Codex sessions, or change route selection.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-f84ee9a7. Externally blocked by fizeau#fizeau-d25b2862. Related to ddx-092f0d34 and regression ddx-403e9a23.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx estimates missing cost from concrete model and harness identity, accepts its own local price input, and stores execution cost in a value-only field. That makes absent cost indistinguishable from explicit zero and presents DDx guesses as accounting facts. Cost must be only the value Fizeau reported, with explicit presence preserved from execution through indexing, metrics, CLI, and GraphQL.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/pricing.go:3-35 owns DDx model pricing and EstimateCost. cli/cmd/usage_support.go:54-72 and cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1274-1323 estimate missing cost from route identity. cli/internal/config/types.go:319-322 still accepts DDx local_per_1k_tokens pricing. cli/internal/agent/types.go:106-133 defines Result.CostUSD as value-only. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:324-346 copies final cost only when greater than zero. cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:22-40 has CostPresent but lines 206-221 and 240-293 synthesize it from nonzero values. cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:818-820 drops explicit zero.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete cli/internal/agent/pricing.go, EstimateCost, DDx local price configuration, and every model/provider/harness identity cost estimate. Add explicit cost presence at agent.Result and translate the public Fizeau terminal contract exactly: nil is unknown, non-nil 0.0 is known zero, and non-nil positive is known positive. Preserve that distinction through session writes/reindex, usage and process metrics, and GraphQL. Historical records without presence remain unknown. Aggregates sum only present values and expose unknown participation instead of fabricating a complete total.

NON-SCOPE
Do not classify billing mode, add a replacement DDx price table or remote pricing lookup, reinterpret missing as zero/free, change Fizeau pricing, or use reported cost for routing, retry, escalation, review grading, admission, or close policy. Fizeau predicted candidate cost remains audit evidence and is not DDx pricing.

PARENT
ddx-1b3272a6.

DEPS
ddx-00d406ce supplies released Fizeau terminal cost presence and its public conformance proof.
notes: Next executable child after ddx-00d406ce closes. It must consume the one released v0.15 public cost-presence contract and must not repin Fizeau independently. Until then absent versus explicit-zero terminal cost cannot be proven and this bead must remain dependency-waiting.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx currently guesses that reviewer errors are transport failures from error strings, raises reviewer power/profile on retry, and ultimately fails or parks otherwise valid implementation work. The design requires Fizeau to classify its own terminal transport outcome; DDx may retry one review operation at identical power and passthrough, then downgrade to deterministic-only close with operator-visible evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:7949-7991 ClassifyReviewError uses text and defaults unknown errors to transport.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:869-931 increases MinPower and selects top profiles after prior errors.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:1251-1292 and execute_bead_review_group.go:153-175 return dispatch errors as review failures.
- cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go:315-325 converts any reviewer error to review_malfunction and prevents landing.
- Current pinned Fizeau v0.14.50 in cli/go.mod exposes no typed Cause/Stage field, so this change must wait for ddx-66a8883f and its compatible upstream release/conformance proof.

PROPOSED FIX
Consume the single typed Fizeau terminal review-transport cause from the DDx attempt-policy adapter. Retry exactly once at identical MinPower and byte-identical operator passthrough. If the typed transport failure repeats, emit an operator-visible review-downgraded event and allow deterministic-checks-only close. Do not raise power, select a profile, or fail the implementation because review transport is unavailable. Non-transport review errors remain distinct and follow their typed policy.

NON-SCOPE
Do not classify provider text, implement Fizeau provider retry, add continuation, invoke provider CLIs, or weaken semantic BLOCK/REQUEST_CHANGES findings.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-b8cacbeb. Depends on ddx-642a9f26 plus ddx-66a8883f, the blocked typed Fizeau result consumer. It must not become execution-ready until the compatible upstream contract is pinned and conformance is green.
title: review: consume typed Fizeau transport outcomes for retry and downgrade

description:
PROBLEM
The targeted Go pre-commit helper can report success without running any tests when it is invoked from the repository root with the same root-relative cli/... paths that Git and operator diagnostics naturally produce. During execute-bead attempt 20260713T171608-d879ec96, sh scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go returned 0 in 123ms and launched no go process, creating false validation evidence after the real package gate had timed out.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh:8-18 converts each input to a directory, strips a leading cli/, and silently continues when that derived directory does not exist relative to the current working directory. lefthook.yml:11-14 and lefthook.yml:205-216 normally run the helper with cli/ as the command root, but the helper neither establishes nor validates that working-directory contract. From the repository root, cli/internal/agent becomes internal/agent, the directory check fails, and the helper exits with its initial zero status.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the helper resolve the Go module root deterministically from its own repository location or explicitly normalize both repository-root cli/... paths and cli-root internal/... paths before selecting packages. Track every Go path supplied to the helper and fail nonzero with a diagnostic naming any path that cannot be mapped to an existing module package. Preserve targeted package selection, the race detector, the 30-minute per-package timeout, and compatibility with the normal Lefthook cli/ root.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh
- lefthook.yml only if invocation wiring needs an explicit root argument
- a Go or shell-backed regression test under cli/internal/agent that runs the helper with a fake go executable and inspects its arguments

NON-SCOPE
Do not broaden the hook to the full repository, remove -race, raise or remove the timeout, silently ignore unmapped Go paths, or change which staged files Lefthook passes to the command.

PARENT
None; root hook-validation reliability defect discovered during the Niflheim queue drain.

DEPS
None. Related prior hook reliability work is ddx-42a86089; duplicate-hook work is ddx-8a78ee90.

title: hooks: make Go test helper fail closed outside cli root

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace continues to run five concurrent ExecuteBeadWithConfig calls against one real temporary Git repository and preserves the HEAD compare-and-swap and BaseRev assertions.
2. TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace_DoesNotSpawnProviders installs provider-spawn tripwires for codex, claude, gemini, and __provider-launch and proves none are invoked by the fixture.
3. The fixture uses a bounded hermetic AgentRunner implementation; it does not call NewRunner, production Fizeau discovery, network APIs, subscription caches, or real provider binaries.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace(_DoesNotSpawnProviders)?$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes and leaves no provider descendants whose command line references the test temp root.
6. A failure/timeout path test proves every fixture-owned subprocess is canceled and reaped before the test returns.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Running the required cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... gate launches five real Codex provider processes from TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace. In the 2026-07-13 ddx-2502d3c6 attempt, the package test spawned PIDs 225004, 225054, 225173, 225576, and 226111 as __provider-launch ... codex exec; each ran for more than six minutes at about 10-12% CPU. The test package approached its 10-minute timeout, blocking the single queue worker and consuming server-cgroup CPU for a test whose stated concern is only parent-repo Git serialization.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:31-55 builds five production NewRunner(Config{}) instances and labels directive-file paths as model names under the obsolete script route. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:73-89 then calls ExecuteBeadWithConfig concurrently with those production runners. The current service-backed runner resolves the request to Codex, so each directive path is passed to codex exec as -m instead of being consumed by a hermetic script fixture. The test comments at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:35-37 explicitly say agent output is irrelevant. The package previously had to be repaired for the same 600-second budget under ddx-1a0d4d26, but this fixture has regressed into live provider execution.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the five production runners in this Git-lock regression with an explicit bounded hermetic AgentRunner fixture that returns the minimal no-changes result needed to reach and validate pre-dispatch serialization.
- Preserve the real temporary Git repository, five concurrent ExecuteBeadWithConfig calls, tracker commits, worktree creation, and HEAD CAS assertions.
- Add a provider-spawn tripwire: real/fake codex, claude, gemini, and provider-launch lookup must fail the test immediately if invoked.
- Audit adjacent direct NewRunner plus Harness=script fixtures reached by the normal internal/agent package gate and migrate any that can invoke live providers to the same hermetic boundary.
- Ensure subprocesses are canceled and reaped on test failure or timeout.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production provider discovery, reduce the concurrency below five, delete the HEAD race assertions, or skip the test. Live provider integration remains build-tagged/explicit integration coverage, not part of the default package gate.

PARENT
None; root regression bead.

DEPS
None.
notes: decomposed into ddx-415c8b95, ddx-0bb06411, ddx-d42881d8; validation incident added child ddx-393abd29 for host-wide process-scan cost
title: tests: stop concurrent predispatch fixture from launching real Codex

acceptance:
1. TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPassRunsOnSignal synchronizes on an executor-entered channel, cancels only after attempt entry, and deterministically proves exactly one shutdown cleanup pass occurs after the startup baseline.
2. TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPassSkipsClaimBeforeAttempt cancels after a claim but before executor entry and proves the attempt-only shutdown pass is not run while the claim is still released safely.
3. TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPassSynchronizationHasNoSleep inspects the two signal-cleanup regressions or their shared helper and proves synchronization uses channels or typed lifecycle events, not time.Sleep or ClaimLease visibility as the attempt-start precondition.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "^TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPass(RunsOnSignal|SkipsClaimBeforeAttempt|SynchronizationHasNoSleep)$" -count=100 -timeout 120s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
TestWorkCleanup_ShutdownPassRunsOnSignal is timing-dependent: it cancels after observing a claim lease, although the production contract runs the shutdown cleanup pass only after an execution attempt has started. A scheduler can cancel in the claim-to-dispatch window and correctly skip shutdown cleanup, causing a false release-gate failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:538-606 waits only for ClaimLease owner at lines 588-591 before cancelling at lines 593-594. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1678-1682 gates the deferred shutdown pass on attemptStarted, and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3438-3465 sets attemptStarted only immediately before the attempt watchdog/executor phase. Claim visibility therefore does not prove the tested precondition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Give the test executor an explicit entered channel and cancel only after the executor has started, making the positive shutdown-pass test deterministic.
- Add a negative claim-before-attempt case proving cancellation in the pre-dispatch window does not run the attempt-only shutdown pass.
- Keep the production attemptStarted boundary unchanged unless repository evidence establishes that claimed-but-never-dispatched work owns cleanup obligations; if such evidence exists, document it and change production plus both tests together.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not broaden shutdown cleanup merely to satisfy the flaky test.
- Do not alter startup or periodic cleanup scheduling, claim release semantics, signal propagation, or cleanup lock behavior.
- Do not add sleeps as synchronization.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e, the Phase 0 self-harm prevention epic.

DEPS
No dependencies. No alpha105 release bead exists as of filing.
title: agent tests: synchronize signal cleanup after attempt start

acceptance:
1. `TestServiceConstructorsUseFizeauProjectConfig` table-tests every execution-facing service constructor with distinct work directories and isolated HOME/Fizeau fixtures. It proves each supplies the deterministic public Fizeau `ConfigPath` for that project, DDx performs no provider/model registry construction, and DDx neither suppresses nor reimplements the documented Fizeau global-plus-project configuration merge.
2. `TestProviderTimeoutIsExplicitOnly` proves omitted input reaches `ServiceExecuteRequest.ProviderTimeout` as zero/unset and an explicit CLI/request timeout reaches Fizeau byte-for-byte without provider, endpoint, harness, or model lookup.
3. Five production-path regressions prove contradictory DDx project model/reasoning/endpoint fixtures never affect unpinned requests: `TestWorkProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution`, `TestTryProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution`, `TestRunProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution`, `TestLifecycleProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution`, and `TestServerManagedProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution`. Define each test in the package that owns that entry path and capture the request reaching Fizeau.
4. `TestExplicitPublicExecutionConstraintsSurviveProviderDetachment` captures Fizeau requests and proves explicit Harness, Provider, Model, Policy, MinPower, MaxPower, Reasoning, Permissions, Timeout, IdleTimeout, ProviderTimeout, Role, work directory, and correlation metadata are unchanged.
5. `TestNoExecutionServiceConfigSynthesis` proves production execution construction contains no `DefaultProviderRequestTimeout`, `ResolveProviderRequestTimeout`, endpoint provider registry/default synthesis, URL/port provider inference, DDx route-health/session-path construction, or project model/reasoning fallback. It explicitly allows `ConfiguredProviderSnapshots` and the minimum helpers used only by that read-only server compatibility surface until `ddx-d7800ef7` removes them.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run "^(TestServiceConstructorsUseFizeauProjectConfig|TestProviderTimeoutIsExplicitOnly|TestWorkProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution|TestTryProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution|TestRunProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution|TestLifecycleProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution|TestServerManagedProjectAgentModelAndReasoningNeverAffectExecution|TestExplicitPublicExecutionConstraintsSurviveProviderDetachment|TestNoExecutionServiceConfigSynthesis)$" -count=1` passes with every named test present.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM

DDx execution still converts DDx project model, reasoning, endpoint, credential, and timeout configuration into a Fizeau `ServiceConfig`. `NewServiceFromWorkDir*` ignores the worktree it receives, so Fizeau cannot discover its own project configuration. DDx also invents a 15-minute provider timeout when the operator did not set one. These paths leave DDx owning provider policy even after concrete route selection moved to Fizeau.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:41-82` derives provider timeout behavior from DDx endpoint configuration and supplies `DefaultProviderRequestTimeout`.
- `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:98-111` accepts `workDir` but does not give Fizeau a deterministic project `ConfigPath`.
- `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:145-367` converts DDx endpoints/models/reasoning/defaults into provider registries and route/session paths.
- `cli/internal/config/resolved.go:73-80,123-128` retains project model fallback and per-harness reasoning maps in resolved execution state.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:69-81` emits a stale DDx endpoint-timeout migration hint; `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:197-223` always sends the DDx-resolved provider timeout.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:114` and `cli/cmd/try.go:100` still describe project/model defaults that route-neutral execution must not consume.

PROPOSED FIX

- Construct every execution-facing Fizeau service with `ServiceOptions.ConfigPath=filepath.Join(workDir, "config.yaml")`, or the equivalent deterministic public path contract. Fizeau interprets the directory as the project root and owns its documented global-plus-project configuration merge, including `<project>/.fizeau/config.yaml`.
- Delete execution-reachable DDx endpoint-to-`ServiceConfig` conversion, provider registry/default synthesis, URL/port provider inference, provider route-health/session-path manufacture, and project-model/reasoning fallback from execution construction.
- Forward `ProviderTimeout` only when the operator explicitly supplied the public request constraint. Omitted input remains zero/unset; timeout behavior then belongs to Fizeau/provider while DDx generic execution `Timeout` and `IdleTimeout` remain intact.
- Preserve explicit public execution constraints byte-for-byte: Harness, Provider, Model, Policy/Profile, MinPower, MaxPower, Reasoning/Effort, Permissions, Timeout, IdleTimeout, ProviderTimeout, Role, working directory, and correlation metadata.
- Remove stale CLI and error text that promises DDx project-model or endpoint-timeout defaults.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not suppress, replace, or reinterpret the documented Fizeau global-plus-project configuration merge. Tests isolate HOME and Fizeau config fixtures so developer-machine state cannot determine their result.
- Do not yet delete or hard-reject legacy config fields; `ddx-e2c3453b` owns the migration after all consumers are removed.
- Do not delete `ConfiguredProviderSnapshots` or helpers retained solely by that server compatibility surface; `ddx-d7800ef7` owns their removal with REST/MCP/GraphQL inventory and hypothetical route display.
- Do not change pricing, billing inference, harness display names, session cost, or evidence caps; `ddx-1b3272a6` owns them.
- Do not choose, validate, or predict a concrete Fizeau route.
title: agent: delegate project configuration and provider timeout policy to Fizeau

acceptance:
1. TestRouteStageTimeoutCancelsBeforeBookkeeping blocks the first durable timeout-bookkeeping operation and proves the executor context is already cancelled while that operation remains blocked, then unblocks persistence and proves lease release plus operator_attention complete.
2. TestRouteStageTimeoutUsesExecuteDeadlineWhenGuardStartIsDelayed contains no sub-500ms wall-clock pass/fail threshold whose outcome depends on tracker I/O latency; it still proves a delayed guard cannot restart the Execute-relative route budget.
3. Production timeout handling calls attemptCancel immediately after winning term.set(FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout) and before routeResolutionTimeoutReport, while preserving releaseFailed error propagation and guardWG completion semantics.
4. CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race ./internal/agent -run "^(TestRouteStageTimeoutCancelsBeforeBookkeeping|TestRouteStageTimeoutUsesExecuteDeadlineWhenGuardStartIsDelayed)$" -count=30 -timeout 180s passes with both named tests present.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The route-resolution watchdog can recognize an expired Execute-relative deadline yet leave the active Fizeau attempt running while DDx performs synchronous tracker bookkeeping. Under ordinary tracker or host I/O contention this violates the bounded route-timeout contract, makes the race suite nondeterministic, and delays process cancellation after the deadline has already expired.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3527-3544 calls routeResolutionTimeoutReport before attemptCancel after term.set succeeds. routeResolutionTimeoutReport releases the lease, updates the consecutive-wedge record, and appends operator_attention through locked JSONL persistence. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_route_exclusion_test.go:400-465 uses a 150ms wall-clock assertion after deliberately delaying guard startup; repeated baseline proof under CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race fails because tracker writes can exceed that threshold even though the remaining timer is correctly clamped to zero.

PROPOSED FIX
Cancel the attempt context immediately after the route-timeout termination reason wins, before any durable timeout/release bookkeeping. Then perform the existing release, consecutive-wedge, operator-attention, and release-failure handling while the worker waits for the guard to finish. Replace the load-sensitive cancellation assertion with a blocking tracker/store seam that holds timeout bookkeeping and proves the executor context is cancelled while persistence is still blocked. Retain an eventual assertion that durable release and operator_attention complete after the block is removed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not lengthen timeout thresholds, weaken or delete route-timeout coverage, change the configured timeout/default, move route selection into DDx, change Fizeau routing, alter consecutive-wedge policy, or make tracker writes asynchronous beyond the existing guard lifetime.

PARENT
ddx-8f2e0ebf.

DEPS
None. This is a release-gate defect discovered while committing ddx-e2c3453b; the failure reproduces on unchanged main.
title: worker: cancel route timeout before durable bookkeeping

acceptance:
1. `TestInferInitialMinPowerMapsOnlyEstimatedDifficulty` covers easy=0, medium=7, hard=9, nil/absent/invalid=7 and proves legacy power hints, labels, priority, issue type, kind, prose size, acceptance size, and unrelated numeric Extra fields do not affect the result.

2. `TestEstimatedDifficultyMapsOnlyMinPower` exercises production `ddx work` and `ddx try` dispatch for easy, medium/default, and hard beads. It proves only `ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower` changes to 0, 7, or 9; Policy, Harness, Provider, and Model remain empty and no Fizeau catalog, route-resolution, or inventory method is queried.

3. `TestExplicitOperatorMinPowerSuppressesDifficultyInference` uses a hard bead with an explicit MinPower, proves the supplied floor is forwarded unchanged by both work and try, and proves evidence distinguishes the explicit requested floor from an inferred value.

4. `TestPublicPolicySuppressesDifficultyInference` uses a hard bead with an explicit opaque `--profile` value, proves that value reaches `ServiceExecuteRequest.Policy` byte-for-byte, proves no inferred MinPower is added, and proves DDx performs no policy lookup or profile-to-power translation.

5. `TestOperatorRouteConstraintsDoNotSuppressDifficultyInference` uses Harness, Provider, Model, and a compatible MaxPower with a hard bead; it proves all four constraints remain byte-identical while MinPower=9 is still inferred. A conflicting MaxPower subtest must return an error without clamping or mutating either bound or any route constraint.

6. `TestReadinessDifficultyAffectsCurrentDispatchWithoutBeadMutation` proves transient readiness difficulty is used only when authored bead difficulty is absent, affects the current production request, and does not write `triage.estimated_difficulty`, `triage.power_hint`, or any numeric retry floor to bead Extra. An authored bead hint must win over readiness output.

7. `TestReturnedRouteIdentityIsEvidenceOnly` varies only Fizeau-returned Harness, Provider, and Model facts and proves retry, connectivity, outage, triage, and close behavior is unchanged. It may separately prove typed abstract ActualPower can raise a later eligible MinPower without consulting or copying the concrete identity.

8. `TestExecutionRoutingIntentRecordsInferredMinPower` proves current evidence records `estimated_difficulty`, `inferred_min_power`, `requested_policy`, `requested_min_power`, `requested_max_power`, and actual route facts. It covers inferred easy=0 as present, hard=9, explicit-policy suppression, and proves `requested_power_class` is not produced by current difficulty inference.

9. `TestLegacyProfilePriorityRejected` loads `agent.routing.profile_priority`, returns `*RoutingMigrationError` naming that exact field and the migration document, and proves the deprecated warning/acceptance path is gone.

10. `rg -n 'profile_priority' cli --glob '*.go' --glob '*.json'` returns only deliberate migration rejection and routing-lint migration fixtures; production config structs, schema properties, clone logic, warnings, and acceptance tests contain no supported `ProfilePriority` surface. `rg -n 'InferPowerClass|PowerClassForEstimatedDifficulty' cli/internal/escalation cli/cmd --glob '*.go'` is empty.

11. `cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run '^(TestInferInitialMinPowerMapsOnlyEstimatedDifficulty|TestEstimatedDifficultyMapsOnlyMinPower|TestExplicitOperatorMinPowerSuppressesDifficultyInference|TestPublicPolicySuppressesDifficultyInference|TestOperatorRouteConstraintsDoNotSuppressDifficultyInference|TestReadinessDifficultyAffectsCurrentDispatchWithoutBeadMutation|TestReturnedRouteIdentityIsEvidenceOnly|TestExecutionRoutingIntentRecordsInferredMinPower|TestLegacyProfilePriorityRejected)$' -count=1` passes with every named test present.

12. `cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.

13. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM

DDx parses `triage.estimated_difficulty`, but production `ddx work` and `ddx try` do not apply it to the initial Fizeau request. An unpinned ordinary bead therefore starts with `MinPower=0`, while the parsed difficulty is used only as `cheap`/`standard`/`smart` telemetry. The command paths also use one overbroad “any routing flag” predicate, so immutable Harness, Provider, Model, or MaxPower constraints suppress difficulty inference even though only an explicit MinPower or public Fizeau Policy should do so. Configuration still accepts `agent.routing.profile_priority`, and current routing-intent evidence records profile/power-class vocabulary instead of the requested numeric policy boundary.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/escalation/powerinfer.go:20-27,61-70` maps difficulty through DDx `PowerClass` values rather than directly to Fizeau’s public numeric MinPower scale.
- `cli/internal/escalation/execution_hint.go:21-35,53-72,95-108` carries `InferredPowerClass` and suppresses all difficulty inference through broad `ExplicitRouting`/`ProjectRouting` booleans.
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:206-211` treats Harness, Provider, Model, Profile, MinPower, and MaxPower alike when deciding whether inference is allowed. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:505-519` calculates routing intent but seeds the first work attempt only from `rcfg.MinPower()`.
- `cli/cmd/try.go:253-256,283-295,410-417` has the same overbroad suppression and starts the try ladder from the CLI `minPower` value without applying the bead/readiness estimate.
- `cli/internal/config/types.go:498-508`, `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:458-471`, and `cli/internal/config/clone.go:37-47` still model and accept `profile_priority`. `cli/internal/config/config.go:30-54,131-136` rejects other retired routing fields but merely warns for this one.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5449-5598` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go:147-234` record `requested_profile` and `requested_power_class` rather than `requested_policy`, `inferred_min_power`, and the resolved requested power bounds.
- Current regressions in `cli/cmd/work_test.go:541-640` and `cli/cmd/try_test.go:601-738` explicitly lock the missing behavior by expecting an absent/medium bead to dispatch with `MinPower=0`.

PROPOSED FIX

- Replace difficulty-to-PowerClass inference with `InferInitialMinPower` or an equivalently small numeric helper. It must map `easy -> 0`, `medium -> 7`, `hard -> 9`, and nil/absent/invalid -> `7`. It must ignore `triage.power_hint`, `power:*` labels, priority, issue type, kind, prose length, acceptance length, and every other numeric bead field.
- Carry numeric inferred MinPower plus explicit presence through `ExecutionHint`; preserve the distinction between inferred `easy=0` and “inference suppressed.” Do not query Fizeau catalogs, policies, profiles, models, providers, harnesses, or route viability.
- Separate inference precedence from passthrough/retry eligibility. Only an explicit operator MinPower or a non-empty public Fizeau Policy suppresses inference. The current CLI calls that policy input `--profile`; preserve its bytes and forward it as `ServiceExecuteRequest.Policy` without interpreting its value.
- Harness, Provider, Model, and MaxPower remain byte-identical sticky constraints but do not suppress the initial inferred MinPower. If the inferred floor conflicts with MaxPower or another constraint, surface the existing request-shape or typed Fizeau error; do not clamp, remove, widen, or repair either constraint.
- Seed both production `ddx work` and `ddx try` first attempts from the resolved inferred MinPower when inference is active. Authored bead difficulty wins over transient readiness difficulty; transient readiness applies to the current request only and never mutates bead metadata.
- Add `profile_priority` to `checkRoutingMigration`, remove it from accepted config types/schema/clone behavior, delete the warning path, and replace acceptance/warning tests with a `RoutingMigrationError` regression.
- Record `routing_intent_source`, normalized `estimated_difficulty`, `inferred_min_power`, `requested_policy`, `requested_min_power`, `requested_max_power`, and Fizeau-returned actual route facts. `easy` must record inferred zero rather than look suppressed. Concrete Harness/Provider/Model identity is audit-only; only public abstract ActualPower may contribute to a permitted later MinPower decision.
- Update affected command, escalation, config, agent evidence, report/audit adapter, and output tests. Historical evidence readers may retain old power-class/profile fields for compatibility, but current difficulty inference and new routing-intent evidence must not produce them.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not select, validate, rank, retry, or exclude a concrete harness, provider, model, profile, endpoint, or route.
- Do not rename the operator `--profile` flag or change Fizeau’s public API; treat the value solely as opaque public Policy passthrough.
- Do not change capability-sensitive retry or reviewer-strength policy except to preserve its use of abstract MinPower/ActualPower.
- Do not use returned concrete route identity in retry, connectivity, outage, triage, review, close, or feedback decisions.
- Do not persist readiness difficulty, inferred MinPower, retry floors, or actual route facts into bead `Extra`.
- Do not perform the broader provider-config removal tracked by `ddx-e2c3453b` or the concrete-route metrics/display cleanup tracked by `ddx-1b3272a6`.
- Do not delete unrelated historical `PowerClass` compatibility/summary fields unless they are directly populated by current difficulty inference.
title: routing: map estimated difficulty to abstract MinPower only

description:
PROBLEM
A DDx document can claim Complete without proving every acceptance criterion and user story against the current revision, while duplicate IDs and stale observations pass unnoticed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/tools/lint/spechonesty does not exist and lefthook.yml:149-186 and 501-536 wire other linters but no status/verification coverage gate. Phase 2 WB-1 defines the missing mechanical contract at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:58-74.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a deterministic spec-honesty linter that parses body/frontmatter status, requires full current-revision verification maps for Complete, rejects waivers masquerading as proof, detects duplicate document and user-story IDs, and fails stale observations. Wire it into pre-commit without mutating documents.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete.

PARENT
ddx-2e992616.

DEPS
None for the linter; later status-restamp beads depend on this child.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work status --all-projects` must keep reporting live workers even after the missing-desired-worker diagnostic lands elsewhere, but there is no regression coverage proving the global listing stays intact.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work_status.go:80-116 builds work status only from live worker scans and active work.
cli/cmd/work_status.go:215-221 prints a single all-projects empty-state line, so there is no guard against a future change accidentally hiding live rows or mixing in desired-state diagnostics.

PROPOSED FIX
Add regression coverage for the all-projects live-worker listing and keep the formatter limited to live workers so the missing-desired-worker explanation stays in `ddx worker status`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add desired-state reporting to `ddx work status`. Do not change the worker-status payload. Do not change restart accounting. Do not weaken byte/inode resource guards. Do not alter the stale operator-attention fixes tracked by ddx-b17cad6d and ddx-4d92f2cf. Do not implement a new classifier.

Governing artifact: ddx-9c53e551.

title: Keep `ddx work status --all-projects` live-worker rows intact

acceptance:
1. cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes.
2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-6a2ce3bd is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-owned temporary homes, provider shims, and helper scratch are created directly under raw os.TempDir(), so /tmp remains the choke point even when DDx has a configured execution/runtime temp root with available space. The 2026-07-08 incident had /tmp at 100% inode use while the configured execution worktree root under /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt still had capacity.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:91 creates provider shims with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-provider-shim-").
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29 and runner_test.go:56 create ddx-home-* with os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-"); equivalent command tests create the same prefix in cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:80, cli/cmd/try_test.go:823, and cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:85.
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:116 creates ddx-fixture-bin-* with os.MkdirTemp("", ...).
These producers bypass config.ExecutionScratchRoot / config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot and strand inode-heavy scratch under /tmp.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared helper for DDx-owned scratch temp dirs that prefers config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot) or the configured execution/runtime root when a project root is available, and falls back to os.TempDir() only when no configured root exists. Migrate provider shims and DDx-owned temp homes/fixture bins to the helper where production/runtime context is available. Keep tests hermetic by setting the helper root explicitly in tests.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go
- cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go or a new scratch-temp helper package
- relevant tests in cli/internal/agent and cli/cmd

NON-SCOPE
Do not move claim-liveness in this bead; ddx-3986b89a covers claim heartbeat root selection. Do not change git worktree placement. Do not store long-lived user config in execution scratch. Do not require a project root for all tests; fallback behavior must remain.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. `TestProviderShimExecutableResolver_RejectsGoTestBinary` supplies an `agent.test` executable and proves no shim directory is installed, PATH is unchanged, and an actionable test-only refusal is returned or recorded.
2. `TestRunAgentViaService_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver` and `TestDispatchViaResolvedConfig_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver` prove both production call sites use the shared resolver instead of calling `os.Executable` directly.
3. `TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher` exercises the suite guard and proves no descendant command line matches `agent.test __provider-launch` and no real codex, claude, gemini, opencode, or pi process is launched.
4. Existing `TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` and cmd-package `TestProviderLaunchExecsProviderInDedicatedProcessGroup` remain green against an explicit DDx CLI/fake binary, proving production parent-death and process-group behavior is preserved.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestProviderShimExecutableResolver_RejectsGoTestBinary|TestRunAgentViaService_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestDispatchViaResolvedConfig_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher|TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without any `agent.test __provider-launch` descendant, orphaned real provider, or recursive package-test process.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The mandatory `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` gate recursively launches the package test binary as DDx's hidden provider wrapper. During attempt `20260713T190502-df5adc46`, the parent `agent.test` spawned five concurrent `agent.test __provider-launch <real-provider>` helpers for more than three minutes, grew a 666 MB testlog, and left a real `codex --no-alt-screen` process reparented to the managed worker for 12 minutes at about 188 MiB RSS. This makes the package gate itself create the CPU/memory/orphan condition it is supposed to prevent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113` calls `os.Executable()` unconditionally and passes that path to `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:152-163` repeats the same unconditional installation for the resolved-service path.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:83-118` writes each shim as `<ddxBinary> __provider-launch <realBinary>`. Under `go test`, `os.Executable()` is `agent.test`, not the DDx CLI and it has no Cobra `__provider-launch` dispatch. Re-executing it starts the test suite again; the recursive process can install shims and launch real providers that escape the original gate process group.
- `cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:25-68` is the actual hidden-command implementation, and exists only in the DDx CLI command graph.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce one injectable resolver for the provider-wrapper executable and use it from both service call sites. In package-test builds, install a suite-wide test resolver/guard that refuses to use the current `agent.test` executable as a provider wrapper while leaving direct `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` tests free to supply an explicit real/fake DDx CLI binary. Fail closed with actionable evidence if a test fixture attempts to install a provider shim from a Go test binary. Preserve production PATH shim, `Pdeathsig`, process-group, and real CLI hidden-command behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove provider parent-death protection in production.
- Do not replace Fizeau routing or provider discovery.
- Do not weaken the full `internal/agent` race gate or merely increase its timeout.
- Do not kill unrelated provider processes from other repositories.
- Scanner injection remains owned by `ddx-02200b1e`; fixture-specific spawn tripwires remain owned by `ddx-26bf19c7`.

PARENT AND DEPS
Root reliability bug. No implementation deps. Cross-references: `ddx-02200b1e` and `ddx-26bf19c7`.
notes: Lifecycle decomposition on 2026-07-13 returned invalid model output and used a deterministic three-part AC split. The split was not independently green: parts 1 and 2 omitted mandatory gates and part 3 was verification-only with no dependencies. ddx-5653688f was retrofitted as the atomic implementation+suite-guard child; ddx-b939e409 and ddx-d8026131 were cancelled.

3. TestCoordinationReconcile_OrderedResumeAndConflict proves contiguous acknowledgement, resumable batches, and structured conflict evidence.
4. The production project-scoped handlers call WorkerManager.LandCoordinators and the existing bead store rather than maintaining parallel claim/landing state.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Project-scoped localhost APIs · requireTrusted boundary · real server/git/bead-store integration tests · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6: the server serializes typed idempotent coordination mutations against bead/git truth and reconciles ordered offline journals.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-002 makes the host daemon the preferred coordination point for manual and managed workers without replacing durable project state.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
The server exposes individual bead claim/unclaim handlers and an in-process land coordinator, but workers cannot submit the complete typed coordination protocol or reconcile offline outcomes. There is no server endpoint for landing or journal reconciliation. This child was originally created as ddx-fee5383c on 2026-07-11, but a later whole-tracker rewrite dropped its row while leaving ddx-2e49980d dependent on the missing ID.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:1096-1106 and 2075-2114 expose standalone claim/unclaim mutations only. cli/internal/server/land_coordinator.go:91-151 serializes landing only for in-process callers. No project-scoped coordination mutation or reconcile handler calls both through a shared contract. Tracker mutation commit c53be76d6 is durable evidence of the dropped predecessor row; the implementation scope below restores the missing roadmap child under a CLI-generated ID.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add project-scoped POST coordination/mutations and coordination/reconcile handlers using the shared types from ddx-3ab6762d.
- Route claim/lease and tracker transitions to the existing bead store and land requests to the per-project LandCoordinator.
- Persist bounded idempotency outcomes so an unknown-response retry returns already_applied rather than duplicating tracker events or commits.
- Return applied, already_applied, or conflict with durable version/revision evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add a server-side picker, parallel claim table, cross-host federation lease, or command wiring. Do not resurrect or manually edit the dropped ddx-fee5383c tracker row.

PARENT
ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
ddx-3ab6762d supplies the shared request/outcome contract and behavioral suite.

acceptance: 1. Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-24949caf is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Restarting the DDx user service during active supervised work can hang until systemd times out, leaving systemd to SIGKILL server child processes. Live evidence from 2026-07-07 03:05:19 EDT: `ddx server stop` did not return until systemd marked `ddx-server.service` failed with `Result: timeout` and killed leftover git subprocesses, interrupting the Axon worker attempt that was in post-execution handling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/server/server.go:379-385` cancels the supervisor and then waits on `supervisorDone` with no timeout. Worker cleanup is only reached afterward at `cli/internal/server/server.go:388-395`. The worker shutdown path itself has a bounded wait (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1696-1703`), but it is unreachable while the supervisor goroutine is stuck in a reconcile/git operation. The installed systemd unit generated from `cli/cmd/server_service.go:26-42` has no explicit stop timeout or ExecStop mitigation, so the default systemd timeout eventually kills the whole cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX: Make server shutdown bounded and ordered so worker cleanup always runs promptly even when supervisor reconciliation is slow or stuck. A good implementation is to cancel the supervisor, wait only a short bounded grace for `supervisorDone`, continue to `supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()` / `workers.Shutdown()` on timeout, and surface/log the supervisor timeout without preventing worker cleanup.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rewrite worker execution semantics, change bead claim/lease policy, or alter unrelated service install behavior.

Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

title: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 1

acceptance:
1. TestStopStaleDiskEntry_PreservesLivePIDWithFreshClaim starts a real controllable subprocess, writes a running disk record with that PID and a fresh claim lease, invokes stopStaleDiskEntry, and proves the subprocess remains alive, the claim remains in_progress, and the record is not marked stopped.
2. TestStopStaleDiskEntry_ReapsLivePIDWithReclaimableStaleClaim proves the same path terminates the process tree, releases the stale claim, emits bead.stopped, and marks the record stopped when the lease is safely reclaimable.
3. TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks removes or omits the manager handle for a real live managed-attempt fixture, runs at least three ReconcileAt ticks, and proves the process and fresh claim survive without a replacement duplicate.
4. Preserving a live disk-only worker emits a structured supervisor/lifecycle diagnostic containing worker ID, PID, bead ID, and preservation reason.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "Test(StopStaleDiskEntry_(PreservesLivePIDWithFreshClaim|ReapsLivePIDWithReclaimableStaleClaim)|SupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks)" passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A server-managed ddx work process can be terminated mid-attempt even while its bead lease and worker heartbeat are fresh. On 2026-07-13, worker worker-20260711T204017-68e9 (PID 303304) emitted a fresh heartbeat for ddx-69591a68 at 05:47:15Z, then received cancellation, exited 130 at 05:47:45Z, and was replaced by the supervisor. The attempt had active provider/test descendants and produced no terminal result. This regresses the incident addressed by ddx-57faa17c: that fix preserved the bead claim but did not protect the live process.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1447-1449 calls cleanupManagedWorkerProcessTree for every live PID named by a disk record before cli/internal/server/workers.go:1451-1467 asks staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim whether the fresh lease is safe to reclaim. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1586-1614 correctly refuses claim release for fresh or live same-machine leases, but that decision arrives after the process has already been killed and the record is still rewritten stopped at cli/internal/server/workers.go:1470-1482. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:270-279 sends every snapshot stale ID through this path. TestSupervisorReconcile_FreshClaimingWorkerSurvivesTicks at cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go:882-962 keeps the live worker in the manager handle map and gives the stale fixture PID 0, so it cannot detect a live PID represented only by a stale disk entry.

PROPOSED FIX
- Classify whether a stale disk record is reclaimable before sending any signal or rewriting its state.
- Preserve or adopt a live same-machine process when its bead lease/heartbeat is fresh, even if the current server lacks an in-memory handle after restart, re-exec, or reconciliation.
- Reap the process tree, unclaim, and mark stopped only when ownership and liveness evidence prove the record is stale.
- Emit a diagnostic lifecycle/supervisor event for preserved live disk records so operators can distinguish adoption/preservation from cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker count, generic process cleanup, provider parent-death behavior, or manual/server coordination owned by ddx-635ae251. Do not weaken cleanup of dead PIDs or genuinely stale leases.

PARENT
None; root regression bead.

DEPS
None.

2. TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute, TestRunTryWorkDelegateRoutingToFizeau, TestRoutingOutcomeDoesNotCreateDDXRouteExclusion, TestEstimatedDifficultyMapsOnlyMinPower, and route-neutral review escalation tests pass.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still duplicates route resolution, provider-error interpretation, retry transport policy, cleanup ownership, and review routing that belong below the DDx/Fizeau boundary.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The governing plan identifies WB-1 through WB-8 at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase1-lower-the-altitude-plan-2026-07-13.md:89-253. DDx route preflight remains wired before Fizeau, provider and model identity still influence control flow, and review/repair can override concrete routing fields instead of expressing route-neutral strength.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete DDx route preflight first, then consume typed Fizeau results through one DDx attempt-policy adapter. DDx may request minimum strength, escalate after failed attempts, and require stronger independent review; it must not select a harness, provider, model, or route. Complete the remaining worktree, liveness, decomposition, closure, and lock-stack work in ordered child beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not teach DDx Fizeau routing tables. Do not ask DDx to inspect or kill provider descendants. Do not make temporary provider identity a durable work-item field.

PARENT
None; root recovery epic.

DEPS
Phase 0 mechanical exit, except WB-2a route-preflight deletion which is explicitly dependency-free.

description:
PROBLEM
The three concurrent ddx try subprocess integration tests no longer have a runnable hermetic execution backend after production DDx concrete harness ownership was removed. They still pin harness=script and pass a directive file through model, so the alpha105 gate either fails route resolution or risks discovering a live provider.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/integration/concurrent_try_test.go:30-76 builds an isolated subprocess environment but spawnTry still sends --harness script and --model <directive>. cli/internal/integration/concurrent_try_test.go:130-470 repeats that retired contract in TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryDistinctBeads_LocalClone, TestIntegration_ConcurrentTrySameBead_OneClaimWins, and TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryPreserveRefsUnique. cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:108-134 builds an ordinary production binary, while cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:19-66 exposes only a process-local service factory that cannot cross an exec boundary.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a build-tagged subprocess test binary path that uses the public Fizeau testseam service/FakeProvider contract and is impossible to activate in production builds.
- Translate the existing deterministic edit/commit fixture behavior into scripted Fizeau fake responses or an equally route-compatible fake service at that test-only boundary.
- Keep each test as a real concurrent ddx try subprocess exercise and add a tripwire proving no external Claude, Codex, Gemini, or HTTP provider is launched.
- Preserve the production route-ownership static guard.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not restore script, virtual, or any concrete harness runner to production DDx.

description:
PROBLEM
TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt is a release-blocking flaky test. Repetition on current main c39d1affc fails because the 150 ms context deadline expires before the worker claims, executes, lands, and closes its bead. The earlier bead ddx-298ade98 specified the correct deterministic rewrite but was closed without that source change landing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:935 creates context.WithTimeout with 150*time.Millisecond.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:937-965 runs worker.Run synchronously and conditionally tolerates timeout/operator attention, so scheduling speed controls whether the intended assertions execute.
- The adjacent deterministic test in the same file uses a 30s safety timeout, worker.Run in a goroutine, require.Eventually on durable bead closure, then explicit cancellation.

PROPOSED FIX
Rewrite only TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt to use the adjacent deterministic goroutine plus require.Eventually pattern. Wait for ddx-int-0001 to close, cancel the worker, collect the outcome, and unconditionally assert one attempt, one success, zero failures, nil operator attention, no loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved event, and no refs/ddx/pre-dispatch refs.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production worker behavior, pre-dispatch dirty classification, retry semantics, or other tests and timeouts.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: none; this is a root regression bead. Deps: none.
title: agent tests: make stable pre-dispatch dirt proof deterministic

description:
PROBLEM
While the managed DDx worker decomposed ddx-29491a0e on 2026-07-13, the preclaim-decompose Codex provider remained live for more than two minutes, but `ddx work status --json` changed to active_work.count=0 and reported no bead_id, phase, or last_activity_at. Operators therefore see an apparently idle worker while queue-critical model work is running.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1604-1617 constructs the sidecar liveness reporter but explicitly defers emission until a bead is claimed. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without setting a resolving phase or ticking liveness.

PROPOSED FIX
Publish candidate-scoped resolving liveness before dispatching preclaim decomposition, heartbeat it while the provider runs, and include provider-child observations without creating a bead lease. The sidecar status must expose the candidate bead ID, phase=resolving, refreshed last_activity_at, and provider-child metadata before any claim or lease exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

1. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected proves a code-changing attempt whose bead requires lefthook is rejected when GateRuns is empty, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files.
2. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected proves an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command rejects the attempt even when another gate run appears meaningful.
3. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits proves scoped checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits remain permitted because they are outside implementation-agent evidence.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.

description:
PROBLEM
A code-changing execute-bead attempt can bypass required staged hooks and still be accepted. Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 committed with git commit --no-verify after its pre-commit run was interrupted; DDx merged and closed it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 validates gate evidence only when GateRuns is non-empty, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 calls ValidateAttemptIntegrity without evidence. The validator therefore treats missing evidence as success and does not distinguish implementation-agent bypass commands from orchestrator-owned commits.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the attempt-integrity validator fail closed when a code-changing bead requires a staged lefthook run but meaningful successful evidence is absent. Reject implementation-agent commit commands that use --no-verify. Explicitly retain the scoped exception for DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits, which are outside implementation-agent evidence.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go

NON-SCOPE
Do not wire live harness evidence or change execute-bead prompts; ddx-03568f89 owns that integration. Do not rewrite existing execution history or forbid DDx-owned orchestration commits.

PARENT
ddx-9c3e703b.

DEPS
None.
