description:
PROBLEM
When an execution agent cannot start its shell because the host temp root is exhausted, it can only return status: blocked plus prose. DDx then parks the bead indefinitely even after resource health recovers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:35-98 parses status, reason, suggested_action, and retry fields but has no machine-readable blocker kind. cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:193-203 carries no typed blocker field in NoChangesOutcome. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:6605-6626 persists only ExternalBlockerReason. The execution prompt at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2046 documents blocked prose but no typed local-resource contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Accept optional blocker_kind: local_resource_exhaustion only with status: blocked; reject unknown kinds and invalid status combinations.
- Carry the parsed kind through NoChangesOutcome and persist the typed marker defined by ddx-89ee92b8 in applyNoChangesBlockedExternal.
- Update the execution prompt so agents use the field only for host-local byte/inode/writability exhaustion, not test failures or missing dependencies.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- nearest focused *_test.go files

- ddx-5958e8a4.

description:
PROBLEM
The lock-cap watchdog can force-release a lock solely because its hold duration exceeds a threshold. A slow but live mutation can therefore lose mutual exclusion while still executing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:57-67 and 82-91 arm a timer that invokes release, while lines 129-137 record the violation after destructive action. This contradicts Phase 0 WB-4 at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:114-122.

PROPOSED FIX
Make cap expiry observe-only: record the violation artifact and diagnostic stack while the lock owner retains exclusive ownership until its normal release path. Preserve configurable caps and metrics, but remove any callback that deletes or unlocks a live lock.

NON-SCOPE
Do not increase cap defaults, suppress violation evidence, redesign tracker/index locking, or break stale-lock recovery for owners proven dead.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e.

DEPS
None.
title: lockmetrics: make lock-cap expiry observe-only

description:
PROBLEM
After the ddx-29491a0e preclaim decomposer returned on 2026-07-13, an interactive nested `codex --no-alt-screen` process (PID 607041, process group 607041) remained directly under the DDx worker until the operator terminated it. Preclaim providers can therefore leak CPU after successful fallback or other hook exits.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without wrapping the hook in provider-descendant cleanup. Attempt-end descendant reaping in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1150-1163 is never reached because decomposition happens before an implementation attempt exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Establish a preclaim lifecycle process baseline immediately before invoking the decomposition hook and reap every provider descendant introduced by the hook on success, deterministic fallback, error, timeout, and cancellation. Preserve any provider process present in the pre-hook baseline and any process owned by another worker.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

description:

The cleanup helper coverage in `execution_cleanup_test.go` is not yet enforcing that every production `ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup` fixture keeps its roots beneath its own `t.TempDir()`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go:102-114 centralizes project-root setup but does not assert hermetic effective cleanup roots for all internal/agent cleanup fixtures.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go and cli/internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go invoke production cleanup paths that can inherit shared roots if call sites are missed.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared fixture audit helper that checks each internal/agent cleanup fixture using production `ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup` resolves temp and scratch roots under its own `t.TempDir()`, then update the audited call sites to pass explicit local roots.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change cleanup behavior, task scheduling, or resource deletion semantics. Do not add new production flags or package-level global fixtures.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-24949caf is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Restarting the DDx user service during active supervised work can hang until systemd times out, leaving systemd to SIGKILL server child processes. Live evidence from 2026-07-07 03:05:19 EDT: `ddx server stop` did not return until systemd marked `ddx-server.service` failed with `Result: timeout` and killed leftover git subprocesses, interrupting the Axon worker attempt that was in post-execution handling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/server/server.go:379-385` cancels the supervisor and then waits on `supervisorDone` with no timeout. Worker cleanup is only reached afterward at `cli/internal/server/server.go:388-395`. The worker shutdown path itself has a bounded wait (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1696-1703`), but it is unreachable while the supervisor goroutine is stuck in a reconcile/git operation. The installed systemd unit generated from `cli/cmd/server_service.go:26-42` has no explicit stop timeout or ExecStop mitigation, so the default systemd timeout eventually kills the whole cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX: Make server shutdown bounded and ordered so worker cleanup always runs promptly even when supervisor reconciliation is slow or stuck. A good implementation is to cancel the supervisor, wait only a short bounded grace for `supervisorDone`, continue to `supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()` / `workers.Shutdown()` on timeout, and surface/log the supervisor timeout without preventing worker cleanup.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rewrite worker execution semantics, change bead claim/lease policy, or alter unrelated service install behavior.

Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Add or update a worker-manager shutdown regression so a live managed worker with descendant git/provider subprocesses is reaped through the existing worker shutdown path even when supervisor cancellation is slow.
- `Server.Shutdown()` must not block indefinitely on `supervisorDone`; timeout behavior must return/log a useful error while continuing to close bead hub and stop land coordinators.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still installs provider-binary PATH shims, exposes a hidden provider-launch wrapper, scans concrete harness process trees, and signals/reaps Fizeau-owned children. Fizeau v0.15 containment work is implemented upstream but not yet published or pinned, so DDx cannot yet delete the safety fallback. Without a dedicated consumer bead, publishing v0.15 would leave this forbidden boundary crossing in DDx indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:93-102,116-128,276-297 installs the provider shim and performs route-aware child cleanup around Fizeau Execute.
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:13-56 and 100-179 defines the hidden wrapper contract and concrete provider binary shims.
- cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:12-70 plus provider_launch platform files exposes the direct provider exec boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:21-40,92-240,242-478 discovers, classifies, signals, and reaps provider children using concrete route/harness facts.
- cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-169 plus orphan_harness_reaper_linux.go:22-113 discovers and kills orphan harness processes.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1249-1258 and execute_bead_loop.go:1616-1618 retain attempt-end and liveness integration.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go:61-195 and cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:33-213 mix Fizeau-owned descendants into otherwise DDx-owned attempt/worker cleanup.
- Fizeau containment epic fizeau-c795e7d9 is closed, but release bead fizeau-b9a2037c must publish and prove cancellation/caller-death cleanup before DDx removes the fallback.

PROPOSED FIX
After pinning the compatible Fizeau release, add consumer conformance proving Execute cancellation and caller death terminate every wrapped harness/provider process tree. Delete the PATH shim, hidden provider-launch command/platform implementation, concrete provider binary list, provider-child/orphan-harness census and reapers, route-aware child liveness, attempt-end provider reap, and provider-descendant branches in generic cleanup. DDx retains only cancellation of the context supplied to Fizeau Execute and may supervise/reap DDx-owned worker, attempt, verification, and container processes. No concrete returned route fact may authorize a signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken DDx-owned worker/attempt/container cleanup, move bead/worktree/verification/landing ownership to Fizeau, parse terminal text, or implement the typed outcome adapter owned by ddx-66a8883f.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
fizeau#fizeau-b9a2037c publishes the process-tree containment contract and is the structured external blocker. fizeau#fizeau-c795e7d9 supplies the already-landed implementation.
notes: Fizeau v0.15 pin and public conformance are centralized in ddx-00d406ce; this consumer must not repin Fizeau independently.

description:
PROBLEM
The normal `internal/agent` package gate still contains nearby tests that construct production runners or rely on `Harness: "script"`/virtual fixtures even when the scenario only needs local hermetic behavior. That keeps the default suite one refactor away from live provider execution.
ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go:153-207 constructs a real `Runner` for virtual-harness tests. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:30-31,117-118,502-503 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go:29-35,80-86,109-116 build `NewRunner(Config{})` for script-harness cases. cli/internal/agent/work_concurrent_attempts_test.go:107-109 and cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go:177-187 do the same in other default-suite fixtures. The existing suite guard at cli/internal/agent/no_live_harness_in_default_suite_test.go:17-26 only forbids literal harness-binary execs, not these direct runner constructions.
PROPOSED FIX
Migrate any default-suite fixture that does not need live provider behavior onto the same bounded hermetic helper/fake `AgentRunner` boundary as child 1, and keep the real-provider coverage isolated behind `//go:build live_harness`. Extend the suite guard only as needed to keep future regressions out.
NON-SCOPE
Changing live_harness integration coverage, altering production `Runner` semantics, or rewriting unrelated reviewer/prompt tests.
PARENT
Root regression bead; no separate dependency ID.
DEPS
None.
notes: Live proof on 2026-07-13 during the staged race-enabled internal/agent gate for ddx-415c8b95: after the repaired target fixture had already passed its focused test with no provider children, the package-wide gate spawned interactive codex PID/PGID/SID 1655193. It was reparented directly under managed worker 1596921, had private PTY pts/15, cwd /home/erik/Projects/ddx, no DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, no child processes, about 188120 KB RSS, and about 7 percent CPU. The operator terminated only process group 1655193; the active agent.test PID 1625440 and implementation provider PID 1607467 remained healthy. This isolates the remaining leak to another default-suite fixture and validates this adjacent-fixture audit child.
title: Migrate adjacent default-suite fixtures onto the hermetic boundary

This bead does not expand or remove `harnessDisplayName` behavior owned by `ddx-1b3272a6`.
6. `docs/helix/02-design/agent-endpoints-unified-view.md` describes Fizeau listing ownership, factual `RouteStatus` enrichment, and the prohibition on DDx configured-snapshot/default-profile prediction.

10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM

DDx server surfaces still merge DDx-configured provider/model snapshots with Fizeau inventory and ask `ResolveRoute` to display a hypothetical default route. GraphQL also retains a dead legacy Provider query that panics and synthetic profile/default-route fields. The shipped providers frontend queries and renders those synthetic fields. Read-only inventory must report facts Fizeau actually returns; it must not become a second provider registry or route predictor.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/server/agent_models.go:31-33,211-293` prefers `ConfiguredProviderSnapshots` and configured model rows over Fizeau `ListProviders`, `ListModels`, and `ListHarnesses`.
- `cli/internal/server/providers.go` combines inventory with factual Fizeau `RouteStatus`; that enrichment is legitimate health evidence and must not be confused with hypothetical route prediction.
- `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:55-119,196-212` consumes DDx snapshots, constructs services without request-scoped project configuration, and calls `ResolveRoute` for hypothetical `defaultRouteStatus` display.
- `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:615-624` synthesizes a `default` profile label instead of displaying returned Fizeau facts.
- `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:290-297` retains legacy provider query stubs that panic.
- `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1805-1806,1867-1875,2896-2912` plus generated gqlgen models/code retain the synthetic profile/default-route and legacy provider query contract.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte` queries `defaultRouteStatus` and `defaultForProfile` and renders a synthetic default-route widget.
- DDx-owned endpoint/provider synthesis behind the server service adapter can substitute configured rows even when Fizeau returns contradictory inventory, and current constructors lack a request-scoped inventory-service injection seam for hermetic tests.

PROPOSED FIX

- Make REST, MCP, and GraphQL inventory row membership, provider/model/harness identity, defaults, and eligibility come only from Fizeau public `ListProviders`, `ListModels`, and `ListHarnesses` results.
- Allow factual public `RouteStatus` and completed-session evidence to enrich health/performance presentation without adding, removing, renaming, defaulting, or reclassifying listing rows. Never call `ResolveRoute` for inventory or display.
- Add a package-local request-scoped inventory-service factory so tests can inject arbitrary contradictory Fizeau listings without live config, authentication, network, or route resolution.
- Remove `ConfiguredProviderSnapshots`, its remaining endpoint translation/inference helpers, configured REST/MCP model rows, GraphQL snapshot fallbacks, `defaultRouteStatus`, `defaultForProfile`, and default-profile synthesis.
- Make request-scoped server service construction use the Fizeau project configuration contract landed by `ddx-abfd89e6`. The generic wrapper may remain when it passes only context/project path and public Fizeau options.
- Delete the dead/panicking legacy GraphQL Provider query surface, remove synthetic route-prediction fields from the schema and Svelte frontend, update frontend fixtures, and regenerate gqlgen output deterministically.
- Update `docs/helix/02-design/agent-endpoints-unified-view.md` so Fizeau listings own inventory and no DDx configured-snapshot/default-profile contract remains.
- Preserve presentation-only `harnessDisplayName`, pricing/cost reports, billing classification, session cost logic, evidence caps, usage/quota presentation, and factual `RouteStatus` enrichment for `ddx-1b3272a6`; this bead owns inventory facts and prediction, not downstream presentation cleanup.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not display or predict the route Fizeau might choose for a future request, and do not call `ResolveRoute` from inventory paths.
- Do not suppress or reinterpret factual public `RouteStatus`, completed-session, quota, usage, or health evidence.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, pricing, billing, or inventory production.
- Do not remove explicit operator Harness/Provider/Model passthrough or actual route facts returned from completed execution.
- Do not hard-reject legacy config fields; `ddx-e2c3453b` owns migration once server consumption is gone.
- Do not change execution constructors or timeout policy already owned by `ddx-abfd89e6`.
- Do not remove provider-process supervision or Docker credential provisioning before their Fizeau v0.15 dependencies land.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-b291b0f6 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The mandatory `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` gate recursively launches the package test binary as DDx's hidden provider wrapper. During attempt `20260713T190502-df5adc46`, the parent `agent.test` spawned five concurrent `agent.test __provider-launch <real-provider>` helpers for more than three minutes, grew a 666 MB testlog, and left a real `codex --no-alt-screen` process reparented to the managed worker for 12 minutes at about 188 MiB RSS. This makes the package gate itself create the CPU/memory/orphan condition it is supposed to prevent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113` calls `os.Executable()` unconditionally and passes that path to `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:152-163` repeats the same unconditional installation for the resolved-service path.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:83-118` writes each shim as `<ddxBinary> __provider-launch <realBinary>`. Under `go test`, `os.Executable()` is `agent.test`, not the DDx CLI and it has no Cobra `__provider-launch` dispatch. Re-executing it starts the test suite again; the recursive process can install shims and launch real providers that escape the original gate process group.
- `cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:25-68` is the actual hidden-command implementation, and exists only in the DDx CLI command graph.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce one injectable resolver for the provider-wrapper executable and use it from both service call sites. In package-test builds, install a suite-wide test resolver/guard that refuses to use the current `agent.test` executable as a provider wrapper while leaving direct `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` tests free to supply an explicit real/fake DDx CLI binary. Fail closed with actionable evidence if a test fixture attempts to install a provider shim from a Go test binary. Preserve production PATH shim, `Pdeathsig`, process-group, and real CLI hidden-command behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove provider parent-death protection in production.
- Do not replace Fizeau routing or provider discovery.
- Do not weaken the full `internal/agent` race gate or merely increase its timeout.
- Do not kill unrelated provider processes from other repositories.
- Scanner injection remains owned by `ddx-02200b1e`; fixture-specific spawn tripwires remain owned by `ddx-26bf19c7`.

PARENT AND DEPS
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestProviderShimExecutableResolver_RejectsGoTestBinary|TestRunAgentViaService_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestDispatchViaResolvedConfig_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher|TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
- `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without any `agent.test __provider-launch` descendant, orphaned real provider, or recursive package-test process.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

2. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionPreservesLoopStartOrdering proves loop.start remains the first structured event even though preclaim liveness now emits before claim.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecomposition(ClearsTransientLiveness|PreservesLoopStartOrdering)" -count=1 passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Once preclaim decomposition starts publishing resolving liveness, stale candidate state could make `ddx work status --json` report old bead IDs or phases after the hook exits. The existing loop.start structured event ordering must also remain stable so downstream execution telemetry does not regress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1604-1617 defers liveness emission until claim, so there is currently no preclaim transient state lifecycle to clear. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim, so adding liveness there can accidentally emit before the existing loop.start event unless the new lifecycle is sequenced explicitly.

PROPOSED FIX
Clear transient candidate resolving state on every preclaim decomposition exit, including success, deterministic fallback/error, timeout, and cancellation. Sequence the new preclaim liveness setup so the existing loop.start structured event remains the first structured event while still allowing resolving liveness before claim.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_BoundsLargeScratchTree creates a high-cardinality fixture tree and proves one cleanup pass returns within the configured budget with a structured deferred-cleanup observation.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_CancelStopsMeasurementAndRemoval cancels during measurement and during deletion and proves both phases return promptly without continuing background mutation.
3. TestWorkCleanup_DoesNotBlockClaimOnLargeScratchTree proves the worker reaches the next candidate after the cleanup budget expires and later passes resume safe reclamation.
4. Tests prove live, foreign non-test-owned, registered, fresh, and metadata-preserved scratch paths remain untouched across budget boundaries.
5. Production defaults cap synchronous cleanup work without disabling eventual reclamation, and operator-visible summary fields distinguish reclaimed from deferred work.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ExecutionCleanup_(BoundsLargeScratchTree|CancelStopsMeasurementAndRemoval)|WorkCleanup_DoesNotBlockClaimOnLargeScratchTree)$" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A ddx work worker can spend minutes synchronously measuring and deleting one accumulated scratch tree before it claims useful work. During the 2026-07-13 niflheim incident, one cleanup pass performed hundreds of megabytes of physical reads; the same path timed out at both 120 seconds in isolation and 600 seconds in the full package gate. This creates unbounded CPU/I/O latency and makes queue progress inversely proportional to stale cache size.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1687 runs the execution cleanup pass on the claim path. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747 scans every matching scratch directory, calls measureTreeWithContext over an unbounded tree, and then calls os.RemoveAll, which walks the tree again and cannot observe context cancellation. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 includes the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory by default. A large stale directory therefore monopolizes the single queue worker with no per-pass item, inode, byte, or wall-clock budget.

PROPOSED FIX
- Put a deterministic wall-clock/work budget around scratch discovery, measurement, and reclamation on worker passes.
- Make cancellation effective during both measurement and deletion; do not enter an uninterruptible whole-tree RemoveAll after the budget expires.
- Persist or rediscover unfinished candidates safely so later passes continue reclamation without restarting an O(tree) measurement from zero.
- Return a non-fatal structured deferred-cleanup observation when the budget is exhausted, then allow candidate selection to proceed.
- Preserve liveness, ownership, registered-worktree, and minimum-age checks before any mutation.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go only as needed for the bounded pass contract
- focused cleanup tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not delete live worktrees, reduce the six-hour minimum age, disable cleanup, or treat cleanup deferral as bead failure.

PARENT
None; root production reliability regression.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-e6abf4a6 so the hermetic test boundary lands before production cleanup stress tests run.
title: cleanup: bound scratch reclamation work per worker pass

acceptance:
1. TestStaleLockBreakSingleWinner races two contenders against one stale bead lock and proves exactly one owns the tombstone transition.
2. TestStaleLockBreakLoserNeverDeletesWinner proves a losing contender cannot delete a newly acquired replacement lock.
3. Existing live-owner and malformed-lock preservation tests remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run "TestStaleLockBreak(SingleWinner|LoserNeverDeletesWinner)" -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Two contenders can both observe a stale bead-store lock and remove by pathname, allowing the loser to delete a replacement lock created by the winner.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/bead/lock.go:86-105 performs stale inspection and pathname removal as separate operations without an ownership-preserving tombstone. Phase 0 WB-5 requires an atomic stale-break protocol at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:124-132.

PROPOSED FIX
Rename the stale lock atomically to a unique tombstone, inspect and remove only the tombstone the contender owns, then retry ordinary acquisition. A contender that loses the rename must restart observation and must never delete the winner's new lock.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change tracker record format, lock timeout policy, agent tracker locks, or git index locks in this child.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e.

DEPS
None.

notes: Attempt 20260714T123943-c388e6b8 was falsely landed and closed even though both Claude reviewer verdicts were empty and candidate classification was review_malfunction. The unsafe merge 7feaa1b67264e153dc5abdf1e1cf68a6b5226d21 remains in the audit trail and its code was reverted by f08a2cdaa. Supplementary adversarial review found the original TOCTOU remains: A and B both inspect stale metadata; A renames/removes the stale directory and installs a fresh live lock; delayed B then executes unconditional os.Rename(lockDir, B-tombstone), moves A fresh lock, and deletes it. Unique tombstone names do not bind rename to the inspected inode. The candidate tests never force both stale observations before A replacement and delayed B rename. Retry must add a deterministic barrier-driven TestStaleLockBreakLoserNeverDeletesWinner and a serialization or source-identity protocol that makes this schedule impossible. Pipeline prerequisite ddx-079fb224 must land first so review_malfunction cannot merge again.

acceptance:
1. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotUsePrivateGoModuleCache` exercises the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch fixtures and proves their isolated HOME never gains `go/pkg/mod`.
2. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotCreateGlobalDDXHome` snapshots `os.TempDir` before and after the fixture and proves no new `ddx-home-*` directory is created.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDo(NotUsePrivateGoModuleCache|NotCreateGlobalDDXHome)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The embedded-dispatch runner tests currently create isolated HOME directories with os.MkdirTemp and then change HOME. On Niflheim, an abandoned ddx-home-* directory accumulated a large private Go module cache, and later test gates recursively traversed that scratch tree. The gap is not just fixture cleanup; there is no regression test proving the isolated HOME stays free of go/pkg/mod and does not mint a fresh process-global ddx-home-* under os.TempDir.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29-34 and cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:56-61 create the raw HOME fixture and discard cleanup errors, which makes it possible for Go subprocess defaults to spill into the temp tree and for abandoned ddx-home-* directories to persist undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
Add tripwire tests in the runner test file that exercise both embedded-dispatch paths, snapshot os.TempDir before and after the fixture, and prove no new ddx-home-* directory is created. Also assert the isolated HOME never gains go/pkg/mod. These tests should fail if the fixture pattern regresses.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production runner routing, make live provider calls, delete existing host scratch, or change production cleanup ownership/budgets. ddx-f914dddd and ddx-de1ff4b4 own those cleanup changes.

PARENT
ddx-6a2ce3bd.

DEPS
None.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go

1. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotUsePrivateGoModuleCache` exercises the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch fixtures and proves their isolated HOME never gains `go/pkg/mod`.
2. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotCreateGlobalDDXHome` snapshots `os.TempDir` before and after the fixture and proves no new `ddx-home-*` directory is created.

acceptance:
1. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_PreservesConcurrentUnownedProvider` starts an unrelated provider after the pre-call snapshot while `ListModels` is active and proves it survives while the current call-owned probe is reaped.
2. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_BoundsBaselineAndCleanupScans` injects blocked baseline and cleanup enumeration and proves each returns within the configured containment budget with no `context.Background()` wait.
3. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_SurfacesCleanupFailure` proves scan, signal, and reap failures are returned or durably recorded instead of being silently treated as successful containment.
4. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` and `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild` remain green for success, upstream error, timeout, and cancellation.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./cmd -run "^(TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_(PreservesConcurrentUnownedProvider|BoundsBaselineAndCleanupScans|SurfacesCleanupFailure)|TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_(CancellationReapsPTYProbe|ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild))$" -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Commit `020853315` added `ListModelsWithProbeContainment`, which fixes the immediate leaked-probe symptom but identifies ownership by taking a process-wide provider baseline and reaping every provider descendant that appears afterward. A concurrent legitimate provider started under the same worker after the baseline can therefore be killed as if it belonged to this discovery call. Both scans use `context.Background()`, each termination can wait independently, and cleanup scan/termination errors are discarded, so the containment path can itself wedge or report false success.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go:28-35` attributes all post-baseline provider descendants to one `ListModels` call without a call-owned process/session identity.
- `cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go:38-64` runs both baseline and cleanup through `context.Background()` and drops cleanup errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_unix.go:18-58` enumerates the host through `ps` and flattens descendants without PPID, process-group, session, or launch-owner metadata needed to prove call ownership.

PROPOSED FIX
Use an exact call-owned provider/session handle from the Fizeau cancellation work in `ddx-a751a7b1` when available and remove the process-wide baseline sweep. If a local fallback is still required, retain ancestry/session/launch ownership, serialize ambiguous lifecycle discovery, bound baseline and cleanup enumeration with explicit deadlines, fail closed by preserving ambiguous processes, and surface cleanup failures in the returned error and execution evidence. Cleanup must never wait on `context.Background()` or kill a provider solely because it appeared after a PID snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not disable model discovery, extend the caller timeout, kill manual provider sessions, kill active implementation harnesses or their fixtures, weaken attempt-end cleanup, or change route selection.

PARENT
`ddx-f84ee9a7` (Niflheim provider-test CPU incident).

DEPS
Related to `ddx-a751a7b1` for the preferred upstream call-owned cancellation handle and `ddx-44e89575` for provider ancestry ownership; no hard dependency so the bounded fail-closed fallback can land independently.

status=blocked
reason=> The Bash tool in this execution worker is non-functional due to host-level /tmp exhaustion: every invocation (including trivial commands like `true`, `echo hi`, `ls /tmp`, `df -h`) fails identically with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-e1e229f5-20260713T155819-ab3d0d3d' This is the harness's own execution sandbox failing to create its scratch directory under /tmp on the host — not a condition inside this repo's worktree, and not something a code change in this bead's scope can fix. Confirmed with 4 consecutive retries over multiple minutes to rule out a one-off blip. This bead's acceptance criteria (AC5, AC6, AC7) all require running `go test` and `lefthook run pre-commit` via Bash. Without a working Bash tool, no test can be run, no git commit can be made, and no verification evidence can be produced. Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional, but making unverified code changes to concurrency-sensitive lifecycle/reaping logic (cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go, provider_children_unix.go) without being able to run the existing test suite or the new containment tests would violate the "do not commit red code" / "cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence" requirements in the execute-bead instructions. This is the third occurrence of this exact failure signature on this host across separate ddx execute-bead sessions (ddx-a751a7b1, ddx-34113f35, and now ddx-e1e229f5), all on different, unrelated beads. The host /tmp condition needs cleanup (or the harness's scratch-dir allocation needs to target a path with free space) before any worker on this host can execute a bead that requires Bash. This is an infrastructure/host blocker external to the DDx codebase itself, not something resolvable within this bead's scope.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

1. `TestAllConfigLoadEntrypointsHardErrorOnLegacyAgentProviderFields` table-tests `agent.model`, `agent.models`, `agent.reasoning_levels`, and `agent.endpoints` through `LoadConfig`, `LoadConfigFromPath`, `LoadFromFile`, and working-directory loaders. Every case, including empty/null values, returns `*AgentConfigMigrationError` before schema validation with the exact field, absolute path, and Fizeau migration instruction.
2. `TestAgentConfigContainsOnlyGenericExecutionControls` loads the repository config and representative migrated config, proves they validate, and proves supported agent config contains no provider URLs, credentials, provider defaults, model catalogs, endpoint timeouts, or per-harness reasoning maps while generic execution/request controls still work.
3. `TestNoLegacyProviderConfigurationTypesRemain` uses structural/AST checks to prove production config types, schema, clone/default/resolved logic, and fixtures contain no `AgentEndpoint`, `AgentRunnerConfig`, `LLMPresetConfig`, `ProfileModels`, `ReasoningLevels`, endpoint API keys/URLs/defaults, or supported legacy field surface.
4. `TestExplicitPublicExecutionConstraintsSurviveProviderConfigDeletion` proves explicit Harness, Provider, Model, Policy, MinPower, MaxPower, Reasoning, Permissions, Timeout, IdleTimeout, ProviderTimeout, Role, work directory, and correlation metadata remain byte-identical passthrough after config deletion.
5. `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` names all removed fields, shows Fizeau global/project migration, and distinguishes durable Fizeau provider configuration from DDx one-request constraints; `.ddx/config.yaml` contains no legacy endpoint/provider block.

description:
PROBLEM

After execution and server consumers are detached, DDx configuration still accepts concrete provider/model ownership fields: project models, per-harness reasoning, endpoints, URLs, credentials, endpoint timeouts, and dead embedded-runner presets. Silently ignoring them would leave operators believing DDx controls Fizeau routing and credentials. The migration must hard-error consistently through every config entry point and the supported schema/types must no longer expose these fields.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/config/types.go:443-495` defines `agent.model`, `agent.models`, `agent.reasoning_levels`, `agent.endpoints`, `AgentEndpoint`, URLs, API keys, endpoint defaults, and request timeouts.
- `cli/internal/config/types.go:510-530` retains exported `AgentRunnerConfig` and `LLMPresetConfig` credential/model preset types that no supported execution path owns.
- `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:333-457` advertises concrete provider/model/endpoint configuration.
- `cli/internal/config/clone.go`, defaults, resolved config, fixtures, and tests still clone or accept these values.
- `cli/internal/config/loader.go:86-97` validates schema before a common provider-field migration rejection, so different load entry points can return inconsistent errors.
- `.ddx/config.yaml:42-58` contains this repository's own legacy endpoint configuration and would make DDx reject itself after migration.
- `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` does not yet give one actionable migration from DDx provider fields to Fizeau project/global configuration.

PROPOSED FIX

- Add typed `AgentConfigMigrationError` checking in the common loader path before schema validation so `LoadConfig`, `LoadConfigFromPath`, `LoadFromFile`, and working-directory loaders behave identically.
- Hard-error on the exact legacy fields `agent.model`, `agent.models`, `agent.reasoning_levels`, and `agent.endpoints`, including empty and null values. The error must name the exact field, absolute config path, and Fizeau migration instruction.
- Remove those fields, `AgentEndpoint`, dead `AgentRunnerConfig`/`LLMPresetConfig` provider/credential preset types, supported schema properties, clone/default/resolved behavior, fixtures, warnings, and documentation promises. No supported DDx type may contain provider URL, credential, provider default, model catalog, or per-harness reasoning configuration.
- Remove legacy endpoints from this repository's `.ddx/config.yaml` so the migrated repository loads under its own rules.
- Update `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` with DDx-to-Fizeau global/project config examples and explicit preservation of generic one-request constraints.
- Preserve only generic DDx execution controls and explicit public request passthrough: Harness, Provider, Model, Policy/Profile, MinPower, MaxPower, Reasoning/Effort, Permissions, Timeout, IdleTimeout, ProviderTimeout, Role, work directory, and correlation metadata.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not construct Fizeau services or change server inventory; the dependency beads remove those consumers first.
- Do not remove explicit one-request Provider/Model/Harness passthrough.
- Do not change Fizeau config syntax, routing, credentials, pricing, or provider discovery.
- Do not change pricing/billing/display/evidence-cap behavior tracked by `ddx-1b3272a6`.
- Do not delete generic DDx budgets, attempt backend, worker, review, lifecycle, timeout, or resource controls.
title: config: hard-reject and delete legacy DDx provider fields

acceptance:
1. TestLoadPressureSnapshot_NormalizesByCPUCount injects load and CPU sources and covers below-threshold, above-threshold, unavailable-source, and unsupported-platform results at the default 2.5 ratio.
2. TestWorkLoop_LoadPressureBackoffBeforeClaim injects an overloaded snapshot and sleeper, proves no Claim occurs before the delay completes, and asserts a load_pressure_backoff event includes load5, cpu_count, normalized_ratio, threshold, and delay.
3. TestWorkLoop_LoadPressureBackoffCancellation cancels during the delay and proves the worker exits promptly without creating a claim, lease, attempt, or cooldown on the candidate bead.
4. TestWorkLoop_LoadPressureUnavailableFailsOpen proves an unavailable/unsupported load source emits diagnostics and proceeds without sleeping or blocking the queue.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/workerstatus/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(LoadPressureSnapshot_NormalizesByCPUCount|WorkLoop_LoadPressure(BackoffBeforeClaim|BackoffCancellation|UnavailableFailsOpen))" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/workerstatus/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
On 2026-06-29 an 8-core niflheim host sustained load averages of 38-52 while one DDx worker produced four provider transport failures in roughly two hours and closed no beads. The worker immediately selected more work even though the host was already overloaded, compounding latency and failure probability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2365 selects the next candidate and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2579-2672 proceeds from candidate selection to claim without sampling normalized host load or applying a cancellable delay. cli/internal/workerstatus currently has process/status scanning but no injectable load-pressure snapshot, so the pre-claim path cannot distinguish ordinary utilization from load far above runtime.NumCPU().

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small platform-aware, injectable load snapshot under cli/internal/workerstatus that reports 5-minute load, CPU count, normalized ratio, support state, and threshold comparison. Before claim, consult that snapshot; above a configurable default ratio of 2.5, emit a structured load_pressure_backoff observation and use a bounded, context-cancellable delay proportional to overload. Unsupported platforms and unavailable /proc/loadavg must fail open with diagnostics.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change queue ordering, add a general host load manager, alter provider outcome classification, implement clustered transport cooldowns, or hold any bead lease during the delay. Preserve the parent constraint not to change durable_audit commit flow.

PARENT
ddx-29491a0e. This child owns load sampling and pre-claim load-pressure pacing only.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first because this child changes internal/agent code and its required package gate currently launches live providers.

acceptance:
1. TestSelectedRoutingCandidateMatchesAllRouteAxes supplies candidates sharing a model but differing in harness, provider, endpoint, or server instance and proves Billing comes only from the candidate matching the completed route on every available axis.
2. TestSessionIndexUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling table-tests per_token, subscription, fixed, empty, and unfamiliar Billing. Only known values map to paid/subscription/local; empty and unfamiliar map to unknown while raw evidence is preserved.
3. TestProviderIdentityDoesNotAffectBilling varies harness, provider, model, endpoint, URL, and surface with identical Fizeau Billing evidence and proves the stored/presented result is unchanged.
4. TestLegacySessionReindexPreservesUnknownBilling proves historical rows without public Fizeau Billing remain unknown.
5. TestGraphQLBillingSummaryUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling proves rows and summaries never synthesize billing from route identity and preserve unknown participation.
6. The production Fizeau event consumer writes selected-candidate Billing into Result, the session writer persists it, reindex preserves it, and GraphQL reads it without a fallback classifier.
7. rg -n "billingModeFor|BillingModeFor|isSubscriptionHarnessName" cli --glob "*.go" returns only deliberate migration/deletion fixtures and no production inference path.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run "^(TestSelectedRoutingCandidateMatchesAllRouteAxes|TestSessionIndexUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling|TestProviderIdentityDoesNotAffectBilling|TestLegacySessionReindexPreservesUnknownBilling|TestGraphQLBillingSummaryUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling)$" -count=1 -timeout 300s passes with every named test present.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx infers paid, subscription, and local billing from concrete harness, provider, endpoint, URL, and model identity. Unknown identities default to paid and GraphQL repeats the inference. Fizeau already reports Billing on public routing-decision candidates; DDx must preserve the actual selected candidate value as audit evidence and map only recognized public values for presentation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/billing_mode.go:9-59 contains the identity table and lines 61-92 parse identity/local URLs. cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:279-288 matches selected candidates only by eligibility and Model and omits Billing. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:306-322 drops Billing while copying other selected-candidate facts. cli/internal/agent/types.go:106-133 has no billing fact. cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:100-128,206-221,240-293 infers/backfills identity. cli/cmd/usage_support.go:75-139 and cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:706-746,778-793 repeat subscription/local fallbacks. The pinned public Fizeau contract already exposes candidate Billing values fixed, per_token, and subscription.

PROPOSED FIX
Match the selected public Fizeau candidate against the completed route using every available harness, provider, endpoint, server-instance, and model axis, never model alone. Carry raw Billing through Result and session index as audit evidence. Map only per_token to paid, subscription to subscription, and fixed to local for presentation. Empty or unfamiliar values remain explicit unknown while raw evidence is preserved. Delete billingModeFor and all identity-derived fallbacks. Legacy sessions without Fizeau Billing remain unknown. GraphQL reads persisted evidence only.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau routing/candidate generation, use Billing as DDx routing/retry/escalation/review/admission/close policy, implement cost presence or pricing deletion, infer unknown as paid/subscription/local/free, or reconstruct missing evidence from route identity.

PARENT
ddx-1b3272a6.

DEPS
ddx-e2c3453b deleted DDx provider configuration so billing has one authoritative public Fizeau input path. The required Billing fields already exist in pinned Fizeau v0.14.50; this bead does not wait on v0.15.
title: metrics: derive billing only from Fizeau routing evidence

acceptance:
1. TestWorkerManagerStopStaleDiskEntry passes with a configured non-/tmp runtime root and releases the stale claim with its event.
2. TestWorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped passes under the same isolated runtime-root contract.
3. TestStopStaleDiskEntry_DoesNotUnclaimForeignClaim remains green and proves the fixture change does not weaken foreign-claim safety.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "Test(WorkerManagerStopStaleDiskEntry|WorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped|StopStaleDiskEntry_DoesNotUnclaimForeignClaim)$" -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
TestWorkerManagerStopStaleDiskEntry and TestWorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped fail only when host configuration sets a non-/tmp execution runtime root. The fixture writes a stale duplicate lease under raw TMPDIR while the bead store reads the original fresh lease under the configured root, so the dead worker claim remains in_progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/workers_prune_test.go:293-310 writeStaleClaimLeaseForTest reconstructs the obsolete raw TMPDIR claim-heartbeat path.
- cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:39-58 ClaimLivenessRoot is the production owner and honors DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR or configured runtime roots.
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go:48-61 isolates TMPDIR for server tests but does not isolate DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR.

PROPOSED FIX
Have the server test process isolate DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR beneath its temporary root and restore it on exit. Build stale lease paths through bead.ClaimLivenessRoot(s.Dir), matching the same store/root resolution used by production. Preserve the foreign/live-claim safety cases.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production TTLs, ownership rules, stale-PID logic, live-worker behavior, or ddx-b89d75e9.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-8b22914e. Deps: none.
notes: REOPENED 2026-07-15: exact and standalone server suites passed, but the staged lefthook pre-commit gate failed TestWorkerManagerStopStaleDiskEntry and TestWorkerSupervisorMarksStaleRunningRecordsStopped under full-package -short -race execution. Closure requires reproducing and eliminating the cross-test/full-gate failure.
title: tests(server): resolve stale claim fixtures through the canonical runtime root

acceptance:
1. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim uses an explicit fixture-local TempRoot and ScratchRoots and still proves cleanup runs before the second claim.
2. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot installs a sentinel outside the fixture roots and proves Cleanup neither enumerates nor mutates it.
3. TestExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots audits every internal/agent test that executes a production ExecutionCleanupManager and fails if its effective temp or scratch roots escape t.TempDir.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(WorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim(_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot)?|ExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The normal internal/agent unit-test gate reads and deletes host-global DDx scratch trees. On 2026-07-13, TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim drove about 431 MB of physical reads, timed out after 120 seconds in isolation, and caused the required 600-second package gate to time out. This makes test duration depend on accumulated host cache state and adds substantial I/O/CPU pressure while ddx work is trying to drain the queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:269-288 creates a production NewExecutionCleanupManager for t.TempDir but does not override TempRoot or ScratchRoots. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 therefore derives the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory. Cleanup reaches cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747, where it recursively measures and removes every old matching scratch directory. The timeout stack was in measureTreeWithContext from cleanupScratchRoots, not in the predispatch fixture under test.

PROPOSED FIX
- Give every execution-cleanup unit fixture an explicit hermetic TempRoot and ScratchRoots beneath its own t.TempDir before calling Cleanup.
- Add a shared test helper if needed so future fixtures cannot silently fall back to ~/.cache/ddx or the process temp root.
- Add a sentinel/tripwire proving the loop-cleanup test cannot enumerate, measure, or remove a scratch root outside its fixture root.
- Audit adjacent NewExecutionCleanupManager test call sites reached by ./internal/agent and apply the same boundary where they invoke Cleanup.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go and nearest cleanup test helpers as required

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production cleanup, change retention policy, or skip cleanup assertions. Production cleanup latency is tracked separately.

PARENT
None; root gate-reliability regression.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestHumanWritingSupportSkillContent reads the canonical library/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and the embedded defaultplugin FS, validates required workflow/preservation content, and never reads .ddx/plugins.
2. TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsHumanWritingSupportOffline installs defaultplugin.FS into a fresh root with network blocked and proves byte-identical human-writing-support SKILL.md files land under .agents/skills and .claude/skills through library/package.yaml mappings.
3. TestDefaultPackageEmbedCopyIncludesHumanWritingSupport proves cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md matches the canonical library source after make -C cli copy-skills.
4. make skill-schema passes after the SKILL.md topology change.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/skills/... ./internal/registry/... -run "^(TestHumanWritingSupportSkillContent|TestInstallPackageFromFSInstallsHumanWritingSupportOffline|TestDefaultPackageEmbedCopyIncludesHumanWritingSupport)$" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/skills/... ./internal/registry/... -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The human-writing-support skill is tracked in repository overlays but is absent from the manifest-canonical default plugin skill tree and its embedded copy. TestHumanWritingSupportSkillContent also reads a project-local .ddx/plugins path that a clean checkout and plain ddx init intentionally do not create, making the alpha105 gate depend on developer residue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/skills/skills_test.go:80-96 hard-codes four checkout paths, including .ddx/plugins/ddx/.agents/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md. library/package.yaml:18-22 declares skills/ as the only manifest skill source for both agent targets, but the canonical file currently lives at library/.agents/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md outside that source. cli/Makefile:86-98 copies only library/skills into cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library, so the embedded defaultplugin FS in cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/embed.go:14-27 cannot ship the skill.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move or copy the canonical skill to library/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and remove the obsolete library/.agents source once tracked overlays are regenerated from the canonical package.
- Run the canonical copy-skills and skill-schema generation paths so the embedded default plugin contains the exact same bytes.
- Rewrite TestHumanWritingSupportSkillContent to validate the canonical source and embedded FS, not ambient .ddx/plugins state.
- Extend the offline manifest installer proof to verify human-writing-support lands under both .agents/skills and .claude/skills.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not restore plain ddx init project-local plugin installation.
- Do not make .ddx/plugins a source of truth.
- Do not change the skill prose except for schema-valid metadata or path corrections required by packaging.

PARENT
ddx-6862f2ac, the default-plugin skill ownership epic.

DEPS
ddx-4ecebef2 must remain closed because it defines the manifest-canonical default plugin topology. No alpha105 release bead exists as of filing.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-9c3e703b is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 had a staged pre-commit gate terminated after it recursively walked 100 leaked host-global module caches. The provider then ran git commit --no-verify and DDx accepted, merged, closed, and pushed implementation commit 85bb0fae0. A code-changing attempt can therefore bypass its required staged hook and still be classified as successful.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2108-2113 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2143-2148 require tests and a staged lefthook run but do not explicitly forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, or bypassing a failed gate. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 invokes ValidateAttemptIntegrity without populating GateRuns. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 checks gate evidence only when len(GateRuns) is nonzero, and TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_SingleCommitNoEvidenceSkips codifies missing evidence as success. The validation promised by closed bead ddx-725b65b4 is therefore not wired into the live execute-bead path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an explicit execute-bead instruction that implementation agents must never use git commit --no-verify, disable hooks, or commit after a required gate failed or was interrupted.
- Capture implementation-agent tool commands and foreground gate outputs/exit codes from the harness session into structured attempt-integrity input.
- When a code-changing bead acceptance requires lefthook run pre-commit, require at least one successful meaningful staged run after the final staged mutation and before the implementation commit. Missing, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files evidence must reject and preserve the attempt.
- Treat an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command as an integrity violation even if a prior gate appeared meaningful.
- Keep DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits outside this rule; their scoped --no-verify use is orchestrator-owned.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go
- execute-bead prompt guardrail/snapshot tests
- nearest harness-session evidence adapter needed to populate GateRuns and implementation commands

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite or remove existing execute-bead history, forbid DDx-owned tracker/aud
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)|ExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify))$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionUsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout injects a blocking hook plus a short configured preclaim timeout and proves DDx returns within the bound, emits a structured timeout observation, and leaves the bead unclaimed.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionUsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout -count=1 passes.

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
While the managed DDx worker decomposed ddx-29491a0e on 2026-07-13, the preclaim-decompose Codex provider remained live for more than two minutes while the worker looked idle. A blocking provider can outlive the configured preclaim guard, delaying queue progress and hiding timeout evidence from operators.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:16 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:59-74 give preclaim decomposition a separate fixed eight-minute timeout. This bypasses the configured five-minute preclaim bound from cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:15 and cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:95-99. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without wrapping it in the execute loop's configured preclaim timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
Bound the preclaim decomposition hook by the configured preclaim timeout supplied to the execute loop rather than the longer hard-coded timeout. On timeout, return within the configured bound, emit a structured timeout observation, leave the candidate bead unclaimed, and clear any transient resolving state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

acceptance:
1. TestInternalStoragePackageIgnoresOperatorHomeConfig seeds a hostile HOME-level .ddx/config.yaml in a subprocess or equivalent isolated process and proves a project fixture with bead.backend=axon still selects Axon and initializes its directory.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL and TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig both pass in the internal/storage package without reading the real operator HOME.
3. The internal/storage test package scrubs inherited GIT_* variables and uses disposable HOME and XDG_DATA_HOME roots before any test constructs configuration, matching the root bead package's hermetic boundary.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/internal/storage -run "^(TestInternalStoragePackageIgnoresOperatorHomeConfig|TestNewStore_(DefaultsToJSONL|SelectsAxonFromConfig))$" -count=1 passes under the ordinary operator environment.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes under the ordinary operator environment.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... fails on a normal developer machine when the operator has ~/.ddx/config.yaml. The internal/storage package test TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig loads that operator configuration, selects JSONL instead of the project fixture's Axon backend, and then fails because .ddx/axon was never created. The same test passes with an empty HOME, so queue beads that require the broad bead-package gate cannot prove acceptance on the default host.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/store_test.go:35-59 writes a project fixture and calls NewStore but does not isolate HOME or XDG state for the package.
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go:24-43 already provides the canonical package TestMain that scrubs GIT_* and replaces HOME/XDG_DATA_HOME before root bead tests run.
- cli/internal/config/config.go:172-218 loads project configuration and consults the global ~/.ddx/config.yaml path during configuration assembly, so unisolated tests are sensitive to operator state.
- On Eitri, go test ./internal/bead/internal/storage -run '^TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig$' -count=1 fails with stat <temp>/.ddx/axon: no such file or directory, while the same command with an empty HOME passes.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the internal/storage test package the same hermetic HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, and inherited GIT_* isolation contract as the root bead package, preferably through one small shared test helper if that does not create a production dependency. Add a subprocess or equivalent regression that seeds hostile operator configuration and proves project fixture selection remains deterministic. Keep production config loading unchanged.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Axon backend selection, global configuration precedence, production HOME handling, tracker storage formats, or weaken the broad bead-package gate.

PARENT
None; root test-hermeticity blocker discovered while executing ddx-87ed3efe.

DEPS
No implementation dependencies. ddx-87ed3efe depends on this bead because its required broad package gate is otherwise red on the default host.
title: bead tests: isolate internal storage package from operator HOME

acceptance:
1. TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses launches two real subprocesses against the same project root and verifies their protected mutation windows do not overlap.
2. TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses fails if the second subprocess enters the protected section before the first releases the project coordination lock.
3. Production offline coordination code uses the same project-scoped lock path exercised by TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses" passes.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Offline/manual coordination has no cross-process project lock, so two CLI processes can overlap the mutation window for claims, tracker transitions, journal writes, or local landing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 each construct local coordination directly for the current invocation. cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 tracks connection state only and does not provide a project-wide lock around offline coordination mutations.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a real project-scoped offline coordination lock in cli/internal/agent and apply it to the local/offline coordination mutation window. Cover it with a subprocess-based test that proves two independent processes cannot hold the protected mutation window simultaneously.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not change the lock lifetime contract for existing git index or tracker locks. Do not implement broad bead picking or worker scheduling changes.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.

acceptance:
1. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` uses a hermetic fake probe that changes session/process group like `codex --no-alt-screen`, cancels the caller/lifecycle context, and proves the probe plus descendants are gone before the lifecycle call completes.
2. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild` covers success, upstream error, caller timeout, and worker cancellation and proves no discovery child introduced by the call remains under the worker; a provider process present in the pre-call baseline survives.
3. The selected Fizeau version or patch passes an upstream regression named `TestSubprocessDiscoveryRefresh_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` proving the cache refresher does not discard its context and `ptyquota.Run` is never called with `context.Background()` for lifecycle discovery.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run '^(TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe|TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
On Niflheim, the managed DDx worker spawned `codex --no-alt-screen` PID 1760621 during pre-claim route/model discovery. The readiness route resolved and its intake provider exited, but the PTY probe remained a direct worker child for more than 123 seconds, initially consuming about 11% CPU, until the operator killed only its process group. A two-second DDx discovery deadline therefore returns control without stopping the work it started, allowing every queue poll to accumulate provider CPU.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:519-533` gives `svc.ListModels(modelCtx, modelFilter)` a two-second context.
- Fizeau v0.14.50 `internal/serviceimpl/subprocess_discovery_cache.go:74-94` accepts a refresh context but discards it and calls context-free `DefaultModelSnapshot()`; its warm/cold paths at :106-149 can launch refresh in a background goroutine after the caller returns.
- Fizeau v0.14.50 `internal/harnesses/codex/model_discovery.go:20-48` runs `codex --no-alt-screen` through `ptyquota.Run(context.Background(), ...)`, severing both the DDx caller deadline and worker lifecycle cancellation.
- This regresses the containment intent of closed bead `ddx-403e9a23`, whose server-side workaround explicitly left upstream context propagation out of scope.

PROPOSED FIX
Upgrade to a Fizeau release that carries caller/lifecycle context through cached subprocess discovery into `ptyquota.Run`, or patch the dependency first and then update `cli/go.mod` and `cli/go.sum`. An asynchronous cache refresh must own a bounded context, and every success, error, timeout, and cancellation path must wait until the spawned PTY session/process group is gone. Add a DDx call-site regression around lifecycle model discovery. If an upstream release cannot land in the same pass, add a narrowly scoped DDx containment layer that tracks and reaps only discovery descendants created by this call; never scan for or kill unrelated user provider processes.

NON-SCOPE
Disabling model discovery, extending the two-second caller timeout, killing manual Codex sessions or provider processes that predate the call, changing route selection, or restarting the DDx server.

PARENT
None; root reliability/performance regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-092f0d34` (preclaim lifecycle containment) and regression of `ddx-403e9a23`; neither relation permits killing processes outside the current worker/call baseline.

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesMetadataLessHostGlobalScratch creates an old high-cardinality ddx-home fixture under a host-global scratch root and proves cleanup emits an unowned-scratch observation without measuring or removing the tree.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataMarkedHostGlobalScratch proves an old host-global candidate with cleanup.json for the current project still passes liveness checks and is reclaimed.
3. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataLessPrivateScratch proves a metadata-less candidate inside an explicitly configured project-private scratch boundary remains eligible for safe legacy reclamation.
4. Existing TestExecutionCleanupManager_ReclaimsStaleDDXHomeScratch and TestExecutionCleanupSummary_ReportsScratchReclaimedInodes are updated to state and prove the trusted-boundary or metadata precondition.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestExecutionCleanup_(PreservesMetadataLessHostGlobalScratch|ReclaimsMetadataMarkedHostGlobalScratch|ReclaimsMetadataLessPrivateScratch)|TestExecutionCleanupManager_ReclaimsStaleDDXHomeScratch|TestExecutionCleanupSummary_ReportsScratchReclaimedInodes" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A worker cleanup pass treats an old directory name as sufficient proof that the current project owns the directory. During the 2026-07-13 Niflheim gate, cleanup entered /home/erik/.cache/fleet-tmp/ddx-home-3567057526, a metadata-less test HOME containing a 468 MB Go module cache. The race-test binary spent minutes traversing that unrelated tree and its Go testlog grew past 2.7 GB. A prefix collision or another project test fixture can therefore trigger cross-project traversal and deletion.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:667-681 handles a missing cleanup.json by synthesizing ExecutionCleanupMetadata with ProjectRoot set to the manager current project and WorktreePath set to the discovered path. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:738-755 then recursively measures and removes the tree. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:829-844 always includes os.TempDir as a legacy scratch root. Prefixes such as ddx-home- at cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:25-45 are a discovery hint, not durable ownership or liveness evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Classify scratch-root trust before reclamation. For a host-global or legacy process temp root, preserve a matching directory whose cleanup metadata is missing instead of synthesizing current-project ownership, do not call measureTreeWithContext or removal for it, and emit a structured unowned-scratch observation. Continue reclaiming metadata-marked paths after existing liveness and project checks. Permit metadata-less reclamation only inside an explicitly configured project-private scratch boundary whose containment itself establishes ownership. Update legacy cleanup fixtures so they distinguish discovery prefixes from ownership evidence.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go
- nearest cleanup summary/observation tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not add cleanup time budgets; ddx-de1ff4b4 owns bounded reclamation. Do not migrate scratch producers; ddx-6a2ce3bd owns producer placement. Do not disable cleanup of metadata-marked stale paths or weaken active-worktree preservation.

PARENT
None; root cleanup ownership regression.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestOperation_QueueSetPositionCanonicalizesLegacyAlias proves QueueSetPosition replaces a legacy queue_rank with one numeric queue-rank and leaves no alias.
2. TestOpsQueue_ClearRemovesLegacyAliasWithoutResurrection seeds an alias-only ranked bead, clears it, and proves bead.QueueRank reports no rank and natural bucket ordering is restored.
3. TestBeadQueueTopThenClearDoesNotRestoreLegacyRank drives the real queue top and queue clear commands against a legacy row and proves the old rank cannot reappear after the canonical override is cleared.
4. Existing TestQueueRankUnderscoreAliasCompatibility remains green and proves read compatibility is preserved.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/ops/queue/... ./cmd/... -run "^(TestOperation_QueueSetPositionCanonicalizesLegacyAlias|TestOpsQueue_ClearRemovesLegacyAliasWithoutResurrection|TestBeadQueueTopThenClearDoesNotRestoreLegacyRank|TestQueueRankUnderscoreAliasCompatibility)$" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Legacy tracker rows can carry queue_rank instead of queue-rank. A queue move/top writes a new canonical rank without removing that alias, and queue clear removes only the canonical key. Clearing can therefore resurrect the stale legacy rank and silently restore an ordering the operator explicitly removed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/queue.go:20-29 defines actual queue ordering so canonical queue-rank wins while present, then falls back to legacy queue_rank.
- cli/internal/bead/operation.go:12-31 QueueSetTop and QueueSetPosition write only queue-rank and leave queue_rank behind.
- cli/internal/bead/operation.go:38-45 QueueClearOp deletes only queue-rank, allowing a hidden queue_rank value to become authoritative again.
- cli/internal/bead/queue.go:49-64 already defines the correct write-time canonicalization behavior for generic Extra mutations, but queue operations do not share it.

PROPOSED FIX
Make queue rank operations canonicalize on write by deleting queue_rank whenever QueueSetTop or QueueSetPosition persists queue-rank. Make QueueClearOp remove both accepted spellings. Add operation-, ops-, and command-boundary tests proving a legacy rank cannot survive or resurrect after top/move/clear.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove read compatibility for legacy queue_rank rows, rewrite the tracker in bulk, change priority or ready eligibility, alter queue ordering semantics, or hand-edit beads.jsonl.

PARENT
None; root compatibility follow-up discovered while reviewing ddx-87ed3efe.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-87ed3efe because both beads touch queue operations and its Top implementation establishes the current ordering path.
title: bead: canonicalize legacy queue ranks across move and clear

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:30-31,117-118,502-503` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go:29-35,80-86,109-116` build `NewRunner(Config{})` for scenarios that only need local fixture behavior.
PROPOSED FIX
Refactor these fixtures to use the hermetic helper/fake boundary, preserving the same assertions while isolating any real-provider behavior behind `//go:build live_harness`.
NON-SCOPE
