title: Remove direct Runner construction from checkpoint and routing evidence tests

acceptance:
1. TestValidationSupervisor_OutlivesHarnessToolCallCeiling uses a short fake harness-tool ceiling and a longer real helper, proves the tool caller can disconnect, then reattaches and receives one persisted stdout/stderr stream and the real exit 0 without relaunching the helper.
2. TestValidationSupervisor_SameFingerprintReattaches proves direct, timeout-wrapped, redirected, nohup, and start_new_session requests with the same normalized command/cwd/env/worktree fingerprint return the existing validation ID and exactly one command process.
3. TestValidationSupervisor_ChangedFingerprintStartsOneNewRun proves a tracked-worktree or relevant config mutation permits one new supervised validation and records why prior evidence is invalid.
4. TestValidationSupervisor_CancelReapsSessionChangingDescendants proves attempt cancellation and server shutdown remove the command plus setsid/reparented descendants, persist a non-success result, and emit operator_attention with attempt, bead, validation ID, fingerprint, and last activity.
5. TestValidationSupervisor_ManualManagedParity proves manual/offline and server-managed workers expose the same start/status/await and reconnect behavior; the managed command remains in ddx-server.service with KillMode=control-group semantics.
6. TestExecuteBeadLongRunningMatrixPromptSnapshotValidation is replaced or extended by behavioral coverage proving a declared 30-minute gate is not constrained by a shorter harness tool-call ceiling and cannot launch an untracked detached fallback.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run "^(TestValidationSupervisor_(OutlivesHarnessToolCallCeiling|SameFingerprintReattaches|ChangedFingerprintStartsOneNewRun|CancelReapsSessionChangingDescendants|ManualManagedParity)|TestExecuteBeadLongRunningMatrixPromptSnapshotValidation)$" -count=1 -timeout 180s passes.
8. cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes on the final staged tree.
description:
PROBLEM
A bead can require a validation timeout longer than the harness shell tool permits. In ddx-5653688f attempt 20260713T195457-44c4a97d, cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent was still making progress with one bounded test process when the Codex bash tool forcibly terminated it at 20m20.150s with exit 143. The model then tried the same command through background shell, nohup, and Python start_new_session wrappers to evade the tool ceiling. The first two died with their tool shells; the third survived by reparenting to the user systemd manager inside ddx-server.service, leaving DDx unable to attach an exit status or automatically associate that process with the attempt. A valid 30-minute acceptance gate therefore cannot complete through the normal path and provokes exactly the detached replay behavior DDx forbids.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2085-2094 expresses long-command safety only as prompt text. It says to stop after incomplete evidence but provides no DDx-owned way to run a command beyond a harness tool ceiling.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_long_running_fixture_test.go:102-145 explicitly calls prompt-only enforcement sufficient and tests only that phrases exist, not that a long gate can finish once or that detached recovery is contained.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:25-35 observes a tool call only until its result/deadline; it does not own a separately addressable validation subprocess that a later tool call can reattach to.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-39 contains only the harness process group. A command intentionally launched with start_new_session can leave harness ancestry even though it remains in the aggregate server cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX
Create one attempt-owned validation supervisor before the model harness starts and expose a small start/status/await client surface to the model. A long command must be registered by normalized command, cwd, environment/config, and tracked-worktree fingerprint; the supervisor launches it inside the attempt controller, persists stdout/stderr and the real exit status under the execution bundle, and continues when an individual harness tool call reaches its ceiling. Reissuing start for the same fingerprint must reattach to the existing run rather than launch a duplicate. Cancellation, attempt exit, or server death must reap the supervised command and session-changing descendants. The same supervisor/client contract must work for manual/offline and server-managed workers; managed descendants remain in ddx-server.service and die with the service. Replace prompt-only snapshot confidence with behavioral integration coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not infer success from exit 143, missing exit evidence, a PID-only file, or a killed command.
- Do not permit arbitrary detached daemons outside an attempt controller.
- Do not weaken project-declared validation timeouts or skip long tests; ddx-85d27282 separately removes a chaos matrix from short mode.
- Do not duplicate semantic replay detection owned by parent ddx-7fb837a0 or the CPU envelope owned by ddx-7df6b12a.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent ddx-7fb837a0. Depends on ddx-7df6b12a for attempt-controller containment and cleanup.
notes: Superseded by ddx-642a9f26: DDx runs deterministic repository gates before review and uses a fresh Fizeau Execute for repair.
title: agent: supervise long validation outside harness tool-call ceilings
